          elif [[ "$COMMENT" == *"issue"* ]] || [[ "$COMMENT" == *"problem"* ]] || [[ "$COMMENT" == *"bug"* ]]; then
            SENTIMENT="negative"
          else
            SENTIMENT="neutral"
          fi
          
          echo "JobPost comment sentiment: $SENTIMENT"
    😊 Analyzes sentiment of JobPost discussion comments

    4. Auto-response system

    on:
  discussion_comment:
    types: [created]

jobs:
  auto-respond:
    if: contains(github.event.comment.body, '@jobpost-bot')
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: JobPost bot auto-response
        run: |
          echo "🤖 JobPost bot auto-response"
          echo "Bot mentioned by: ${{ github.event.comment.user.login }}"
          echo "In discussion: ${{ github.event.discussion.title }}"
          
          # Generate helpful responses
          COMMENT="${{ github.event.comment.body }}"
          if [[ "$COMMENT" == *"documentation"* ]]; then
            echo "Providing JobPost documentation links"
            RESPONSE="Here are the relevant JobPost documentation links..."
          elif [[ "$COMMENT" == *"example"* ]]; then
            echo "Providing JobPost code examples"
            RESPONSE="Here are some JobPost code examples..."
          fi
          
          echo "Response: $RESPONSE"
    🤖 Provides automated JobPost bot responses when mentioned

    5. Engagement tracking

    on:
  discussion_comment:
    types: [created, edited, deleted]

jobs:
  track-engagement:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Track JobPost discussion engagement
        run: |
          echo "📈 JobPost engagement tracking"
          echo "Action: ${{ github.event.action }}"
          echo "User: ${{ github.event.comment.user.login }}"
          echo "Discussion: ${{ github.event.discussion.title }}"
          echo "Category: ${{ github.event.discussion.category.name }}"
          
          # Track user engagement patterns
          echo "Recording JobPost community engagement"
          echo "User activity: ${{ github.event.comment.user.login }}"
          echo "Engagement type: comment_${{ github.event.action }}"
          
          # Update user engagement score
          curl -X POST "https://analytics.jobpost.com/api/engagement" \
            -d "user=${{ github.event.comment.user.login }}&action=${{ github.event.action }}"
    📈 Tracks JobPost discussion engagement and user activity

    fork

    Docs: fork event

    Triggers workflows when the repository is forked.

    Full description

    The fork event triggers workflows when someone forks your repository. This is useful for tracking repository usage, implementing welcome processes for new contributors, analyzing fork patterns, and setting up automated processes for the JobPost project community.

    Syntax

    on:
  fork

    Parameters description

    No activity types: The fork event doesn't use activity types.
    Fork information: Details about the fork and forker are available.
    Repository context: Access to both original and forked repository info.
    User context: Information about who created the fork.

    Return value

    Workflow runs with access to fork details and forker information.

    Examples

    1. Welcome new JobPost contributors

    on:
  fork

jobs:
  welcome-contributor:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Welcome JobPost fork creator
        run: |
          echo "🍴 JobPost repository forked!"
          echo "Forked by: ${{ github.event.forkee.owner.login }}"
          echo "Fork URL: ${{ github.event.forkee.html_url }}"
          echo "Fork created: ${{ github.event.forkee.created_at }}"
          
          # Send welcome message or setup instructions
          echo "Welcome to the JobPost community!"
          echo "Consider checking our contributing guidelines"
    🍴 Welcomes new JobPost contributors when they fork the repository

    2. Fork analytics tracking

    on:
  fork

jobs:
  track-fork:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Track JobPost fork analytics
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost fork analytics"
          echo "Forker: ${{ github.event.forkee.owner.login }}"
          echo "Forker type: ${{ github.event.forkee.owner.type }}"
          echo "Fork stars: ${{ github.event.forkee.stargazers_count }}"
          echo "Fork language: ${{ github.event.forkee.language }}"
          
          # Log analytics data
          echo "Recording JobPost fork metrics for analysis"
    📊 Tracks JobPost fork analytics and contributor metrics

    3. Community notification

    on:
  fork

jobs:
  notify-community:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Notify JobPost community
        run: |
          echo "🌟 JobPost community growth!"
          echo "New fork by: ${{ github.event.forkee.owner.login }}"
          echo "Total forks: ${{ github.repository.forks_count }}"
          echo "Fork description: ${{ github.event.forkee.description }}"
          
          # Notify community channels
          echo "JobPost project gaining traction - new fork created!"
    🌟 Notifies JobPost community about new forks and growth

    4. Fork validation

    on:
  fork

jobs:
  validate-fork:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Validate JobPost fork
        run: |
          echo "✅ Validating JobPost fork"
          echo "Fork private: ${{ github.event.forkee.private }}"
          echo "Fork has issues: ${{ github.event.forkee.has_issues }}"
          echo "Fork has wiki: ${{ github.event.forkee.has_wiki }}"
          
          # Check for proper fork setup
          if [ "${{ github.event.forkee.private }}" = "false" ]; then
            echo "Public fork - good for contributions"
          fi
    ✅ Validates JobPost fork settings and configuration

    5. Contributor onboarding

    on:
  fork

jobs:
  onboard-contributor:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: JobPost contributor onboarding
        run: |
          echo "🎯 JobPost contributor onboarding"
          echo "New contributor: ${{ github.event.forkee.owner.login }}"
          echo "Public repos: ${{ github.event.forkee.owner.public_repos }}"
          echo "Followers: ${{ github.event.forkee.owner.followers }}"
          
          # Setup contributor resources
          echo "Setting up JobPost development environment resources"
          echo "Sending contributor documentation links"
    🎯 Onboards new JobPost contributors with resources and documentation

    gollum

    Docs: gollum event

    Triggers when Wiki pages are created or updated in the repository.

    Full description

    The gollum event triggers when Wiki pages are created, edited, or updated in your JobPost repository. This enables automated documentation workflows, content synchronization, knowledge base management, and documentation quality assurance for maintaining comprehensive project documentation.

    Syntax

    on:
  gollum

    Parameters description

    No types: Triggers on any Wiki page activity.
    Page information: Access to changed pages via github.event.pages.
    Change details: Information about what was added, modified, or removed.
    Author context: Details about who made the Wiki changes.
    Multiple pages: Can handle multiple page changes in single event.

    Return value

    Workflow runs with access to Wiki page changes, content, and modification details.

    Examples

    1. JobPost documentation sync

    on:
  gollum

jobs:
  sync-documentation:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Sync JobPost Wiki to documentation
        run: |
          echo "📚 JobPost Wiki documentation sync"
          echo "Modified by: ${{ github.event.sender.login }}"
          echo "Pages changed: ${{ github.event.pages.length }}"
          
          # Process each changed page
          for page in ${{ join(github.event.pages, ' ') }}; do
            echo "Processing JobPost Wiki page: $page"
            echo "Page title: ${{ page.title }}"
            echo "Action: ${{ page.action }}"
            echo "SHA: ${{ page.sha }}"
          done
          
          # Sync to main documentation
          echo "Syncing JobPost Wiki changes to main docs"
          echo "Updating API documentation links"
          echo "Refreshing knowledge base index"
    📚 Synchronizes JobPost Wiki changes with main documentation system

    2. Documentation quality check

    on:
  gollum

jobs:
  quality-check:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: JobPost Wiki quality validation
        run: |
          echo "🔍 JobPost Wiki quality validation"
          echo "Checking pages modified by: ${{ github.event.sender.login }}"
          
          # Quality checks for each page
          echo "Running JobPost documentation quality checks:"
          echo "✅ Checking for broken links"
          echo "✅ Validating code examples"
          echo "✅ Ensuring proper formatting"
          echo "✅ Checking for sensitive information"
          echo "✅ Validating API endpoint references"
          
          # Simulate quality check results
          echo "JobPost Wiki quality score: 95/100"
          echo "Recommendations: Add more code examples"
          echo "Auto-fixes applied: 3 formatting issues"
    🔍 Validates JobPost Wiki documentation quality and standards

    3. Team notification system

    on:
  gollum

jobs:
  notify-team:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Notify JobPost team of Wiki changes
        run: |
          echo "📢 JobPost Wiki change notification"
          echo "Editor: ${{ github.event.sender.login }}"
          echo "Changes summary:"
          
          # Create notification for each page
          for i in $(seq 0 $((${#github_event_pages[@]} - 1))); do
            echo "Page: ${github_event_pages[i].title}"
            echo "Action: ${github_event_pages[i].action}"
            echo "URL: ${github_event_pages[i].html_url}"
          done
          
          # Send to team channels
          echo "Sending JobPost Wiki updates to:"
          echo "📧 Development team email list"
          echo "💬 Slack #jobpost-docs channel"
          echo "📱 Mobile app notifications"
          
          # Log for audit trail
          echo "Wiki change logged for JobPost documentation audit"
    📢 Notifies JobPost team about Wiki documentation changes

    4. Knowledge base indexing

    on:
  gollum

jobs:
  update-search-index:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Update JobPost knowledge base index
        run: |
          echo "🔍 JobPost knowledge base indexing"
          echo "Indexing Wiki changes for searchability"
          
          # Extract content for search indexing
          echo "Processing JobPost Wiki content:"
          echo "- Extracting keywords and tags"
          echo "- Building search index"
          echo "- Creating cross-references"
          echo "- Updating FAQ database"
          echo "- Generating topic suggestions"
          
          # Update search systems
          echo "Updating JobPost search indices:"
          echo "✅ Full-text search updated"
          echo "✅ Tag-based navigation refreshed"
          echo "✅ Related articles index rebuilt"
          echo "✅ Help system synchronized"
          
          # Send to search service
          curl -X POST "https://search.jobpost.com/api/wiki/index" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d '{
              "pages": ${{ toJson(github.event.pages) }},
              "timestamp": "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
            }'
    🔍 Updates JobPost knowledge base search index for better discoverability

    5. Documentation analytics

    on:
  gollum

jobs:
  track-documentation-metrics:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Track JobPost documentation analytics
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost documentation analytics"
          echo "Tracking Wiki activity for insights"
          
          # Analyze documentation patterns
          echo "JobPost documentation metrics:"
          echo "- Most active contributors"
          echo "- Popular documentation topics"
          echo "- Documentation growth trends"
          echo "- Knowledge gaps identification"
          echo "- User engagement patterns"
          
          # Generate insights
          HOUR=$(date +%H)
          if [ $HOUR -ge 9 ] && [ $HOUR -le 17 ]; then
            echo "Documentation updated during business hours"
            URGENCY="normal"
          else
            echo "After-hours documentation update detected"
            URGENCY="low"
          fi
          
          # Store analytics data
          echo "Storing JobPost documentation analytics"
          echo "Editor: ${{ github.event.sender.login }}"
          echo "Pages modified: ${{ github.event.pages.length }}"
          echo "Update time: $(date)"
          echo "Urgency level: $URGENCY"
          
          # Send to analytics dashboard
          curl -X POST "https://analytics.jobpost.com/api/wiki" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d '{
              "event": "wiki_update",
              "editor": "${{ github.event.sender.login }}",
              "pages_count": ${{ github.event.pages.length }},
              "urgency": "'$URGENCY'",
              "timestamp": "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
            }'
    📊 Collects JobPost Wiki analytics for documentation strategy insights

    issue_comment

    Docs: issue_comment event

    Triggers workflows when comments are made on issues or pull requests.

    Full description

    The issue_comment event triggers workflows when comments are created, edited, or deleted on issues and pull requests. This enables automation for comment-based commands, bot interactions, issue management, and collaborative workflows in your JobPost project development.

    Syntax

    on:
  issue_comment:
    types: [created, edited, deleted]

    Activity types

    created: A new comment was added.
    edited: An existing comment was modified.
    deleted: A comment was removed.
    Note: Triggers for both issues and pull request comments. Use github.event.issue.pull_request to distinguish.

    Return value

    Workflow runs with access to comment content, author, and associated issue/PR.

    Examples

    1. JobPost bot commands

    on:
  issue_comment:
    types: [created]

jobs:
  bot-commands:
    if: |
      github.event.issue.pull_request && 
      startsWith(github.event.comment.body, '/deploy')
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Handle JobPost deploy command
        run: |
          echo "🤖 JobPost bot command received"
          echo "Command: ${{ github.event.comment.body }}"
          echo "Author: ${{ github.event.comment.user.login }}"
          echo "PR: #${{ github.event.issue.number }}"
          
          # Parse deploy command
          if [[ "${{ github.event.comment.body }}" == "/deploy staging" ]]; then
            echo "Deploying JobPost to staging environment"
          fi
    🤖 Handles JobPost bot commands via PR comments

    2. Issue triage automation

    on:
  issue_comment:
    types: [created]

jobs:
  triage-comment:
    if: |
      !github.event.issue.pull_request &&
      contains(github.event.comment.body, 'bug report')
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Auto-triage JobPost bug
        run: |
          echo "🏷️ Auto-triaging JobPost bug report"
          echo "Issue: #${{ github.event.issue.number }}"
          echo "Comment by: ${{ github.event.comment.user.login }}"
          echo "Bug details: ${{ github.event.comment.body }}"
          
          # Add bug label and assign to triage team
          echo "Adding 'bug' label to JobPost issue"
    🏷️ Auto-triages JobPost bugs based on comment content

    3. Code review automation

    on:
  issue_comment:
    types: [created]

jobs:
  review-automation:
    if: |
      github.event.issue.pull_request &&
      contains(github.event.comment.body, 'LGTM') &&
      github.event.comment.user.login != github.event.issue.user.login
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Handle JobPost LGTM comment
        run: |
          echo "✅ JobPost code review approved"
          echo "Reviewer: ${{ github.event.comment.user.login }}"
          echo "PR: #${{ github.event.issue.number }}"
          echo "Comment: ${{ github.event.comment.body }}"
          
          # Check if ready for merge
          echo "Checking JobPost PR merge requirements"
    ✅ Handles JobPost code review approvals via LGTM comments

    4. Help command response

    on:
  issue_comment:
    types: [created]

jobs:
  help-response:
    if: |
      contains(github.event.comment.body, '/help') ||
      contains(github.event.comment.body, '@jobpost-bot help')
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Provide JobPost help
        run: |
          echo "❓ JobPost help requested"
          echo "Requester: ${{ github.event.comment.user.login }}"
          echo "Context: ${{ github.event.issue.title }}"
          
          # Provide help information
          echo "Available JobPost commands:"
          echo "- /deploy [environment] - Deploy to environment"
          echo "- /test - Run test suite"
          echo "- /docs - Get documentation links"
    ❓ Provides JobPost help information when requested via comments

    5. Mention notification

    on:
  issue_comment:
    types: [created]

jobs:
  mention-notification:
    if: contains(github.event.comment.body, '@jobpost-team')
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Handle JobPost team mention
        run: |
          echo "📢 JobPost team mentioned"
          echo "Mentioned by: ${{ github.event.comment.user.login }}"
          echo "Issue/PR: #${{ github.event.issue.number }}"
          echo "Comment: ${{ github.event.comment.body }}"
          echo "URL: ${{ github.event.comment.html_url }}"
          
          # Notify team members
          echo "Notifying JobPost team about mention"
    📢 Notifies JobPost team when mentioned in comments

    issues

    Docs: issues event

    Triggers workflows when issue activities occur in the repository.

    Full description

    The issues event triggers workflows when issues are created, updated, closed, or other activities occur. This enables automation for issue management, bug tracking, feature requests, and project management for your JobPost application development.

    Syntax

    on:
  issues:
    types: [opened]  # default

    Activity types

    opened: A new issue was created.
    edited: Issue title or body was modified.
    deleted: An issue was deleted.
    pinned: An issue was pinned to the repository.
    unpinned: An issue was unpinned from the repository.
    closed: An issue was closed.
    reopened: A previously closed issue was reopened.
    assigned: Someone was assigned to the issue.
    unassigned: An assignee was removed from the issue.
    labeled: A label was added to the issue.
    unlabeled: A label was removed from the issue.
    locked: An issue was locked for conversations.
    unlocked: An issue was unlocked for conversations.
    transferred: An issue was transferred to another repository.
    milestoned: An issue was added to a milestone.
    demilestoned: An issue was removed from a milestone.

    Return value

    Workflow runs with access to issue details, labels, assignees, and metadata.

    Examples

    1. JobPost bug triage

    on:
  issues:
    types: [opened]

jobs:
  triage-bug:
    if: contains(github.event.issue.title, 'JobPost') && contains(github.event.issue.labels.*.name, 'bug')
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Triage JobPost bug
        run: |
          echo "🐛 New JobPost bug reported: #${{ github.event.issue.number }}"
          echo "Title: ${{ github.event.issue.title }}"
          echo "Reporter: ${{ github.event.issue.user.login }}"
          echo "Labels: ${{ join(github.event.issue.labels.*.name, ', ') }}"
    🐛 Triages new JobPost bug reports automatically

    2. Feature request processing

    on:
  issues:
    types: [labeled]

jobs:
  process-feature-request:
    if: github.event.label.name == 'feature-request'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Process JobPost feature request
        run: |
          echo "💡 JobPost feature request labeled: #${{ github.event.issue.number }}"
          echo "Feature: ${{ github.event.issue.title }}"
          echo "Requester: ${{ github.event.issue.user.login }}"
          # Add to product backlog, notify product team
    💡 Processes JobPost feature requests when labeled

    3. Issue assignment notification

    on:
  issues:
    types: [assigned]

jobs:
  notify-assignee:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Notify JobPost issue assignee
        run: |
          echo "👤 JobPost issue assigned: #${{ github.event.issue.number }}"
          echo "Assigned to: ${{ github.event.assignee.login }}"
          echo "Issue: ${{ github.event.issue.title }}"
          echo "Priority: ${{ contains(github.event.issue.labels.*.name, 'high-priority') && 'HIGH' || 'NORMAL' }}"
    👤 Notifies assignees of JobPost issue assignments

    4. Closed issue analysis

    on:
  issues:
    types: [closed]

jobs:
  analyze-closure:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Analyze JobPost issue closure
        run: |
          echo "✅ JobPost issue closed: #${{ github.event.issue.number }}"
          echo "State reason: ${{ github.event.issue.state_reason }}"
          echo "Closed by: ${{ github.event.issue.closed_by.login }}"
          echo "Resolution time: Created ${{ github.event.issue.created_at }}, Closed ${{ github.event.issue.closed_at }}"
    ✅ Analyzes JobPost issue resolution metrics

    5. Issue template validation

    on:
  issues:
    types: [opened, edited]

jobs:
  validate-template:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Validate JobPost issue template
        run: |
          BODY="${{ github.event.issue.body }}"
          echo "📝 Validating JobPost issue template: #${{ github.event.issue.number }}"
          
          if [[ "$BODY" == *"Steps to Reproduce"* ]] && [[ "$BODY" == *"Expected Behavior"* ]]; then
            echo "✅ Issue follows JobPost template"
          else
            echo "❌ Issue missing required JobPost template sections"
          fi
    📝 Validates JobPost issues follow required templates

    label

    Docs: label event

    Triggers when repository labels are created, edited, or deleted.

    Full description

    The label event triggers when labels are managed in your JobPost repository. This enables label governance, automated workflows based on label changes, project organization updates, and integration with project management tools.

    Syntax

    on:
  label:
    types: [created, edited, deleted]

    Activity types

    created: A new label was added to the repository.
    edited: An existing label was modified (name, color, description).
    deleted: A label was removed from the repository.

    Return value

    Workflow runs with access to label details including name, color, and description.

    Examples

    1. Label standardization enforcement

    on:
  label:
    types: [created]

jobs:
  enforce-standards:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Enforce JobPost label standards
        run: |
          echo "🏷️ JobPost label standardization"
          echo "New label: ${{ github.event.label.name }}"
          echo "Color: ${{ github.event.label.color }}"
          echo "Description: ${{ github.event.label.description }}"
          
          # Check label naming conventions
          LABEL_NAME="${{ github.event.label.name }}"
          if [[ ! "$LABEL_NAME" =~ ^(bug|feature|enhancement|documentation|question|wontfix|duplicate|invalid|help-wanted|good-first-issue|priority-high|priority-medium|priority-low|status-).*$ ]]; then
            echo "⚠️ JobPost label doesn't follow naming conventions"
            echo "Suggesting standard label format"
          fi
          
          echo "JobPost label standards check completed"
    🏷️ Enforces JobPost label naming and formatting standards

    2. Project board synchronization

    on:
  label:
    types: [created, edited]

jobs:
  sync-project-boards:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Sync JobPost project boards
        run: |
          echo "📋 JobPost project board sync"
          echo "Label: ${{ github.event.label.name }}"
          echo "Action: ${{ github.event.action }}"
          
          # Sync with project management tools
          LABEL_NAME="${{ github.event.label.name }}"
          if [[ "$LABEL_NAME" == "priority-"* ]]; then
            echo "Updating JobPost priority tracking"
            echo "Syncing with project board columns"
          elif [[ "$LABEL_NAME" == "status-"* ]]; then
            echo "Updating JobPost status tracking"
            echo "Syncing with workflow states"
          fi
          
          echo "JobPost project board synchronization completed"
    📋 Synchronizes JobPost labels with project management boards

    3. Label analytics tracking

    on:
  label:
    types: [created, deleted]

jobs:
  track-label-usage:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Track JobPost label analytics
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost label analytics"
          echo "Action: ${{ github.event.action }}"
          echo "Label: ${{ github.event.label.name }}"
          echo "Created by: ${{ github.actor }}"
          
          # Track label lifecycle
          if [ "${{ github.event.action }}" = "created" ]; then
            echo "New JobPost label created: ${{ github.event.label.name }}"
            echo "Recording label creation metrics"
          elif [ "${{ github.event.action }}" = "deleted" ]; then
            echo "JobPost label deleted: ${{ github.event.label.name }}"
            echo "Recording label deletion metrics"
          fi
          
          # Send to analytics service
          curl -X POST "https://analytics.jobpost.com/api/labels" \
            -d "action=${{ github.event.action }}&label=${{ github.event.label.name }}"
    📊 Tracks JobPost label creation and deletion analytics

    4. Automated documentation update

    on:
  label:
    types: [created, edited, deleted]

jobs:
  update-documentation:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Update JobPost label documentation
        run: |
          echo "📚 JobPost label documentation update"
          echo "Label action: ${{ github.event.action }}"
          echo "Label: ${{ github.event.label.name }}"
          echo "Description: ${{ github.event.label.description }}"
          
          # Update label documentation
          echo "Updating JobPost contributing guidelines"
          echo "Refreshing label reference documentation"
          
          # Generate updated label list
          echo "Generating current JobPost label inventory"
          echo "Updating developer documentation with new labels"
          
          # Commit documentation changes
          echo "Committing JobPost label documentation updates"
    📚 Updates JobPost documentation when labels change

    5. Label migration assistance

    on:
  label:
    types: [deleted]

jobs:
  handle-label-migration:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Handle JobPost label migration
        run: |
          echo "🔄 JobPost label migration assistance"
          echo "Deleted label: ${{ github.event.label.name }}"
          echo "Previous color: ${{ github.event.label.color }}"
          
          # Check for issues/PRs using this label
          echo "Checking for JobPost issues/PRs with deleted label"
          echo "Searching for: label:\"${{ github.event.label.name }}\""
          
          # Suggest replacement labels
          DELETED_LABEL="${{ github.event.label.name }}"
          if [[ "$DELETED_LABEL" == "bug"* ]]; then
            echo "Suggesting replacement: 'bug' or 'defect'"
          elif [[ "$DELETED_LABEL" == "feature"* ]]; then
            echo "Suggesting replacement: 'enhancement' or 'new-feature'"
          fi
          
          echo "JobPost label migration guidance provided"
    🔄 Provides JobPost label migration assistance and suggestions

    merge_group

    Docs: merge_group event

    Triggers when pull requests are added or removed from a merge queue group.

    Full description

    The merge_group event triggers when GitHub's merge queue creates or destroys merge groups. This enables advanced merge queue workflows, batch testing, conflict resolution, and sophisticated CI/CD orchestration for your JobPost repository's merge strategy.

    Syntax

    on:
  merge_group:
    types: [checks_requested]

    Activity types

    checks_requested: GitHub requested checks for the merge group (only supported type).
    merge_group: Contains information about the grouped pull requests.
    head_sha: The commit SHA that represents the merge group.
    base_sha: The base commit SHA for the merge group.

    Return value

    Workflow runs with access to merge group details and pull request information.

    Examples

    1. Merge group validation

    on:
  merge_group:
    types: [checks_requested]

jobs:
  validate-merge-group:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Validate JobPost merge group
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.event.merge_group.head_sha }}
      - name: Run JobPost merge group tests
        run: |
          echo "🔀 JobPost merge group validation"
          echo "Group head: ${{ github.event.merge_group.head_sha }}"
          echo "Base SHA: ${{ github.event.merge_group.base_sha }}"
          echo "Head ref: ${{ github.event.merge_group.head_ref }}"
          
          # Run comprehensive tests for the merge group
          echo "Running JobPost integration tests"
          python manage.py test jobpost --settings=test_settings
          
          echo "Validating JobPost merge group compatibility"
    🔀 Validates JobPost merge groups with comprehensive testing

    2. Performance testing for merge groups

    on:
  merge_group:
    types: [checks_requested]

jobs:
  performance-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: JobPost merge group performance testing
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.event.merge_group.head_sha }}
      - name: Run performance tests
        run: |
          echo "⚡ JobPost merge group performance testing"
          echo "Testing combined changes from merge group"
          echo "Head SHA: ${{ github.event.merge_group.head_sha }}"
          
          # Run performance benchmarks
          echo "Running JobPost API performance tests"
          echo "Testing database query performance"
          echo "Measuring JobPost response times"
          
          # Compare with baseline
          echo "Comparing performance against JobPost baseline"
    ⚡ Runs JobPost performance tests on merge groups

    3. Security scanning for merge groups

    on:
  merge_group:
    types: [checks_requested]

jobs:
  security-scan:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: JobPost merge group security scan
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.event.merge_group.head_sha }}
      - name: Run security scans
        run: |
          echo "🔒 JobPost merge group security scanning"
          echo "Scanning combined changes: ${{ github.event.merge_group.head_sha }}"
          
          # Run security tools
          echo "Running JobPost dependency security scan"
          pip install safety
          safety check --json
          
          echo "Running JobPost code security analysis"
          bandit -r jobpost/ -f json
          
          echo "JobPost merge group security scan completed"
    🔒 Performs JobPost security scanning on merge groups

    4. Database migration testing

    on:
  merge_group:
    types: [checks_requested]

jobs:
  test-migrations:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Test JobPost migrations in merge group
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.event.merge_group.head_sha }}
      - name: Run migration tests
        run: |
          echo "🗃️ JobPost merge group migration testing"
          echo "Testing migrations for: ${{ github.event.merge_group.head_sha }}"
          
          # Test forward migrations
          echo "Testing JobPost forward migrations"
          python manage.py migrate --check
          python manage.py makemigrations --check --dry-run
          
          # Test rollback capability
          echo "Testing JobPost migration rollback capability"
          python manage.py migrate jobpost zero --plan
          
          echo "JobPost merge group migration tests passed"
    🗃️ Tests JobPost database migrations in merge groups

    5. Merge group analytics

    on:
  merge_group:
    types: [checks_requested]

jobs:
  track-merge-metrics:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Track JobPost merge group metrics
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost merge group analytics"
          echo "Group head: ${{ github.event.merge_group.head_sha }}"
          echo "Base: ${{ github.event.merge_group.base_sha }}"
          echo "Head ref: ${{ github.event.merge_group.head_ref }}"
          
          # Track merge queue metrics
          echo "Recording JobPost merge group metrics"
          echo "Queue size and processing time"
          echo "Success/failure rates"
          
          # Send to monitoring
          curl -X POST "https://metrics.jobpost.com/api/merge-group" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d '{
              "head_sha": "${{ github.event.merge_group.head_sha }}",
              "timestamp": "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)",
              "ref": "${{ github.event.merge_group.head_ref }}"
            }'
    📊 Tracks JobPost merge group analytics and performance metrics

    milestone

    Docs: milestone event

    Triggers when milestones are created, closed, opened, edited, or deleted.

    Full description

    The milestone event triggers when project milestones are managed in your JobPost repository. This enables release planning automation, progress tracking, team notifications, and integration with project management workflows for coordinated development cycles.

    Syntax

    on:
  milestone:
    types: [created, closed]  # common types

    Activity types

    created: A new milestone was created.
    closed: A milestone was marked as completed.
    opened: A closed milestone was reopened.
    edited: Milestone details were modified.
    deleted: A milestone was removed from the repository.

    Return value

    Workflow runs with access to milestone details, progress, and associated issues/PRs.

    Examples

    1. Release planning automation

    on:
  milestone:
    types: [created]

jobs:
  setup-release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Setup JobPost release planning
        run: |
          echo "🎯 JobPost release planning"
          echo "New milestone: ${{ github.event.milestone.title }}"
          echo "Due date: ${{ github.event.milestone.due_on }}"
          echo "Description: ${{ github.event.milestone.description }}"
          
          # Create release branch if it's a release milestone
          MILESTONE_TITLE="${{ github.event.milestone.title }}"
          if [[ "$MILESTONE_TITLE" == *"v"*"."* ]]; then
            echo "Creating JobPost release branch: release/$MILESTONE_TITLE"
            echo "Setting up release automation"
            echo "Preparing JobPost release checklist"
          fi
          
          echo "JobPost release planning initialized"
    🎯 Sets up JobPost release planning when new milestones are created

    2. Milestone completion celebration

    on:
  milestone:
    types: [closed]

jobs:
  celebrate-completion:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Celebrate JobPost milestone completion
        run: |
          echo "🎉 JobPost milestone completed!"
          echo "Milestone: ${{ github.event.milestone.title }}"
          echo "Closed issues: ${{ github.event.milestone.closed_issues }}"
          echo "Total issues: ${{ github.event.milestone.open_issues + github.event.milestone.closed_issues }}"
          
          # Calculate completion percentage
          TOTAL=$((github.event.milestone.open_issues + github.event.milestone.closed_issues))
          COMPLETION=$(( (github.event.milestone.closed_issues * 100) / TOTAL ))
          echo "Completion rate: $COMPLETION%"
          
          # Send celebration message
          echo "Sending JobPost team celebration message"
          echo "Milestone achievements and statistics"
    🎉 Celebrates JobPost milestone completion with team statistics

    3. Progress tracking dashboard

    on:
  milestone:
    types: [edited]

jobs:
  update-progress:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Update JobPost progress dashboard
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost milestone progress update"
          echo "Milestone: ${{ github.event.milestone.title }}"
          echo "Open issues: ${{ github.event.milestone.open_issues }}"
          echo "Closed issues: ${{ github.event.milestone.closed_issues }}"
          echo "Due date: ${{ github.event.milestone.due_on }}"
          
          # Calculate progress metrics
          TOTAL=$((github.event.milestone.open_issues + github.event.milestone.closed_issues))
          PROGRESS=$(( (github.event.milestone.closed_issues * 100) / TOTAL ))
          
          # Update project dashboard
          curl -X POST "https://dashboard.jobpost.com/api/milestone" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d '{
              "title": "${{ github.event.milestone.title }}",
              "progress": '$PROGRESS',
              "due_date": "${{ github.event.milestone.due_on }}"
            }'
    📊 Updates JobPost progress dashboard with milestone metrics

    4. Due date reminder system

    on:
  milestone:
    types: [created, edited]

jobs:
  setup-reminders:
    if: github.event.milestone.due_on != null
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Setup JobPost milestone reminders
        run: |
          echo "⏰ JobPost milestone reminder setup"
          echo "Milestone: ${{ github.event.milestone.title }}"
          echo "Due date: ${{ github.event.milestone.due_on }}"
          
          # Calculate days until due date
          DUE_DATE="${{ github.event.milestone.due_on }}"
          CURRENT_DATE=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%d)
          
          echo "Setting up JobPost milestone reminders"
          echo "Due date: $DUE_DATE"
          echo "Current date: $CURRENT_DATE"
          
          # Schedule reminder notifications
          echo "Scheduling JobPost team reminders"
          echo "- 2 weeks before due date"
          echo "- 1 week before due date"
          echo "- 2 days before due date"
    ⏰ Sets up JobPost milestone reminder notifications

    5. Release notes preparation

    on:
  milestone:
    types: [closed]

jobs:
  prepare-release-notes:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Prepare JobPost release notes
        run: |
          echo "📝 JobPost release notes preparation"
          echo "Completed milestone: ${{ github.event.milestone.title }}"
          echo "Closed issues: ${{ github.event.milestone.closed_issues }}"
          
          # Generate release notes template
          echo "Generating JobPost release notes for ${{ github.event.milestone.title }}"
          echo ""
          echo "## What's New in ${{ github.event.milestone.title }}"
          echo ""
          echo "### Features"
          echo "### Bug Fixes"
          echo "### Improvements"
          echo "### Breaking Changes"
          echo ""
          echo "### Contributors"
          
          # Collect issue details for release notes
          echo "Collecting JobPost issue details for release notes"
    📝 Prepares JobPost release notes when milestones are completed

    page_build

    Docs: page_build event

    Triggers when GitHub Pages site builds are requested or completed.

    Full description

    The page_build event triggers when GitHub Pages builds your site. This enables post-build automation, deployment notifications, CDN cache invalidation, and integration with external services when your JobPost documentation or marketing site is updated.

    Syntax

    on:
  page_build

    Parameters description

    build: Information about the GitHub Pages build.
    status: Build status (built, errored).
    commit: The commit that triggered the build.
    pusher: User who triggered the build.
    No configuration: Automatically triggers on all page builds.

    Return value

    Workflow runs with access to build status and commit information.

    Examples

    1. Post-build site validation

    on:
  page_build

jobs:
  validate-site:
    if: github.event.build.status == 'built'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Validate JobPost documentation site
        run: |
          echo "✅ JobPost documentation site validation"
          echo "Build status: ${{ github.event.build.status }}"
          echo "Build commit: ${{ github.event.build.commit }}"
          echo "Pusher: ${{ github.event.build.pusher.login }}"
          
          # Wait for site to be available
          sleep 30
          
          # Validate site accessibility
          SITE_URL="https://${{ github.repository_owner }}.github.io/jobpost"
          echo "Testing JobPost documentation site: $SITE_URL"
          
          # Check critical pages
          curl -f "$SITE_URL" || echo "Homepage check failed"
          curl -f "$SITE_URL/api/" || echo "API docs check failed"
          curl -f "$SITE_URL/setup/" || echo "Setup guide check failed"
    ✅ Validates JobPost documentation site after successful build

    2. CDN cache invalidation

    on:
  page_build

jobs:
  invalidate-cache:
    if: github.event.build.status == 'built'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Invalidate JobPost CDN cache
        run: |
          echo "🔄 JobPost CDN cache invalidation"
          echo "New build completed: ${{ github.event.build.commit }}"
          echo "Build pusher: ${{ github.event.build.pusher.login }}"
          
          # Invalidate CloudFlare cache
          echo "Invalidating JobPost documentation CDN cache"
          
          # Purge specific paths
          echo "Purging updated JobPost documentation paths"
          echo "- /api/*"
          echo "- /setup/*"
          echo "- /examples/*"
          
          # Example CDN invalidation
          # curl -X POST "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones/ZONE_ID/purge_cache"
    🔄 Invalidates JobPost CDN cache after documentation updates

    3. SEO and analytics update

    on:
  page_build

jobs:
  update-seo:
    if: github.event.build.status == 'built'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Update JobPost SEO and analytics
        run: |
          echo "🔍 JobPost SEO and analytics update"
          echo "Documentation updated: ${{ github.event.build.commit }}"
          
          # Notify search engines about updates
          echo "Notifying search engines about JobPost documentation updates"
          
          # Update sitemap
          SITE_URL="https://${{ github.repository_owner }}.github.io/jobpost"
          echo "Submitting JobPost sitemap to search engines"
          echo "Sitemap URL: $SITE_URL/sitemap.xml"
          
          # Update analytics
          echo "Updating JobPost documentation analytics tracking"
          echo "Recording new content deployment"
    🔍 Updates JobPost SEO and analytics after documentation builds

    4. Build failure notification

    on:
  page_build

jobs:
  handle-build-failure:
    if: github.event.build.status == 'errored'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Handle JobPost build failure
        run: |
          echo "❌ JobPost documentation build failed"
          echo "Build status: ${{ github.event.build.status }}"
          echo "Failed commit: ${{ github.event.build.commit }}"
          echo "Error by: ${{ github.event.build.pusher.login }}"
          echo "Error message: ${{ github.event.build.error.message }}"
          
          # Send immediate notification
          echo "Sending urgent notification about JobPost docs build failure"
          echo "Alerting documentation team"
          
          # Create issue for tracking
          echo "Creating issue to track JobPost documentation build failure"
          echo "Assigning to: ${{ github.event.build.pusher.login }}"
    ❌ Handles JobPost documentation build failures with notifications

    5. Documentation metrics tracking

    on:
  page_build

jobs:
  track-docs-metrics:
    if: github.event.build.status == 'built'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Track JobPost documentation metrics
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost documentation metrics"
          echo "Build completed: ${{ github.event.build.commit }}"
          echo "Updated by: ${{ github.event.build.pusher.login }}"
          echo "Build duration: ${{ github.event.build.duration }}ms"
          
          # Track documentation update frequency
          echo "Recording JobPost documentation update"
          echo "Author: ${{ github.event.build.pusher.login }}"
          echo "Timestamp: $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
          
          # Send metrics to analytics
          curl -X POST "https://analytics.jobpost.com/api/docs-build" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d '{
              "commit": "${{ github.event.build.commit }}",
              "author": "${{ github.event.build.pusher.login }}",
              "status": "${{ github.event.build.status }}"
            }'
    📊 Tracks JobPost documentation build metrics and analytics

    public

    Docs: public event

    Triggers when a private repository is made public.

    Full description

    The public event triggers when your JobPost repository changes from private to public visibility. This enables open-source preparation workflows, security audits, documentation updates, and community engagement setup for your newly public Django project.

    Syntax

    on:
  public

    Parameters description

    No configuration: Automatically triggers when repository becomes public.
    Repository context: Full access to repository information.
    One-time event: Only occurs during privacy status change.
    Security implications: Important for auditing before public release.
    Community setup: Perfect for initializing open-source workflows.

    Return value

    Workflow runs with repository context when visibility changes to public.

    Examples

    1. Open-source preparation checklist

    on:
  public

jobs:
  opensource-prep:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: JobPost open-source preparation
        run: |
          echo "🌍 JobPost is now public!"
          echo "Repository: ${{ github.repository }}"
          echo "Made public by: ${{ github.actor }}"
          
          # Open-source checklist
          echo "JobPost open-source preparation checklist:"
          echo "✅ Repository is now public"
          echo "🔍 Running security audit"
          echo "📚 Updating documentation"
          echo "🎯 Setting up community guidelines"
          echo "📝 Creating contribution guidelines"
          echo "🏷️ Adding appropriate labels"
          echo "🔒 Reviewing sensitive information"
    🌍 Runs JobPost open-source preparation checklist

    2. Security audit for public release

    on:
  public

jobs:
  security-audit:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: JobPost public security audit
        run: |
          echo "🔒 JobPost public security audit"
          echo "Auditing newly public repository"
          
          # Check for sensitive information
          echo "Scanning JobPost for sensitive data"
          echo "Checking for:"
          echo "- API keys and secrets"
          echo "- Database credentials"
          echo "- Internal URLs and configurations"
          echo "- Personal information"
          
          # Run security tools
          echo "Running JobPost security scanners"
          # git-secrets or similar tools
          echo "Security audit completed for public JobPost repository"
    🔒 Performs JobPost security audit for public repository

    3. Community setup automation

    on:
  public

jobs:
  setup-community:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Setup JobPost community features
        run: |
          echo "👥 JobPost community setup"
          echo "Setting up open-source community features"
          
          # Create community files if they don't exist
          echo "Setting up JobPost community files:"
          
          if [ ! -f "README.md" ]; then
            echo "Creating JobPost README.md"
          fi
          
          if [ ! -f "CONTRIBUTING.md" ]; then
            echo "Creating JobPost CONTRIBUTING.md"
          fi
          
          if [ ! -f "CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md" ]; then
            echo "Creating JobPost CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md"
          fi
          
          if [ ! -f "LICENSE" ]; then
            echo "Adding JobPost LICENSE file"
          fi
    👥 Sets up JobPost community features and guidelines

    4. Documentation enhancement

    on:
  public

jobs:
  enhance-documentation:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Enhance JobPost documentation
        run: |
          echo "📚 JobPost documentation enhancement"
          echo "Enhancing documentation for public release"
          
          # Update documentation for public audience
          echo "Updating JobPost documentation:"
          echo "- Installation instructions"
          echo "- Quick start guide"
          echo "- API documentation"
          echo "- Configuration examples"
          echo "- Deployment guide"
          echo "- Contributing guidelines"
          
          # Generate API documentation
          echo "Generating JobPost API documentation"
          python manage.py spectacular --file schema.yml
          
          echo "JobPost documentation enhancement completed"
    📚 Enhances JobPost documentation for public audience

    5. Marketing and promotion setup

    on:
  public

jobs:
  marketing-setup:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Setup JobPost marketing and promotion
        run: |
          echo "📢 JobPost marketing setup"
          echo "Setting up promotion for newly public repository"
          
          # Social media preparation
          echo "Preparing JobPost social media announcements"
          echo "- Twitter announcement"
          echo "- LinkedIn post"
          echo "- Dev.to article"
          echo "- Reddit submission"
          
          # Developer community outreach
          echo "JobPost developer community outreach:"
          echo "- Django community forums"
          echo "- Python job board discussions"
          echo "- Open source showcases"
          
          # SEO optimization
          echo "Optimizing JobPost for search engines"
          echo "- Repository description"
          echo "- Topic tags"
          echo "- README optimization"
    📢 Sets up JobPost marketing and promotion for public launch

    pull_request

    Docs: pull_request event

    Triggers workflows when pull request activities occur.

    Full description

    The pull_request event triggers workflows when pull requests are opened, updated, closed, or other activities occur. This is crucial for implementing code review automation, running tests on proposed changes, and ensuring code quality before merging JobPost features.

    Syntax

    on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]  # defaults
    branches: [main, develop]
    paths: ['jobpost/**']

    Activity types

    opened: A pull request was created.
    edited: PR title, body, or base branch was edited.
    closed: Pull request was closed (merged or closed without merging).
    reopened: A previously closed pull request was reopened.
    synchronize: New commits were pushed to the PR branch.
    converted_to_draft: PR was converted to a draft.
    ready_for_review: PR was moved from draft to ready state.
    locked: PR conversation was locked.
    unlocked: PR conversation was unlocked.
    review_requested: A reviewer was requested for the PR.
    review_request_removed: A reviewer request was removed.
    auto_merge_enabled: Auto-merge was enabled on the PR.
    auto_merge_disabled: Auto-merge was disabled on the PR.
    labeled: A label was added to the PR.
    unlabeled: A label was removed from the PR.
    assigned: Someone was assigned to the PR.
    unassigned: An assignee was removed from the PR.
    milestoned: PR was added to a milestone.
    demilestoned: PR was removed from a milestone.
    enqueued: PR was added to the merge queue.
    dequeued: PR was removed from the merge queue.

    Return value

    Workflow runs with access to PR details, changed files, and merge information.

    Examples

    1. JobPost PR validation

    on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize]
    paths: ['jobpost/**']

jobs:
  validate-pr:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Validate JobPost PR
        run: |
          echo "🔍 Validating JobPost PR #${{ github.event.number }}"
          echo "Title: ${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}"
          echo "Author: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login }}"
          python manage.py test jobpost --verbosity=2
    🔍 Validates JobPost pull requests with comprehensive testing

    2. Code review automation

    on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened]

jobs:
  auto-review:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Auto-review JobPost changes
        run: |
          echo "🤖 Auto-reviewing JobPost PR #${{ github.event.number }}"
          echo "Files changed: ${{ github.event.pull_request.changed_files }}"
          echo "Additions: ${{ github.event.pull_request.additions }}"
          echo "Deletions: ${{ github.event.pull_request.deletions }}"
    🤖 Automatically reviews JobPost pull requests

    3. Merge automation

    on:
  pull_request:
    types: [closed]

jobs:
  handle-merge:
    if: github.event.pull_request.merged == true
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Handle JobPost merge
        run: |
          echo "✅ JobPost PR #${{ github.event.number }} merged"
          echo "Merged by: ${{ github.event.pull_request.merged_by.login }}"
          echo "Target branch: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.ref }}"
          # Trigger deployment or cleanup
    ✅ Handles JobPost PR merges and triggers follow-up actions

    4. Draft PR handling

    on:
  pull_request:
    types: [ready_for_review]

jobs:
  pr-ready:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: JobPost PR ready for review
        run: |
          echo "📝 JobPost PR #${{ github.event.number }} ready for review"
          echo "From draft to ready by: ${{ github.event.sender.login }}"
          # Notify reviewers, run full test suite
    📝 Handles JobPost PRs transitioning from draft to ready

    5. PR conflict detection

    on:
  pull_request:
    types: [synchronize]

jobs:
  check-conflicts:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Check JobPost PR conflicts
        run: |
          echo "🔄 Checking JobPost PR #${{ github.event.number }} for conflicts"
          echo "Mergeable: ${{ github.event.pull_request.mergeable }}"
          echo "Mergeable state: ${{ github.event.pull_request.mergeable_state }}"
          if [ "${{ github.event.pull_request.mergeable }}" = "false" ]; then
            echo "❌ JobPost PR has merge conflicts"
          fi
    🔄 Checks JobPost PRs for merge conflicts and status

    pull_request_review

    Docs: pull_request_review event

    Triggers when pull request reviews are submitted, edited, or dismissed.

    Full description

    The pull_request_review event triggers when code reviews are conducted on JobPost pull requests. This enables review workflow automation, approval tracking, reviewer assignment, and integration with quality gates for your Django development process.

    Syntax

    on:
  pull_request_review:
    types: [submitted, edited, dismissed]

    Activity types

    submitted: A review was submitted (approved, changes_requested, or commented).
    edited: A review body was modified.
    dismissed: A review was dismissed by a maintainer.
    Review states: Access via github.event.review.state (approved, changes_requested, commented).

    Return value

    Workflow runs with detailed review information and pull request context.

    Examples

    1. Auto-merge on approval

    on:
  pull_request_review:
    types: [submitted]

jobs:
  auto-merge:
    if: github.event.review.state == 'approved'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: JobPost auto-merge on approval
        run: |
          echo "✅ JobPost PR approved for auto-merge"
          echo "PR: #${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
          echo "Reviewer: ${{ github.event.review.user.login }}"
          echo "Review state: ${{ github.event.review.state }}"
          
          # Check if all required reviews are received
          echo "Checking JobPost PR approval requirements"
          echo "Required approvals: 2"
          echo "Current approvals: checking..."
          
          # Auto-merge if conditions are met
          echo "JobPost PR meets approval criteria"
          echo "Triggering auto-merge workflow"
          
          # Use GitHub CLI to merge
          # gh pr merge ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }} --auto --squash
    ✅ Auto-merges JobPost PRs when approval requirements are met

    2. Changes requested notification

    on:
  pull_request_review:
    types: [submitted]

jobs:
  handle-changes-requested:
    if: github.event.review.state == 'changes_requested'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Handle JobPost changes requested
        run: |
          echo "🔄 JobPost PR changes requested"
          echo "PR: #${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
          echo "Reviewer: ${{ github.event.review.user.login }}"
          echo "Author: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login }}"
          echo "Review body: ${{ github.event.review.body }}"
          
          # Notify PR author
          echo "Notifying JobPost PR author about requested changes"
          echo "Setting PR status to 'changes requested'"
          
          # Add label
          echo "Adding 'changes-requested' label to JobPost PR"
          
          # Send notification
          echo "Sending change request notification for JobPost PR"
    🔄 Handles JobPost PR change requests with notifications

    3. Review quality tracking

    on:
  pull_request_review:
    types: [submitted]

jobs:
  track-review-quality:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Track JobPost review quality
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost review quality tracking"
          echo "Reviewer: ${{ github.event.review.user.login }}"
          echo "Review state: ${{ github.event.review.state }}"
          echo "Review length: ${#github.event.review.body}"
          echo "PR size: ${{ github.event.pull_request.additions }} additions, ${{ github.event.pull_request.deletions }} deletions"
          
          # Calculate review metrics
          REVIEW_LENGTH=${#github.event.review.body}
          PR_SIZE=$((github.event.pull_request.additions + github.event.pull_request.deletions))
          
          echo "JobPost review metrics:"
          echo "- Review thoroughness: $REVIEW_LENGTH characters"
          echo "- Code change size: $PR_SIZE lines"
          echo "- Review state: ${{ github.event.review.state }}"
          
          # Send to analytics
          curl -X POST "https://analytics.jobpost.com/api/reviews" \
            -d "reviewer=${{ github.event.review.user.login }}&quality=$REVIEW_LENGTH"
    📊 Tracks JobPost code review quality and thoroughness

    4. Expert reviewer assignment

    on:
  pull_request_review:
    types: [submitted]

jobs:
  assign-expert-reviewers:
    if: github.event.review.state == 'changes_requested' && contains(github.event.review.body, 'needs-expert')
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Assign JobPost expert reviewers
        run: |
          echo "👨‍💼 JobPost expert reviewer assignment"
          echo "PR: #${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
          echo "Current reviewer: ${{ github.event.review.user.login }}"
          echo "Requesting expert review"
          
          # Determine expert based on files changed
          echo "Analyzing JobPost files changed for expert assignment"
          
          # Example expert assignment logic
          if [[ "${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}" == *"database"* ]]; then
            echo "Assigning database expert for JobPost PR"
            EXPERT="db-team"
          elif [[ "${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}" == *"api"* ]]; then
            echo "Assigning API expert for JobPost PR"
            EXPERT="api-team"
          elif [[ "${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}" == *"security"* ]]; then
            echo "Assigning security expert for JobPost PR"
            EXPERT="security-team"
          fi
          
          echo "Expert assigned: $EXPERT"
    👨‍💼 Assigns JobPost expert reviewers for complex changes

    5. Review acknowledgment system

    on:
  pull_request_review:
    types: [submitted]

jobs:
  acknowledge-review:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Acknowledge JobPost review
        run: |
          echo "🙏 JobPost review acknowledgment"
          echo "Reviewer: ${{ github.event.review.user.login }}"
          echo "PR Author: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login }}"
          echo "Review type: ${{ github.event.review.state }}"
          
          # Track reviewer contributions
          echo "Recording JobPost review contribution"
          echo "Reviewer: ${{ github.event.review.user.login }}"
          echo "Review quality score: calculating..."
          
          # Send thank you based on review type
          case "${{ github.event.review.state }}" in
            "approved")
              echo "Thank you for approving the JobPost PR! ✅"
              ;;
            "changes_requested")
              echo "Thank you for the detailed JobPost review feedback! 📝"
              ;;
            "commented")
              echo "Thank you for commenting on the JobPost PR! 💬"
              ;;
          esac
          
          # Update reviewer leaderboard
          echo "Updating JobPost reviewer leaderboard"
    🙏 Acknowledges JobPost reviewers and tracks contributions

    pull_request_review_comment

    Docs: pull_request_review_comment event

    Triggers when pull request review comments are created, edited, or deleted.

    Full description

    The pull_request_review_comment event triggers when line-specific comments are made during JobPost code reviews. This enables detailed code discussion automation, suggestion tracking, automated fixes, and enhanced collaboration workflows.

    Syntax

    on:
  pull_request_review_comment:
    types: [created, edited, deleted]

    Activity types

    created: A new review comment was added to a specific code line.
    edited: An existing review comment was modified.
    deleted: A review comment was removed.
    Context: Access to file path, line number, and diff context via github.event.comment.

    Return value

    Workflow runs with detailed comment context including file path and line information.

    Examples

    1. Auto-suggestion implementation

    on:
  pull_request_review_comment:
    types: [created]

jobs:
  implement-suggestions:
    if: contains(github.event.comment.body, '```suggestion')
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
      - name: Implement JobPost code suggestions
        run: |
          echo "💡 JobPost auto-suggestion implementation"
          echo "Comment by: ${{ github.event.comment.user.login }}"
          echo "File: ${{ github.event.comment.path }}"
          echo "Line: ${{ github.event.comment.line }}"
          echo "Suggestion: ${{ github.event.comment.body }}"
          
          # Extract suggestion from comment
          echo "Extracting JobPost code suggestion"
          echo "Applying suggestion to: ${{ github.event.comment.path }}"
          echo "Target line: ${{ github.event.comment.line }}"
          
          # Apply suggestion (simplified)
          echo "Suggestion applied to JobPost code"
          echo "Committing automated fix"
    💡 Automatically implements JobPost code suggestions from reviews

    2. Code quality issue detection

    on:
  pull_request_review_comment:
    types: [created]

jobs:
  detect-quality-issues:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Detect JobPost code quality issues
        run: |
          echo "🔍 JobPost code quality issue detection"
          echo "Comment: ${{ github.event.comment.body }}"
          echo "File: ${{ github.event.comment.path }}"
          echo "Reviewer: ${{ github.event.comment.user.login }}"
          
          # Detect common quality issues mentioned
          COMMENT="${{ github.event.comment.body }}"
          if [[ "$COMMENT" == *"performance"* ]]; then
            echo "Performance issue detected in JobPost code"
            ISSUE_TYPE="performance"
          elif [[ "$COMMENT" == *"security"* ]]; then
            echo "Security issue detected in JobPost code"
            ISSUE_TYPE="security"
          elif [[ "$COMMENT" == *"bug"* ]]; then
            echo "Bug detected in JobPost code"
            ISSUE_TYPE="bug"
          fi
          
          echo "JobPost quality issue type: $ISSUE_TYPE"
          echo "Adding appropriate labels and tracking"
    🔍 Detects JobPost code quality issues from review comments

    3. Documentation generation from comments

    on:
  pull_request_review_comment:
    types: [created]

jobs:
  generate-docs:
    if: contains(github.event.comment.body, 'needs-docs')
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Generate JobPost documentation
        run: |
          echo "📚 JobPost documentation generation"
          echo "Comment requesting docs: ${{ github.event.comment.body }}"
          echo "File: ${{ github.event.comment.path }}"
          echo "Line: ${{ github.event.comment.line }}"
          
          # Analyze code context for documentation
          FILE_PATH="${{ github.event.comment.path }}"
          if [[ "$FILE_PATH" == *"models.py" ]]; then
            echo "Generating JobPost model documentation"
          elif [[ "$FILE_PATH" == *"views.py" ]]; then
            echo "Generating JobPost API documentation"
          elif [[ "$FILE_PATH" == *"utils.py" ]]; then
            echo "Generating JobPost utility documentation"
          fi
          
          echo "Creating documentation issue for JobPost"
          echo "Assigning to documentation team"
    📚 Generates JobPost documentation requests from review comments

    4. Test coverage analysis

    on:
  pull_request_review_comment:
    types: [created]

jobs:
  analyze-test-coverage:
    if: contains(github.event.comment.body, 'needs-test') || contains(github.event.comment.body, 'test-coverage')
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Analyze JobPost test coverage
        run: |
          echo "🧪 JobPost test coverage analysis"
          echo "Test request: ${{ github.event.comment.body }}"
          echo "File: ${{ github.event.comment.path }}"
          echo "Line: ${{ github.event.comment.line }}"
          
          # Analyze current test coverage for the file
          FILE_PATH="${{ github.event.comment.path }}"
          echo "Analyzing test coverage for: $FILE_PATH"
          
          # Run coverage analysis
          echo "Running JobPost test coverage analysis"
          # coverage run --source=jobpost manage.py test
          # coverage report --show-missing
          
          echo "Current JobPost test coverage:"
          echo "- File coverage: calculating..."
          echo "- Line coverage: calculating..."
          echo "Generating test suggestions for JobPost"
    🧪 Analyzes JobPost test coverage based on review comments

    5. Review comment analytics

    on:
  pull_request_review_comment:
    types: [created, edited, deleted]

jobs:
  track-comment-analytics:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Track JobPost review comment analytics
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost review comment analytics"
          echo "Action: ${{ github.event.action }}"
          echo "Commenter: ${{ github.event.comment.user.login }}"
          echo "File: ${{ github.event.comment.path }}"
          echo "Line: ${{ github.event.comment.line }}"
          echo "Comment length: ${#github.event.comment.body}"
          
          # Track comment patterns
          echo "Recording JobPost review patterns"
          echo "File type: $(echo ${{ github.event.comment.path }} | grep -oE '\.[^.]+$')"
          echo "Comment sentiment: analyzing..."
          
          # Send to analytics
          curl -X POST "https://analytics.jobpost.com/api/review-comments" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d '{
              "action": "${{ github.event.action }}",
              "file": "${{ github.event.comment.path }}",
              "line": ${{ github.event.comment.line }},
              "commenter": "${{ github.event.comment.user.login }}"
            }'
    📊 Tracks JobPost review comment analytics and patterns

    pull_request_target

    Docs: pull_request_target event

    Safely triggers workflows for pull requests from forks with base repository context.

    Full description

    The pull_request_target event is similar to pull_request but runs in the context of the base repository instead of the fork. This provides access to secrets and write permissions while maintaining security, making it ideal for automated processes that need to interact with JobPost PRs from external contributors.

    Syntax

    on:
  pull_request_target:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]  # defaults
    branches: [main]

    Activity types

    Same types as pull_request: Supports all pull_request activity types.
    opened: A pull request was created.
    edited: PR title, body, or base branch was edited.
    closed: Pull request was closed (merged or not merged).
    reopened: A previously closed pull request was reopened.
    synchronize: New commits were pushed to the PR branch.
    converted_to_draft: PR was converted to a draft.
    ready_for_review: PR was moved from draft to ready state.
    labeled/unlabeled: Labels were added or removed.
    assigned/unassigned: Assignees were added or removed.
    Security note: Runs with base repository context - has access to secrets.

    Return value

    Workflow runs with base repository context and access to PR details from forks.

    Examples

    1. Safe JobPost fork PR testing

    on:
  pull_request_target:
    types: [opened, synchronize]

jobs:
  test-fork-pr:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout PR code safely
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
          
      - name: Test JobPost fork PR
        run: |
          echo "🔒 Testing JobPost fork PR safely"
          echo "PR: #${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
          echo "Fork: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name }}"
          echo "Author: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login }}"
          
          # Run tests with base repo context
          python manage.py test jobpost --settings=test_settings
    🔒 Safely tests JobPost PRs from forks with base repository context

    2. Automatic labeling for forks

    on:
  pull_request_target:
    types: [opened]

jobs:
  auto-label:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Auto-label JobPost fork PR
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          echo "🏷️ Auto-labeling JobPost fork PR"
          echo "PR: #${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
          echo "Files changed: ${{ github.event.pull_request.changed_files }}"
          
          # Add labels based on changes
          if [ ${{ github.event.pull_request.changed_files }} -gt 10 ]; then
            echo "Adding 'large-pr' label to JobPost PR"
            # Use GitHub API to add labels
          fi
    🏷️ Automatically labels JobPost PRs from forks based on changes

    3. Fork contributor welcome

    on:
  pull_request_target:
    types: [opened]

jobs:
  welcome-contributor:
    if: github.event.pull_request.author_association == 'FIRST_TIME_CONTRIBUTOR'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Welcome JobPost contributor
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          echo "🎉 Welcome new JobPost contributor!"
          echo "Contributor: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login }}"
          echo "PR title: ${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}"
          
          # Post welcome comment
          echo "Posting welcome message to JobPost PR"
          # Use GitHub API to comment
    🎉 Welcomes first-time JobPost contributors from forks

    4. Security scanning for forks

    on:
  pull_request_target:
    types: [opened, synchronize]

jobs:
  security-scan:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout PR code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
          
      - name: Scan JobPost fork PR
        run: |
          echo "🛡️ Security scanning JobPost fork PR"
          echo "Scanning changes from: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name }}"
          
          # Run security scans
          bandit -r jobpost/ || true
          safety check || true
          
          echo "Security scan completed for JobPost PR"
    🛡️ Performs security scanning on JobPost PRs from forks

    5. Preview deployment for forks

    on:
  pull_request_target:
    types: [opened, synchronize]

jobs:
  deploy-preview:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: preview
    steps:
      - name: Deploy JobPost preview
        run: |
          echo "🚀 Deploying JobPost preview for fork PR"
          echo "PR: #${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
          echo "Branch: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}"
          
          # Deploy preview environment
          PREVIEW_URL="https://pr-${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}.jobpost-preview.app"
          echo "Preview URL: $PREVIEW_URL"
          
          # Comment preview URL back to PR
          echo "Preview deployment completed"
    🚀 Deploys JobPost preview environments for fork PRs

    push

    Docs: push event

    Triggers workflows when commits are pushed to the repository.

    Full description

    The push event is one of the most commonly used triggers in GitHub Actions. It runs workflows when commits are pushed to specified branches or tags. This is essential for continuous integration of your JobPost application, running tests, builds, and deployments when code changes are made.

    Syntax

    on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]
    branches-ignore: [temp/**]
    tags: [v*]
    paths: ['jobpost/**', '**.py']
    paths-ignore: ['docs/**']

    Parameters description

    branches: Specify which branches trigger the workflow.
    branches-ignore: Exclude specific branches from triggering.
    tags: Trigger on specific tag patterns.
    paths: Only trigger when specified files/directories change.
    paths-ignore: Exclude changes in specific paths from triggering.

    Return value

    Workflow runs with access to pushed commits, changed files, and branch information.

    Examples

    1. JobPost CI on main branches

    on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]
    paths: ['jobpost/**', 'requirements.txt']

jobs:
  test-jobpost:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Test JobPost models
        run: |
          python manage.py test jobpost
          echo "Testing JobPost changes in: ${{ github.event.head_commit.message }}"
    🧪 Runs JobPost tests when code changes are pushed to main branches

    2. Production deployment on tags

    on:
  push:
    tags: ['v*.*.*']

jobs:
  deploy-production:
    if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Deploy JobPost to production
        run: |
          echo "🚀 Deploying JobPost version: ${{ github.ref_name }}"
          echo "Commit: ${{ github.sha }}"
          # Deploy to production
    🚀 Deploys JobPost to production when version tags are pushed

    3. Feature branch automation

    on:
  push:
    branches: ['feature/**']
    paths: ['jobpost/**']

jobs:
  feature-validation:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Validate JobPost features
        run: |
          echo "📝 Validating JobPost feature: ${{ github.ref_name }}"
          echo "Changed files: ${{ github.event.head_commit.modified }}"
          python manage.py check jobpost
    📝 Validates JobPost features when pushed to feature branches

    4. Documentation updates

    on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    paths: ['docs/**', '**.md']

jobs:
  update-docs:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Update JobPost documentation
        run: |
          echo "📚 Updating JobPost documentation"
          echo "Changes by: ${{ github.event.head_commit.author.name }}"
          echo "Message: ${{ github.event.head_commit.message }}"
    📚 Updates JobPost documentation when docs are modified

    5. Hotfix deployment

    on:
  push:
    branches: ['hotfix/**']

jobs:
  hotfix-deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Emergency JobPost hotfix
        run: |
          echo "🚨 Emergency JobPost hotfix deployment"
          echo "Branch: ${{ github.ref_name }}"
          echo "Pusher: ${{ github.event.pusher.name }}"
          # Fast-track deployment process
    🚨 Fast-tracks JobPost hotfix deployments

    registry_package

    Docs: registry_package event

    Triggers when packages are published, updated, or deleted in GitHub Package Registry.

    Full description

    The registry_package event triggers when packages are managed in GitHub's registry. This enables package deployment automation, version tracking, downstream notifications, and integration with package management workflows for your JobPost Django application.

    Syntax

    on:
  registry_package:
    types: [published, updated]

    Activity types

    published: A new package version was published to the registry.
    updated: Package metadata or details were updated.

    Return value

    Workflow runs with detailed package information and registry context.

    Examples

    1. Package deployment notification

    on:
  registry_package:
    types: [published]

jobs:
  notify-deployment:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Notify JobPost package deployment
        run: |
