          echo "📦 JobPost package published!"
          echo "Package: ${{ github.event.registry_package.name }}"
          echo "Version: ${{ github.event.registry_package.package_version.version }}"
          echo "Registry: ${{ github.event.registry_package.registry.name }}"
          echo "Published by: ${{ github.actor }}"
          
          # Send deployment notification
          echo "Sending JobPost package deployment notification"
          echo "Package details:"
          echo "- Name: ${{ github.event.registry_package.name }}"
          echo "- Version: ${{ github.event.registry_package.package_version.version }}"
          echo "- Size: ${{ github.event.registry_package.package_version.package_files[0].size }} bytes"
          
          # Notify downstream dependencies
          echo "Notifying JobPost package consumers"
    📦 Notifies team about JobPost package deployments

    2. Automated testing of published packages

    on:
  registry_package:
    types: [published]

jobs:
  test-package:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Test JobPost published package
        run: |
          echo "🧪 Testing JobPost published package"
          echo "Package: ${{ github.event.registry_package.name }}"
          echo "Version: ${{ github.event.registry_package.package_version.version }}"
          
          # Install and test the published package
          echo "Installing JobPost package from registry"
          pip install ${{ github.event.registry_package.name }}==${{ github.event.registry_package.package_version.version }}
          
          # Run package tests
          echo "Running JobPost package integration tests"
          python -c "
          import ${{ github.event.registry_package.name }}
          print('JobPost package imported successfully')
          print('Version:', ${{ github.event.registry_package.name }}.__version__)
          "
          
          echo "JobPost package testing completed"
    🧪 Tests JobPost packages after publication

    3. Version changelog generation

    on:
  registry_package:
    types: [published]

jobs:
  generate-changelog:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Generate JobPost changelog
        run: |
          echo "📝 JobPost package changelog generation"
          echo "New version: ${{ github.event.registry_package.package_version.version }}"
          echo "Package: ${{ github.event.registry_package.name }}"
          
          # Generate changelog entry
          echo "Generating JobPost changelog for version ${{ github.event.registry_package.package_version.version }}"
          
          # Create changelog entry
          DATE=$(date +%Y-%m-%d)
          echo "## [${{ github.event.registry_package.package_version.version }}] - $DATE" >> CHANGELOG.md
          echo "" >> CHANGELOG.md
          echo "### Added" >> CHANGELOG.md
          echo "### Changed" >> CHANGELOG.md
          echo "### Fixed" >> CHANGELOG.md
          echo "" >> CHANGELOG.md
          
          echo "JobPost changelog updated"
    📝 Generates JobPost changelog for new package versions

    4. Package security scanning

    on:
  registry_package:
    types: [published]

jobs:
  security-scan:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Scan JobPost package security
        run: |
          echo "🔒 JobPost package security scanning"
          echo "Scanning package: ${{ github.event.registry_package.name }}"
          echo "Version: ${{ github.event.registry_package.package_version.version }}"
          
          # Download and scan package
          echo "Downloading JobPost package for security scan"
          
          # Run security scanners
          echo "Running JobPost package security analysis"
          echo "- Dependency vulnerability scan"
          echo "- Malware detection"
          echo "- License compliance check"
          
          # Generate security report
          echo "Generating JobPost package security report"
          echo "Security scan completed for JobPost package"
          
          # Alert if issues found
          echo "No security issues detected in JobPost package"
    🔒 Performs security scanning on JobPost packages

    5. Package analytics and metrics

    on:
  registry_package:
    types: [published, updated]

jobs:
  track-package-metrics:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Track JobPost package metrics
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost package analytics"
          echo "Action: ${{ github.event.action }}"
          echo "Package: ${{ github.event.registry_package.name }}"
          echo "Version: ${{ github.event.registry_package.package_version.version }}"
          echo "Registry: ${{ github.event.registry_package.registry.name }}"
          echo "Published by: ${{ github.actor }}"
          
          # Track package metrics
          echo "Recording JobPost package metrics"
          echo "- Release frequency"
          echo "- Version numbering patterns" 
          echo "- Package size trends"
          echo "- Download statistics"
          
          # Send to analytics service
          curl -X POST "https://analytics.jobpost.com/api/packages" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d '{
              "action": "${{ github.event.action }}",
              "package": "${{ github.event.registry_package.name }}",
              "version": "${{ github.event.registry_package.package_version.version }}",
              "timestamp": "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
            }'
    📊 Tracks JobPost package analytics and distribution metrics

    release

    Docs: release event

    Triggers workflows when release activities occur in the repository.

    Full description

    The release event triggers workflows when GitHub releases are created, published, updated, or deleted. This is essential for automating deployment pipelines, distribution processes, and post-release activities for your JobPost application when new versions are released.

    Syntax

    on:
  release:
    types: [published]  # most common

    Activity types

    published: A release was published (most common for deployments).
    unpublished: A release was unpublished (hidden from public view).
    created: A release was created (including drafts).
    edited: Release details were modified (name, body, or assets).
    deleted: A release was deleted permanently.
    prereleased: A pre-release was published.
    released: A draft release was published, or a pre-release was converted to a full release.

    Return value

    Workflow runs with access to release details, tag information, and assets.

    Examples

    1. JobPost production deployment

    on:
  release:
    types: [published]

jobs:
  deploy-production:
    if: "!github.event.release.prerelease"
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Deploy JobPost to production
        run: |
          echo "🚀 Deploying JobPost to production"
          echo "Version: ${{ github.event.release.tag_name }}"
          echo "Release: ${{ github.event.release.name }}"
          echo "Published by: ${{ github.event.release.author.login }}"
          
          # Deploy to production servers
          echo "Deploying JobPost version ${{ github.event.release.tag_name }}"
    🚀 Deploys JobPost to production when releases are published

    2. Pre-release staging deployment

    on:
  release:
    types: [prereleased]

jobs:
  deploy-staging:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Deploy JobPost pre-release to staging
        run: |
          echo "🧪 Deploying JobPost pre-release to staging"
          echo "Pre-release: ${{ github.event.release.tag_name }}"
          echo "Draft: ${{ github.event.release.draft }}"
          echo "Pre-release: ${{ github.event.release.prerelease }}"
          
          # Deploy to staging environment
          echo "Testing JobPost pre-release features"
    🧪 Deploys JobPost pre-releases to staging for testing

    3. Release notification

    on:
  release:
    types: [published]

jobs:
  notify-release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Notify JobPost release
        run: |
          echo "📢 JobPost release notification"
          echo "Release: ${{ github.event.release.name }}"
          echo "Tag: ${{ github.event.release.tag_name }}"
          echo "Body: ${{ github.event.release.body }}"
          echo "Download URL: ${{ github.event.release.html_url }}"
          
          # Send notifications to team/users
    📢 Notifies team and users about new JobPost releases

    4. Release asset processing

    on:
  release:
    types: [published]

jobs:
  process-assets:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Process JobPost release assets
        run: |
          echo "📦 Processing JobPost release assets"
          echo "Assets count: ${{ github.event.release.assets | length }}"
          echo "Tarball: ${{ github.event.release.tarball_url }}"
          echo "Zipball: ${{ github.event.release.zipball_url }}"
          
          # Process and distribute release assets
          echo "Uploading JobPost release to distribution servers"
    📦 Processes and distributes JobPost release assets

    5. Rollback on release deletion

    on:
  release:
    types: [deleted]

jobs:
  handle-rollback:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Handle JobPost release deletion
        run: |
          echo "🔄 JobPost release deleted: ${{ github.event.release.tag_name }}"
          echo "Deleted by: ${{ github.actor }}"
          echo "Previous release action may be needed"
          
          # Check if rollback is needed
          echo "Checking if JobPost rollback is required"
    🔄 Handles JobPost release deletions and potential rollbacks

    repository_dispatch

    Docs: repository_dispatch event

    Triggers workflows via external API calls with custom event types.

    Full description

    The repository_dispatch event allows external systems to trigger workflows via the GitHub API. This enables integration with external services, webhooks from other platforms, and custom automation triggers for your JobPost application from outside GitHub's ecosystem.

    Syntax

    on:
  repository_dispatch:
    types: [custom-event-type, another-event]

    Parameters description

    types: Specify which custom event types trigger this workflow.
    API triggered: Must be triggered via GitHub's REST API.
    Custom payload: Can include custom data in the event payload.
    External integration: Perfect for third-party service integration.
    Authentication: Requires a token with repository permissions.

    Return value

    Workflow runs with access to custom event type and payload data.

    Examples

    1. External JobPost deployment

    on:
  repository_dispatch:
    types: [deploy-jobpost]

jobs:
  external-deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Handle external JobPost deployment
        run: |
          echo "🌐 External JobPost deployment triggered"
          echo "Event type: ${{ github.event.action }}"
          echo "Client payload: ${{ toJson(github.event.client_payload) }}"
          echo "Environment: ${{ github.event.client_payload.environment }}"
          echo "Version: ${{ github.event.client_payload.version }}"
          
          # Deploy based on external request
          echo "Deploying JobPost version ${{ github.event.client_payload.version }}"
    🌐 Handles external JobPost deployment requests via API

    2. Data synchronization trigger

    on:
  repository_dispatch:
    types: [sync-jobpost-data]

jobs:
  sync-data:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Sync JobPost data
        run: |
          echo "🔄 JobPost data synchronization triggered"
          echo "Source: ${{ github.event.client_payload.source }}"
          echo "Data type: ${{ github.event.client_payload.data_type }}"
          echo "Batch size: ${{ github.event.client_payload.batch_size }}"
          
          # Sync data from external source
          python manage.py shell -c "
          print('Syncing JobPost data from external source')
          print('Source: ${{ github.event.client_payload.source }}')
          # Implement data sync logic
          "
    🔄 Synchronizes JobPost data from external systems

    3. Monitoring alert response

    on:
  repository_dispatch:
    types: [jobpost-alert]

jobs:
  handle-alert:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Handle JobPost monitoring alert
        run: |
          echo "🚨 JobPost monitoring alert received"
          echo "Alert type: ${{ github.event.client_payload.alert_type }}"
          echo "Severity: ${{ github.event.client_payload.severity }}"
          echo "Message: ${{ github.event.client_payload.message }}"
          echo "Timestamp: ${{ github.event.client_payload.timestamp }}"
          
          # Respond to alert based on severity
          if [ "${{ github.event.client_payload.severity }}" = "critical" ]; then
            echo "Critical JobPost alert - initiating emergency response"
          fi
    🚨 Responds to JobPost monitoring alerts from external systems

    4. Third-party integration

    on:
  repository_dispatch:
    types: [external-webhook]

jobs:
  process-webhook:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Process external webhook
        run: |
          echo "🔗 Processing external webhook for JobPost"
          echo "Source service: ${{ github.event.client_payload.service }}"
          echo "Event data: ${{ toJson(github.event.client_payload.data) }}"
          
          # Process different webhook sources
          case "${{ github.event.client_payload.service }}" in
            "stripe")
              echo "Processing JobPost payment webhook"
              ;;
            "sendgrid")
              echo "Processing JobPost email webhook"
              ;;
            "slack")
              echo "Processing JobPost Slack webhook"
              ;;
          esac
    🔗 Processes JobPost webhooks from various third-party services

    5. Scheduled maintenance trigger

    on:
  repository_dispatch:
    types: [maintenance-window]

jobs:
  maintenance:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: JobPost maintenance window
        run: |
          echo "🔧 JobPost maintenance window triggered"
          echo "Maintenance type: ${{ github.event.client_payload.maintenance_type }}"
          echo "Duration: ${{ github.event.client_payload.duration }}"
          echo "Scheduled by: ${{ github.event.client_payload.scheduled_by }}"
          
          # Perform maintenance tasks
          case "${{ github.event.client_payload.maintenance_type }}" in
            "database")
              echo "Performing JobPost database maintenance"
              ;;
            "cache")
              echo "Clearing JobPost caches"
              ;;
            "logs")
              echo "Rotating JobPost logs"
              ;;
          esac
    🔧 Performs JobPost maintenance tasks triggered by external scheduling

    schedule

    Docs: schedule event

    Triggers workflows on a scheduled basis using cron syntax.

    Full description

    The schedule event allows workflows to run on a time-based schedule using POSIX cron syntax. This is essential for automating regular maintenance tasks, running periodic tests, generating reports, backing up data, and performing routine operations for your JobPost application.

    Syntax

    on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 2 * * *'    # Daily at 2 AM UTC
    - cron: '0 0 * * 0'    # Weekly on Sunday
    - cron: '0 0 1 * *'    # Monthly on 1st

    Parameters description

    Cron format: minute hour day(month) month day(week) (0-59 0-23 1-31 1-12 0-6).
    UTC timezone: All scheduled workflows run in UTC time.
    Multiple schedules: Define multiple cron expressions for different intervals.
    Branch restriction: Only runs from the default branch (usually main).
    Minimum interval: GitHub enforces a 5-minute minimum interval.

    Return value

    Workflow runs at specified intervals with schedule context information.

    Examples

    1. Daily JobPost cleanup

    on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 2 * * *'  # Daily at 2 AM UTC

jobs:
  daily-cleanup:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: JobPost daily maintenance
        run: |
          echo "🧹 Running daily JobPost cleanup at $(date)"
          # Clean expired job postings
          python manage.py shell -c "
          from jobpost.models import JobPost
          from django.utils import timezone
          expired = JobPost.objects.filter(expiry__lt=timezone.now().date())
          count = expired.count()
          expired.delete()
          print(f'Removed {count} expired JobPost entries')
          "
    🧹 Runs daily JobPost cleanup removing expired job postings

    2. Weekly JobPost reports

    on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 9 * * 1'  # Monday at 9 AM UTC

jobs:
  weekly-report:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Generate JobPost weekly report
        run: |
          echo "📊 Generating weekly JobPost report for $(date +%Y-%m-%d)"
          python manage.py shell -c "
          from jobpost.models import JobPost, Skills, Author
          from datetime import datetime, timedelta
          
          week_ago = datetime.now() - timedelta(days=7)
          new_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(date__gte=week_ago).count()
          total_jobs = JobPost.objects.count()
          
          print(f'New jobs this week: {new_jobs}')
          print(f'Total active jobs: {total_jobs}')
          "
    📊 Generates weekly JobPost analytics and reports

    3. Monthly JobPost backup

    on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 1 1 * *'  # 1st of month at 1 AM UTC

jobs:
  monthly-backup:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: JobPost monthly backup
        run: |
          echo "💾 Creating monthly JobPost backup for $(date +%B-%Y)"
          # Export JobPost data
          python manage.py dumpdata jobpost --format=json > "jobpost-backup-$(date +%Y%m).json"
          echo "Backup created: jobpost-backup-$(date +%Y%m).json"
    💾 Creates monthly backups of JobPost application data

    4. Hourly JobPost monitoring

    on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 * * * *'  # Every hour

jobs:
  health-check:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: JobPost health monitoring
        run: |
          echo "❤️ JobPost health check at $(date)"
          # Check database connectivity
          python manage.py shell -c "
          from django.db import connection
          try:
              connection.ensure_connection()
              print('✅ Database connection healthy')
          except Exception as e:
              print(f'❌ Database error: {e}')
          "
    ❤️ Monitors JobPost application health every hour

    5. Business hours notifications

    on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 9-17 * * 1-5'  # Business hours, weekdays

jobs:
  business-notifications:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: JobPost business hours check
        run: |
          echo "🏢 JobPost business hours notification at $(date)"
          python manage.py shell -c "
          from jobpost.models import JobPost
          from datetime import datetime, timedelta
          
          today_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(date__date=datetime.now().date())
          urgent_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(
              date__gte=datetime.now() - timedelta(hours=1)
          )
          print(f'Jobs posted today: {today_jobs.count()}')
          print(f'Urgent jobs (last hour): {urgent_jobs.count()}')
          "
    🏢 Sends JobPost notifications during business hours only

    status

    Docs: status event

    Triggers when the status of a Git commit changes through external status APIs.

    Full description

    The status event triggers when the status of a Git commit changes via GitHub's Status API. This is useful for responding to external CI/CD systems, third-party integrations, or manual status updates that affect your JobPost application's commit statuses.

    Syntax

    on:
  status

    Parameters description

    No configuration: Triggers on any status change.
    External systems: Responds to status updates from external services.
    Commit context: Provides access to commit and status information.
    Status states: Handles pending, success, failure, and error states.
    Multiple statuses: Can trigger multiple times for different status contexts.

    Return value

    Workflow runs with access to commit status change information.

    Examples

    1. JobPost deployment on success

    on:
  status

jobs:
  deploy-on-success:
    if: github.event.state == 'success' && github.event.context == 'ci/external-tests'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Deploy JobPost on external test success
        run: |
          echo "✅ JobPost external tests passed"
          echo "Commit: ${{ github.event.sha }}"
          echo "Status: ${{ github.event.state }}"
          echo "Context: ${{ github.event.context }}"
          echo "Description: ${{ github.event.description }}"
          
          echo "Deploying JobPost to production"
          # Deploy logic here
    ✅ Deploys JobPost when external tests pass

    2. Failure notification system

    on:
  status

jobs:
  notify-failure:
    if: github.event.state == 'failure'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Notify JobPost team of failure
        run: |
          echo "❌ JobPost status failure detected"
          echo "Context: ${{ github.event.context }}"
          echo "Target URL: ${{ github.event.target_url }}"
          echo "Commit: ${{ github.event.commit.sha }}"
          echo "Author: ${{ github.event.commit.commit.author.name }}"
          
          # Send notification to team
          echo "Sending failure notification for JobPost"
    ❌ Notifies JobPost team when external status checks fail

    3. Status aggregation dashboard

    on:
  status

jobs:
  update-dashboard:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Update JobPost status dashboard
        run: |
          echo "📊 Updating JobPost status dashboard"
          echo "State: ${{ github.event.state }}"
          echo "Context: ${{ github.event.context }}"
          echo "Branch: ${{ github.event.branches[0].name }}"
          
          # Update external dashboard
          curl -X POST "https://dashboard.jobpost.com/api/status" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d '{
              "commit": "${{ github.event.sha }}",
              "state": "${{ github.event.state }}",
              "context": "${{ github.event.context }}"
            }'
    📊 Updates JobPost status dashboard with external status changes

    4. Quality gate enforcement

    on:
  status

jobs:
  enforce-quality-gate:
    if: github.event.context == 'sonarqube' && github.event.state == 'failure'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Handle JobPost quality gate failure
        run: |
          echo "🚫 JobPost quality gate failed"
          echo "Analysis URL: ${{ github.event.target_url }}"
          echo "Commit: ${{ github.event.sha }}"
          
          # Block deployment and notify
          echo "Blocking JobPost deployment due to quality gate failure"
          echo "Creating issue for code quality review"
          
          # Create GitHub issue
          echo "Quality gate failure requires attention"
    🚫 Enforces JobPost quality gates and blocks deployment on failure

    5. Multi-context validation

    on:
  status

jobs:
  validate-all-contexts:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Check JobPost validation status
        run: |
          echo "🔍 JobPost multi-context validation"
          echo "Current status: ${{ github.event.state }}"
          echo "Context: ${{ github.event.context }}"
          
          # Check if all required contexts are successful
          REQUIRED_CONTEXTS="ci/tests ci/lint ci/security ci/performance"
          echo "Required contexts for JobPost: $REQUIRED_CONTEXTS"
          
          if [ "${{ github.event.state }}" = "success" ]; then
            echo "✅ ${{ github.event.context }} passed for JobPost"
          fi
    🔍 Validates JobPost against multiple required status contexts

    watch

    Docs: watch event

    Triggers when someone stars or unstars the repository.

    Full description

    The watch event triggers when a user stars or unstars your JobPost repository. This event is useful for tracking repository popularity, sending thank you messages to new stargazers, or triggering marketing analytics for your JobPost project.

    Syntax

    on:
  watch:
    types: [started]

    Parameters description

    types: [started]: Only 'started' type is available (when starred).
    User information: Provides details about the user who starred.
    Repository context: Access to repository information.
    Star counting: Can be used for analytics and notifications.
    Community building: Perfect for engaging with your JobPost community.

    Return value

    Workflow runs with information about the starring event and user.

    Examples

    1. Thank you message for JobPost stars

    on:
  watch:
    types: [started]

jobs:
  thank-stargazer:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Thank JobPost stargazer
        run: |
          echo "⭐ New JobPost stargazer!"
          echo "User: ${{ github.event.sender.login }}"
          echo "Profile: ${{ github.event.sender.html_url }}"
          echo "Total stars: ${{ github.event.repository.stargazers_count }}"
          
          echo "Thank you for starring JobPost! 🎉"
          echo "Consider contributing to our Django job board project"
    ⭐ Thanks new JobPost stargazers and encourages contribution

    2. Star milestone celebrations

    on:
  watch:
    types: [started]

jobs:
  milestone-check:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Check JobPost star milestones
        run: |
          echo "🎯 JobPost star milestone check"
          STARS=${{ github.event.repository.stargazers_count }}
          echo "Current stars: $STARS"
          
          # Check for milestone achievements
          if [ $((STARS % 100)) -eq 0 ]; then
            echo "🎉 JobPost reached $STARS stars!"
            echo "Creating celebration issue"
          elif [ $((STARS % 50)) -eq 0 ]; then
            echo "🌟 JobPost milestone: $STARS stars"
          fi
    🎯 Celebrates JobPost star milestones and creates special issues

    3. Analytics tracking

    on:
  watch:
    types: [started]

jobs:
  track-analytics:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Track JobPost star analytics
        run: |
          echo "📈 JobPost star analytics tracking"
          echo "Stargazer: ${{ github.event.sender.login }}"
          echo "Timestamp: $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
          echo "Repository: ${{ github.event.repository.name }}"
          echo "Stars: ${{ github.event.repository.stargazers_count }}"
          
          # Send to analytics service
          curl -X POST "https://analytics.jobpost.com/api/star" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d '{
              "user": "${{ github.event.sender.login }}",
              "repo": "${{ github.event.repository.name }}",
              "stars": ${{ github.event.repository.stargazers_count }}
            }'
    📈 Tracks JobPost star analytics for growth insights

    4. Community engagement

    on:
  watch:
    types: [started]

jobs:
  community-engagement:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Engage JobPost community
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          echo "👥 JobPost community engagement"
          echo "New member: ${{ github.event.sender.login }}"
          
          # Check if user is a developer (has public repos)
          USER_REPOS=$(curl -s "https://api.github.com/users/${{ github.event.sender.login }}/repos" | jq length)
          
          if [ $USER_REPOS -gt 5 ]; then
            echo "Developer detected! Inviting to contribute to JobPost"
            # Could create a welcoming issue or discussion
          fi
    👥 Engages JobPost community and identifies potential contributors

    5. Newsletter subscription invite

    on:
  watch:
    types: [started]

jobs:
  newsletter-invite:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Invite to JobPost newsletter
        run: |
          echo "📧 JobPost newsletter invitation"
          echo "Stargazer: ${{ github.event.sender.login }}"
          echo "Email lookup needed for newsletter"
          
          # Create a welcoming issue with newsletter info
          echo "Creating welcome issue with JobPost newsletter info"
          echo "Mentioning documentation and contribution guidelines"
          
          # Log for newsletter team
          echo "New potential subscriber: ${{ github.event.sender.login }}"
    📧 Invites JobPost stargazers to newsletter and community

    workflow_call

    Docs: workflow_call event

    Enables workflows to be called by other workflows as reusable components.

    Full description

    The workflow_call event allows workflows to be used as reusable components by other workflows. This promotes code reuse, modular CI/CD design, and standardization across your JobPost project by creating shared workflow templates that can be called with different parameters.

    Syntax

    on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      environment:
        required: true
        type: string
    secrets:
      deploy_token:
        required: true

    Parameters description

    inputs: Define input parameters that calling workflows must provide.
    secrets: Specify secrets that calling workflows must pass.
    outputs: Define outputs that the workflow returns to callers.
    Reusability: Enables workflow composition and standardization.
    Cross-repository: Can be called from other repositories if public.

    Return value

    Reusable workflow that can be called by other workflows with specified inputs and secrets.

    Examples

    1. Reusable JobPost deployment

    on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      environment:
        required: true
        type: string
      version:
        required: false
        type: string
        default: 'latest'
    secrets:
      deploy_key:
        required: true

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Deploy JobPost
        run: |
          echo "🚀 Deploying JobPost to ${{ inputs.environment }}"
          echo "Version: ${{ inputs.version }}"
          echo "Using deploy key: ${{ secrets.deploy_key != '' && 'Yes' || 'No' }}"
    🚀 Reusable JobPost deployment workflow for multiple environments

    2. JobPost testing template

    on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      python_version:
        required: false
        type: string
        default: '3.11'
      test_type:
        required: true
        type: choice
        options: [unit, integration, e2e]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Test JobPost
        run: |
          echo "🧪 Running JobPost ${{ inputs.test_type }} tests"
          echo "Python version: ${{ inputs.python_version }}"
          
          case "${{ inputs.test_type }}" in
            "unit")
              python manage.py test jobpost.tests.unit
              ;;
            "integration")
              python manage.py test jobpost.tests.integration
              ;;
            "e2e")
              python manage.py test jobpost.tests.e2e
              ;;
          esac
    🧪 Reusable JobPost testing workflow for different test types

    3. Database migration workflow

    on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      migration_direction:
        required: true
        type: choice
        options: [forward, backward]
      target_migration:
        required: false
        type: string
    outputs:
      migration_status:
        description: "Migration completion status"
        value: ${{ jobs.migrate.outputs.status }}

jobs:
  migrate:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      status: ${{ steps.migrate.outputs.result }}
    steps:
      - name: Run JobPost migration
        id: migrate
        run: |
          echo "🗃️ JobPost database migration"
          echo "Direction: ${{ inputs.migration_direction }}"
          echo "Target: ${{ inputs.target_migration || 'latest' }}"
          
          if [ "${{ inputs.migration_direction }}" = "forward" ]; then
            python manage.py migrate ${{ inputs.target_migration }}
            echo "result=success" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi
    🗃️ Reusable JobPost database migration workflow with outputs

    4. Security scanning template

    on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      scan_type:
        required: true
        type: choice
        options: [dependency, code, container]
      severity_threshold:
        required: false
        type: choice
        options: [low, medium, high, critical]
        default: 'medium'

jobs:
  security-scan:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: JobPost security scan
        run: |
          echo "🔒 JobPost security scan: ${{ inputs.scan_type }}"
          echo "Threshold: ${{ inputs.severity_threshold }}"
          
          case "${{ inputs.scan_type }}" in
            "dependency")
              pip install safety
              safety check --json
              ;;
            "code")
              bandit -r jobpost/
              ;;
            "container")
              echo "Scanning JobPost container images"
              ;;
          esac
    🔒 Reusable JobPost security scanning workflow

    5. Release preparation workflow

    on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      release_type:
        required: true
        type: choice
        options: [patch, minor, major]
      pre_release:
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: false
    outputs:
      version:
        description: "New version number"
        value: ${{ jobs.prepare.outputs.version }}

jobs:
  prepare:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      version: ${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}
    steps:
      - name: Prepare JobPost release
        id: version
        run: |
          echo "📦 Preparing JobPost release"
          echo "Type: ${{ inputs.release_type }}"
          echo "Pre-release: ${{ inputs.pre_release }}"
          
          # Calculate new version
          NEW_VERSION="1.2.3"  # Logic to increment version
          echo "new_version=$NEW_VERSION" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "New JobPost version: $NEW_VERSION"
    📦 Reusable JobPost release preparation workflow

    workflow_dispatch

    Docs: workflow_dispatch event

    Enables manual triggering of workflows with optional input parameters.

    Full description

    The workflow_dispatch event allows workflows to be triggered manually from the GitHub Actions UI, API, or CLI. You can define input parameters that users can specify when triggering the workflow, making it perfect for on-demand operations like deployments, data migrations, or maintenance tasks for your JobPost application.

    Syntax

    on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      environment:
        description: 'Deployment environment'
        required: true
        default: 'staging'
        type: choice
        options:
          - staging
          - production

    Parameters description

    inputs: Define input parameters for manual triggers.
    description: Human-readable description of the input.
    required: Whether the input is mandatory (true/false).
    default: Default value if not specified.
    type: Input type (string, number, boolean, choice, environment).

    Return value

    Workflow runs with input values accessible via github.event.inputs.*.

    Examples

    1. Manual JobPost deployment

    on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      environment:
        description: 'Target environment'
        required: true
        type: choice
        options: [staging, production]
      version:
        description: 'JobPost version'
        required: true
        type: string

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Deploy JobPost
        run: |
          echo "🚀 Deploying JobPost to ${{ github.event.inputs.environment }}"
          echo "Version: ${{ github.event.inputs.version }}"
          echo "Triggered by: ${{ github.actor }}"
    🚀 Enables manual JobPost deployments with environment and version selection

    2. Data migration workflow

    on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      migration_type:
        description: 'Type of migration'
        type: choice
        options: [forward, backward, fake]
      migration_name:
        description: 'Migration name'
        required: true
        type: string

jobs:
  migrate:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Run JobPost migration
        run: |
          echo "🗃️ Running JobPost migration: ${{ github.event.inputs.migration_name }}"
          echo "Type: ${{ github.event.inputs.migration_type }}"
          
          case "${{ github.event.inputs.migration_type }}" in
            "forward")
              python manage.py migrate ${{ github.event.inputs.migration_name }}
              ;;
            "backward")
              python manage.py migrate ${{ github.event.inputs.migration_name }} --backwards
              ;;
            "fake")
              python manage.py migrate ${{ github.event.inputs.migration_name }} --fake
              ;;
          esac
    🗃️ Runs JobPost database migrations with manual control

    3. Data seeding workflow

    on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      record_count:
        description: 'Number of JobPost records to create'
        required: true
        type: number
        default: 10
      include_skills:
        description: 'Include skills data'
        type: boolean
        default: true

jobs:
  seed-data:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Seed JobPost data
        run: |
          echo "🌱 Seeding JobPost data"
          echo "Records: ${{ github.event.inputs.record_count }}"
          echo "Include skills: ${{ github.event.inputs.include_skills }}"
          
          python manage.py shell -c "
          from jobpost.models import seed_demo_data
          
          # Create the specified number of records
          for i in range(${{ github.event.inputs.record_count }}):
              data = seed_demo_data()
              print(f'Created JobPost batch {i+1}')
          "
    🌱 Seeds JobPost database with specified number of test records

    4. Performance testing

    on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      test_duration:
        description: 'Test duration (minutes)'
        type: number
        default: 5
      concurrent_users:
        description: 'Concurrent users'
        type: number
        default: 10

jobs:
  performance-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Run JobPost performance test
        run: |
          echo "⚡ Running JobPost performance test"
          echo "Duration: ${{ github.event.inputs.test_duration }} minutes"
          echo "Users: ${{ github.event.inputs.concurrent_users }}"
          
          # Simulate load testing
          echo "Testing JobPost endpoints with ${{ github.event.inputs.concurrent_users }} concurrent users"
          echo "Test will run for ${{ github.event.inputs.test_duration }} minutes"
    ⚡ Runs JobPost performance tests with configurable parameters

    5. Environment cleanup

    on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      cleanup_type:
        description: 'Cleanup type'
        type: choice
        options: [cache, logs, temp-files, all]
      confirm_cleanup:
        description: 'Confirm cleanup (type YES)'
        required: true
        type: string

jobs:
  cleanup:
    if: github.event.inputs.confirm_cleanup == 'YES'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: JobPost environment cleanup
        run: |
          echo "🧹 JobPost cleanup: ${{ github.event.inputs.cleanup_type }}"
          echo "Confirmed by: ${{ github.actor }}"
          
          case "${{ github.event.inputs.cleanup_type }}" in
            "cache")
              echo "Clearing JobPost cache"
              ;;
            "logs")
              echo "Cleaning JobPost logs"
              ;;
            "temp-files")
              echo "Removing JobPost temporary files"
              ;;
            "all")
              echo "Full JobPost cleanup"
              ;;
          esac
    🧹 Performs JobPost environment cleanup with safety confirmation

    workflow_run

    Docs: workflow_run event

    Triggers when another workflow run is requested or completed.

    Full description

    The workflow_run event allows workflows to trigger based on the completion or start of other workflows. This enables workflow chaining, post-processing tasks, and conditional flows for your JobPost application, creating sophisticated CI/CD pipelines with dependencies between different workflow stages.

    Syntax

    on:
  workflow_run:
    workflows: ["CI Tests", "Build"]
    types: [completed]  # default

    Activity types

    completed: The specified workflow has finished (regardless of conclusion).
    requested: The specified workflow was requested to start (before actual execution).
    in_progress: The specified workflow has started running.
    workflows: Required - specify which workflows trigger this event.
    branches: Optional - filter by branches that triggered the upstream workflow.
    Workflow chaining: Create dependent workflow sequences based on upstream results.

    Return value

    Workflow runs with access to triggering workflow information and status.

    Examples

    1. JobPost deployment after tests

    on:
  workflow_run:
    workflows: ["JobPost CI Tests"]
    types: [completed]
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  deploy-after-tests:
    if: github.event.workflow_run.conclusion == 'success'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Deploy JobPost after successful tests
        run: |
          echo "🚀 Deploying JobPost after test success"
          echo "Triggering workflow: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.name }}"
          echo "Status: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.conclusion }}"
          echo "Branch: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.head_branch }}"
          echo "Commit: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.head_sha }}"
          
          echo "Starting JobPost production deployment"
          # Deployment logic here
    🚀 Deploys JobPost only after successful test completion

    2. Security scan after build

    on:
  workflow_run:
    workflows: ["JobPost Build"]
    types: [completed]

jobs:
  security-scan:
    if: github.event.workflow_run.conclusion == 'success'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Security scan JobPost build
        run: |
          echo "🔒 Security scanning JobPost build artifacts"
          echo "Build workflow: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.name }}"
          echo "Build ID: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.id }}"
          echo "Artifacts available: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.run_number }}"
          
          # Download and scan build artifacts
          echo "Downloading JobPost build artifacts"
          echo "Running security scans on JobPost images"
    🔒 Performs security scanning on JobPost build artifacts

    3. Notification on failure

    on:
  workflow_run:
    workflows: ["JobPost Tests", "JobPost Build", "JobPost Deploy"]
    types: [completed]

jobs:
  notify-on-failure:
    if: github.event.workflow_run.conclusion == 'failure'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Notify JobPost team of failure
        run: |
          echo "❌ JobPost workflow failure notification"
          echo "Failed workflow: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.name }}"
          echo "Run number: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.run_number }}"
          echo "URL: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.html_url }}"
          echo "Actor: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.actor.login }}"
          
          # Send notification to team
          echo "Sending Slack notification about JobPost failure"
    ❌ Notifies JobPost team when any critical workflow fails

    4. Performance testing after deployment

    on:
  workflow_run:
    workflows: ["JobPost Deploy"]
    types: [completed]

jobs:
  performance-test:
    if: github.event.workflow_run.conclusion == 'success'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Run JobPost performance tests
        run: |
          echo "⚡ JobPost performance testing"
          echo "Deployment completed: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.name }}"
          echo "Environment: production"
          
          # Wait for deployment to be ready
          sleep 30
          
          # Run performance tests
          echo "Testing JobPost API performance"
          echo "Testing JobPost UI load times"
          echo "Testing JobPost database queries"
    ⚡ Runs JobPost performance tests after successful deployment

    5. Workflow metrics collection

    on:
  workflow_run:
    workflows: ["JobPost CI", "JobPost Deploy"]
    types: [completed]

jobs:
  collect-metrics:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Collect JobPost workflow metrics
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost workflow metrics collection"
          echo "Workflow: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.name }}"
          echo "Duration: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.updated_at }} - ${{ github.event.workflow_run.created_at }}"
          echo "Conclusion: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.conclusion }}"
          echo "Attempt: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.run_attempt }}"
          
          # Send metrics to monitoring system
          curl -X POST "https://metrics.jobpost.com/api/workflow" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d '{
              "workflow": "${{ github.event.workflow_run.name }}",
              "conclusion": "${{ github.event.workflow_run.conclusion }}",
              "duration": "calculated_duration",
              "run_number": ${{ github.event.workflow_run.run_number }}
            }'
    📊 Collects JobPost workflow metrics for performance monitoring

    About workflow commands

    Docs: Workflow commands documentation

    You can use workflow commands when running shell commands in a workflow or in an action's code.

    Full description

    Actions can communicate with the runner machine to set environment variables, output values used by other actions, add debug messages to the output logs, and other tasks. Most workflow commands use the echo command in a specific format, while others are invoked by writing to a file. For more information, see Environment files.

    📝 Note: Workflow command and parameter names are case insensitive.
    ⚠️ Warning: If you are using Command Prompt, omit double quote characters (") when using workflow commands.

    Example of a workflow command

    echo "::workflow-command parameter1={data},parameter2={data}::{command value}"

    Syntax

    # Basic workflow command structure
echo "::command parameter1=value1,parameter2=value2::command value"

# Examples
echo "::debug::This is a debug message"
echo "::notice file=app.js,line=10::This is a notice"
echo "::error title=Error Title::This is an error message"

    Parameters description

    workflow-command: The command name (e.g., debug, notice, error, warning, group, add-mask)
    parameter1, parameter2: Optional parameters specific to each command (e.g., file, line, col, title)
    command value: The message or value for the command
    Note: Command names and parameter names are case-insensitive, but command values are case-sensitive.

    Common workflow commands

    Logging Commands:
    • ::debug:: - Debug message (requires ACTIONS_STEP_DEBUG=true)
    • ::notice:: - Notice message (creates annotation)
    • ::warning:: - Warning message (creates annotation)
    • ::error:: - Error message (creates annotation)

    Grouping Commands:
    • ::group:: - Start collapsible log group
    • ::endgroup:: - End collapsible log group

    Security Commands:
    • ::add-mask:: - Mask a value in logs
    • ::stop-commands:: - Stop processing commands

    Environment Files:
    • $GITHUB_ENV - Set environment variables
    • $GITHUB_OUTPUT - Set step outputs
    • $GITHUB_PATH - Add to system PATH
    • $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY - Add job summary (Markdown)

    Django JobPost Examples

    1. Basic workflow command usage in JobPost CI

    name: JobPost CI

on: [push]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Run tests with workflow commands
        run: |
          echo "::notice::Starting JobPost test suite"
          python manage.py test
          echo "::notice::JobPost tests completed successfully"
    ✅ Uses workflow commands to add notices to JobPost CI logs

    2. JobPost deployment with multiple commands

    - name: Deploy JobPost with commands
  run: |
    echo "::group::Pre-deployment checks"
    python manage.py check --deploy
    echo "::endgroup::"
    
    echo "::notice title=JobPost Deploy::Starting production deployment"
    
    if python manage.py migrate; then
      echo "::notice::JobPost database migrated successfully"
    else
      echo "::error::JobPost migration failed"
      exit 1
    fi
    🚀 Multiple workflow commands in JobPost deployment

    3. JobPost error handling with commands

    - name: JobPost quality checks
  run: |
    echo "::group::Running JobPost linting"
    if ! flake8 jobpost/; then
      echo "::warning file=jobpost,title=Linting::Code style issues found"
    fi
    echo "::endgroup::"
    
    echo "::group::Running JobPost tests"
    if ! python manage.py test; then
      echo "::error title=Test Failure::JobPost tests failed"
      exit 1
    fi
    echo "::endgroup::"
    🔍 Error handling in JobPost CI with workflow commands

    4. JobPost build information logging

    - name: JobPost build info
  run: |
    echo "::notice title=Build Info::Building JobPost version 2.0.0"
    echo "::debug::Commit SHA: $GITHUB_SHA"
    echo "::debug::Branch: $GITHUB_REF_NAME"
    echo "::debug::Runner: $RUNNER_OS"
    
    echo "JOBPOST_BUILD_ID=$(date +%s)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    echo "::notice::JobPost build ID set"
    📋 Logging JobPost build metadata with commands

    5. JobPost multi-stage workflow with commands

    - name: JobPost complete workflow
  run: |
    echo "::group::JobPost Setup"
    echo "::notice::Starting JobPost workflow"
    pip install -r requirements.txt
    echo "::endgroup::"
    
    echo "::group::JobPost Tests"
    if python manage.py test; then
      echo "::notice title=Tests Passed::All JobPost tests successful"
    else
      echo "::error title=Tests Failed::JobPost test suite failed"
      exit 1
    fi
    echo "::endgroup::"
    
    echo "::notice title=Complete::JobPost workflow finished successfully"
    🎯 Complete JobPost workflow using multiple commands

    Using workflow commands to access toolkit functions

    Docs: Toolkit functions | Toolkit: actions/toolkit

    The actions/toolkit includes a number of functions that can be executed as workflow commands.

    Full description

    The actions/toolkit includes a number of functions that can be executed as workflow commands. Use the :: syntax to run the workflow commands within your YAML file; these commands are then sent to the runner over stdout.

    For example, instead of using code to create an error annotation, as below:

    core.error('Missing semicolon', {file: 'app.js', startLine: 1})

    You can use the error command in your workflow to create the same error annotation:

    - name: Create annotation for build error
  run: echo "::error file=app.js,line=1::Missing semicolon"

    Toolkit function mapping

    The following table shows which toolkit functions are available within a workflow:

    Toolkit function Equivalent workflow command
    core.addPath Accessible using environment file GITHUB_PATH
    core.debug debug
    core.notice notice
    core.error error
    core.endGroup endgroup
    core.exportVariable Accessible using environment file GITHUB_ENV
    core.getInput Accessible using environment variable INPUT_{NAME}
    core.getState Accessible using environment variable STATE_{NAME}
    core.isDebug Accessible using environment variable RUNNER_DEBUG
    core.summary Accessible using environment file GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
    core.saveState Accessible using environment file GITHUB_STATE
    core.setCommandEcho echo
    core.setFailed Used as a shortcut for ::error and exit 1
    core.setOutput Accessible using environment file GITHUB_OUTPUT
    core.setSecret add-mask
    core.startGroup group
    core.warning warning

    Django JobPost Examples

    1. JobPost deployment with toolkit commands

    name: JobPost Deploy

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Deploy JobPost with toolkit commands
        run: |
          echo "::group::JobPost Deployment Preparation"
          echo "Setting up deployment environment..."
          echo "deploy_time=$(date +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "::endgroup::"
          
          echo "::group::JobPost Database Migration"
          python manage.py migrate
          echo "::endgroup::"
          
          echo "::notice::JobPost deployed successfully at $(date)"
    🚀 Uses toolkit commands to organize JobPost deployment logs

    2. JobPost test suite with error handling

    - name: Run JobPost tests with error handling
  run: |
    echo "::group::JobPost Unit Tests"
    if ! python manage.py test jobpost.tests.test_models; then
      echo "::error file=jobpost/models.py,line=1::JobPost model tests failed"
      exit 1
    fi
    echo "::endgroup::"
    
    echo "::group::JobPost Integration Tests"
    if ! python manage.py test jobpost.tests.test_views; then
      echo "::error file=jobpost/views.py,line=1::JobPost view tests failed"
      exit 1
    fi
    echo "::endgroup::"
    🧪 Structured test execution with error annotations for JobPost

    3. JobPost CI with comprehensive toolkit usage

    - name: JobPost CI with toolkit
  id: ci
  run: |
    # Mask sensitive data
    API_KEY="${{ secrets.JOBPOST_API_KEY }}"
    echo "::add-mask::$API_KEY"
    
    # Group related operations
    echo "::group::Installing JobPost dependencies"
    pip install -r requirements.txt
    echo "::endgroup::"
    
    # Debug information
    echo "::debug::Python version: $(python --version)"
    echo "::debug::Pip packages: $(pip list | wc -l)"
    
    # Set outputs for other steps
    echo "test_count=42" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    echo "coverage=87" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    
    # Final notice
    echo "::notice title=JobPost CI::All checks passed"
    ✨ Comprehensive JobPost CI using toolkit commands

    4. JobPost state management

    - name: JobPost pre-deployment
  run: |
    # Save state for post-deployment cleanup
    DEPLOY_ID=$(date +%s)
    echo "::save-state name=deploy_id::$DEPLOY_ID"
    echo "::save-state name=previous_version::$(cat VERSION)"
    
    # Add custom tools to PATH
    echo "${{ github.workspace }}/jobpost/bin" >> $GITHUB_PATH
    
    echo "::notice::JobPost deployment state saved"
    💾 Save JobPost state using toolkit commands

    5. JobPost advanced toolkit integration

    - name: JobPost advanced CI
  run: |
    # Mask sensitive values
    echo "::add-mask::${{ secrets.JOBPOST_SECRET }}"
    
    # Group operations
    echo "::group::JobPost Validation"
    python manage.py check
    echo "::endgroup::"
    
    # Set outputs
    echo "version=$(python -c 'import jobpost; print(jobpost.__version__)')" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    
    # Add notice
    echo "::notice title=CI Complete::JobPost validation and versioning complete"
    🚀 Advanced JobPost CI with multiple toolkit functions

    Setting a debug message

    Docs: Debug messages

    Print debug messages to the log. Enable by setting the secret ACTIONS_STEP_DEBUG to true.

    Full description

    Debug messages are only visible when debug logging is enabled. To enable, set the ACTIONS_STEP_DEBUG repository secret to true. Debug messages help troubleshoot workflows without cluttering regular logs. They're perfect for logging detailed information about variable values, system state, or intermediate computation results that you only need when investigating issues.

    Syntax

    ::debug::{message}

    Parameters description

    message: The debug message to log (required)
    Visibility: Only shown when ACTIONS_STEP_DEBUG secret is set to true
    Use case: Detailed logging for troubleshooting without affecting normal logs

    Return value

    Debug messages appear in workflow logs with a purple/debug icon when debug logging is enabled. They don't affect workflow execution.

    Examples

    1. JobPost configuration debugging

    - name: Debug JobPost configuration
  run: |
    echo "::debug::JobPost DEBUG setting: ${{ secrets.DJANGO_DEBUG }}"
    echo "::debug::Database host: ${{ secrets.DB_HOST }}"
    echo "::debug::JobPost SECRET_KEY length: ${#SECRET_KEY}"
    echo "::debug::Python version: $(python --version)"
    echo "::debug::JobPost app installed: $(pip list | grep -i jobpost || echo 'Not found')"
    🔍 Debug JobPost configuration without exposing secrets

    2. JobPost database connection debugging

    - name: Debug JobPost database
  run: |
    echo "::debug::Checking JobPost database connection"
    echo "::debug::Running Django check command"
    python manage.py check --database default
    echo "::debug::JobPost models registered: $(python manage.py showmigrations --list | wc -l)"
    echo "::debug::Pending migrations: $(python manage.py showmigrations --plan | grep '\[ \]' | wc -l)"
    🔍 Debug JobPost database state and migrations

    3. JobPost API endpoint debugging

    - name: Debug JobPost API
  run: |
    echo "::debug::Testing JobPost API endpoints"
    echo "::debug::Starting Django development server"
    python manage.py runserver &
    SERVER_PID=$!
    sleep 5
    
    echo "::debug::Testing /api/jobs/ endpoint"
    RESPONSE=$(curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/jobs/)
    echo "::debug::Response: $RESPONSE"
    
    kill $SERVER_PID
    echo "::debug::JobPost API debug complete"
    🔍 Debug JobPost API responses during CI

    4. JobPost deployment variable debugging

    - name: Debug JobPost deployment
  run: |
    echo "::debug::Deployment variables:"
    echo "::debug::GITHUB_REF=$GITHUB_REF"
    echo "::debug::GITHUB_SHA=$GITHUB_SHA"
    echo "::debug::RUNNER_OS=$RUNNER_OS"
    echo "::debug::Workspace: $GITHUB_WORKSPACE"
    echo "::debug::JobPost files: $(ls -la $GITHUB_WORKSPACE/jobpost/)"
    echo "::debug::Python path: $(which python)"
    echo "::debug::Pip version: $(pip --version)"
    🔍 Debug JobPost deployment environment

    5. JobPost workflow execution debugging

    - name: Debug JobPost workflow execution
  run: |
    echo "::debug::Workflow: ${{ github.workflow }}"
    echo "::debug::Run ID: ${{ github.run_id }}"
    echo "::debug::Run number: ${{ github.run_number }}"
    echo "::debug::Actor: ${{ github.actor }}"
    echo "::debug::Event: ${{ github.event_name }}"
    echo "::debug::JobPost branch: ${{ github.ref_name }}"
    echo "::debug::Commit message: ${{ github.event.head_commit.message }}"
    echo "::debug::Environment variables: $(env | grep JOBPOST || echo 'None')"
    🔍 Debug complete JobPost workflow context

    Setting a notice message

    Docs: Notice messages

    Create a notice message that appears in the workflow log as an annotation.

    Full description

    Notice messages create informational annotations in your workflow logs. They're displayed prominently in the Actions UI with a blue icon, making important milestones and status updates stand out. Unlike debug messages, notices are always visible regardless of log settings. You can optionally specify file path, line number, column, and title to make notices more contextual and actionable.

    Syntax

    ::notice file={name},line={line},col={col},title={title}::{message}

    Parameters description

    file: Filename to associate with the notice (optional)
    line: Line number in the file (optional)
    col: Column number in the file (optional)
    title: Custom title for the notice (optional)
    message: The notice message content (required)
    Visibility: Always visible in logs and annotations tab

    Return value

    Notice annotations appear in workflow logs with a blue icon and in the workflow's Annotations tab, optionally linked to specific files and lines.

    Examples

    1. JobPost deployment success notice

    - name: Deploy JobPost
  run: |
    python manage.py migrate
    python manage.py collectstatic --noinput
    echo "::notice title=JobPost Deployed::Successfully deployed JobPost to production at $(date)"
    📢 Creates a prominent notice for JobPost deployment success

    2. JobPost test coverage notice

    - name: Run JobPost tests with coverage
  run: |
    coverage run --source='jobpost' manage.py test
    COVERAGE=$(coverage report | grep TOTAL | awk '{print $4}')
    echo "::notice title=Test Coverage::JobPost test coverage is $COVERAGE"
    
    if [ "${COVERAGE%\%}" -lt 80 ]; then
      echo "::notice file=jobpost/tests.py,title=Coverage Warning::JobPost coverage below 80%"
    fi
    📊 Display JobPost test coverage with conditional notices

    3. JobPost migration status notice

    - name: Check JobPost migrations
  run: |
    PENDING=$(python manage.py showmigrations --plan | grep '\[ \]' | wc -l)
    
    if [ $PENDING -eq 0 ]; then
      echo "::notice title=Migrations::JobPost database is up to date"
    else
      echo "::notice file=jobpost/migrations,title=Migrations::JobPost has $PENDING pending migrations"
      python manage.py migrate
      echo "::notice title=Migrations Applied::JobPost migrations completed successfully"
    fi
    � Track JobPost migration status with notices

    4. JobPost security scan notice

    - name: JobPost security check
  run: |
    echo "::notice title=Security Scan::Starting JobPost security audit"
    
    pip install safety
    safety check --json > safety-report.json || true
    
    VULNERABILITIES=$(cat safety-report.json | jq length)
    
    if [ $VULNERABILITIES -eq 0 ]; then
      echo "::notice title=Security::No vulnerabilities found in JobPost dependencies"
    else
      echo "::notice file=requirements.txt,title=Security::Found $VULNERABILITIES vulnerabilities in JobPost dependencies"
    fi
    🔒 Report JobPost security scan results as notices

    5. JobPost performance metrics notice

    - name: JobPost performance analysis
  run: |
    START=$(date +%s)
    python manage.py test jobpost
    END=$(date +%s)
    DURATION=$((END - START))
    
    echo "::notice title=Performance::JobPost tests completed in ${DURATION}s"
    
    # Check coverage
    coverage run manage.py test jobpost
    COVERAGE=$(coverage report | grep TOTAL | awk '{print $4}')
    echo "::notice title=Coverage::JobPost test coverage: $COVERAGE"
    
    # Report lines of code
    LOC=$(find jobpost -name '*.py' -exec wc -l {} + | tail -1 | awk '{print $1}')
    echo "::notice title=Code Size::JobPost has $LOC lines of Python code"
    📊 Comprehensive JobPost metrics in notices

    Setting a warning message

    Docs: Warning messages

    Create a warning message that appears in the workflow log as a yellow annotation.

    Full description

    Warning messages create yellow-highlighted annotations in your workflow logs to flag potential issues that don't stop execution but should be addressed. They're visible in both the workflow log and the Annotations tab. Warnings are ideal for deprecation notices, non-critical test failures, code quality issues, or configuration problems that might cause issues later. Like notices, warnings support optional file, line, column, and title parameters for better context.

    Syntax

    ::warning file={name},line={line},col={col},title={title}::{message}

    Parameters description

    file: Filename to associate with the warning (optional)
    line: Line number in the file (optional)
    col: Column number in the file (optional)
    title: Custom title for the warning (optional)
    message: The warning message content (required)
    Visibility: Always visible with yellow icon in logs and annotations

    Return value

    Warning annotations appear in logs with a yellow icon and in the Annotations tab. Warnings don't fail the workflow but are prominently displayed.

    Examples

    1. JobPost code quality warnings

    - name: JobPost linting
  run: |
    echo "::notice::Running flake8 on JobPost"
    
    if ! flake8 jobpost/ --max-line-length=120 > flake8.txt; then
      WARNINGS=$(cat flake8.txt | wc -l)
      echo "::warning file=jobpost,title=Code Quality::Found $WARNINGS style issues in JobPost"
      
      while IFS= read -r line; do
        echo "::warning::$line"
      done < flake8.txt
    fi
    ⚠️ Report JobPost code quality issues as warnings

    2. JobPost deprecated API warnings

    - name: Check JobPost for deprecated APIs
  run: |
    if grep -r "django.conf.urls.url" jobpost/; then
      echo "::warning file=jobpost/urls.py,line=1,title=Deprecated API::JobPost uses deprecated django.conf.urls.url - migrate to django.urls.path"
    fi
    
    if grep -r "render_to_response" jobpost/views.py; then
      echo "::warning file=jobpost/views.py,title=Deprecated::render_to_response is deprecated - use render() instead"
    fi
    
    if grep -r "ugettext" jobpost/; then
      echo "::warning file=jobpost,title=Deprecated::ugettext is deprecated - use gettext instead"
    fi
    ⚠️ Warn about deprecated Django APIs in JobPost

    3. JobPost test performance warnings

    - name: JobPost performance tests
  run: |
    START_TIME=$(date +%s)
    python manage.py test jobpost
    END_TIME=$(date +%s)
    DURATION=$((END_TIME - START_TIME))
    
    if [ $DURATION -gt 300 ]; then
      echo "::warning title=Performance::JobPost tests took ${DURATION}s (over 5 minutes)"
    fi
    
    if [ $DURATION -gt 600 ]; then
      echo "::warning title=Performance Critical::JobPost tests took ${DURATION}s (over 10 minutes) - consider optimization"
    fi
    ⚠️ Warn about slow JobPost test execution

    4. JobPost configuration warnings

    - name: Validate JobPost configuration
  run: |
    if grep "DEBUG = True" anihpj/settings.py; then
      echo "::warning file=anihpj/settings.py,title=Config::DEBUG=True in JobPost settings - should be False for production"
    fi
    
    if ! grep "ALLOWED_HOSTS" anihpj/settings.py | grep -v "\[\]"; then
      echo "::warning file=anihpj/settings.py,title=Config::ALLOWED_HOSTS is empty in JobPost settings"
    fi
    
    if ! grep "SECURE_SSL_REDIRECT = True" anihpj/settings.py; then
      echo "::warning file=anihpj/settings.py,title=Security::SECURE_SSL_REDIRECT not enabled for JobPost"
    fi
    ⚠️ Validate JobPost Django settings with warnings

    5. JobPost dependency and compatibility warnings

    - name: Check JobPost dependencies
  run: |
    # Check for outdated packages
    pip list --outdated > outdated.txt
    if [ -s outdated.txt ]; then
      COUNT=$(cat outdated.txt | wc -l)
      echo "::warning file=requirements.txt,title=Outdated::JobPost has $COUNT outdated dependencies"
    fi
    
    # Check Python version compatibility
    PYTHON_VERSION=$(python --version | cut -d' ' -f2)
    if [[ "$PYTHON_VERSION" < "3.9" ]]; then
      echo "::warning title=Python Version::JobPost running on Python $PYTHON_VERSION - recommend 3.9+"
    fi
    
    # Check for missing migrations
    if python manage.py makemigrations --dry-run --check 2>&1 | grep -q "Migrations"; then
      echo "::warning file=jobpost/models.py,title=Migrations::JobPost has unapplied model changes"
    fi
    ⚠️ Comprehensive JobPost dependency and compatibility warnings

    Setting an error message

    Docs: Error messages

    Create an error message that appears in the workflow log as a red annotation.

    Full description

    Error messages create red-highlighted annotations in your workflow logs to indicate critical failures. They're displayed prominently with a red icon in both the workflow log and the Annotations tab. Error messages are typically used to report test failures, build errors, or critical validation failures. While error messages don't automatically fail the workflow (you need to explicitly exit with a non-zero code), they mark the workflow run with a visible failure indicator, making it easy to identify what went wrong.

    Syntax

    ::error file={name},line={line},col={col},title={title}::{message}

    Parameters description

    file: Filename to associate with the error (optional)
    line: Line number in the file (optional)
    col: Column number in the file (optional)
    title: Custom title for the error (optional)
    message: The error message content (required)
    Note: Error command doesn't stop execution - use exit 1 to fail the workflow

    Return value

    Error annotations appear in logs with a red icon and in the Annotations tab. The workflow continues unless explicitly stopped with exit code.

    Examples

    1. JobPost test failure errors

    - name: Run JobPost tests
  run: |
    if ! python manage.py test jobpost.tests.test_models; then
      echo "::error file=jobpost/tests/test_models.py,title=Test Failure::JobPost model tests failed"
      exit 1
    fi
    
    if ! python manage.py test jobpost.tests.test_views; then
      echo "::error file=jobpost/tests/test_views.py,title=Test Failure::JobPost view tests failed"
      exit 1
    fi
    
    if ! python manage.py test jobpost.tests.test_api; then
      echo "::error file=jobpost/tests/test_api.py,title=Test Failure::JobPost API tests failed"
      exit 1
    fi
    ❌ Report JobPost test failures as errors

    2. JobPost migration errors

    - name: Run JobPost migrations
  run: |
    echo "::notice::Checking JobPost database migrations"
    
    if ! python manage.py makemigrations --check --dry-run; then
      echo "::error file=jobpost/models.py,title=Migration Error::JobPost has unmade migrations"
      exit 1
    fi
    
    if ! python manage.py migrate --noinput; then
      echo "::error title=Migration Failed::JobPost database migration failed"
      python manage.py showmigrations
      exit 1
    fi
    ❌ Handle JobPost migration errors with annotations

    3. JobPost security audit errors

    - name: JobPost security audit
  run: |
    pip install bandit
    
    if ! bandit -r jobpost/ -ll > bandit-report.txt; then
      echo "::error file=jobpost,title=Security::Critical security issues found in JobPost"
      
      while IFS= read -r line; do
        if [[ $line == *"HIGH"* ]] || [[ $line == *"CRITICAL"* ]]; then
          echo "::error::$line"
        fi
      done < bandit-report.txt
      
      exit 1
    fi
    ❌ Report critical JobPost security issues as errors

    4. JobPost deployment validation errors

    - name: Validate JobPost deployment
  run: |
    ERROR_COUNT=0
    
    if ! python manage.py check --deploy; then
      echo "::error file=anihpj/settings.py,title=Deployment Check::JobPost deployment checks failed"
      ERROR_COUNT=$((ERROR_COUNT + 1))
    fi
    
    if [ ! -f "jobpost/static/css/style.css" ]; then
      echo "::error file=jobpost/static,title=Missing File::JobPost CSS file not found"
      ERROR_COUNT=$((ERROR_COUNT + 1))
    fi
    
    if ! grep "SECURE_HSTS_SECONDS" anihpj/settings.py; then
      echo "::error file=anihpj/settings.py,line=1,title=Security::HSTS not configured for JobPost"
      ERROR_COUNT=$((ERROR_COUNT + 1))
    fi
    
    if [ $ERROR_COUNT -gt 0 ]; then
      echo "::error title=Validation Failed::JobPost failed $ERROR_COUNT deployment validation checks"
      exit 1
    fi
    ❌ Comprehensive JobPost deployment validation with error tracking

    5. JobPost build and integration errors

    - name: Build and test JobPost
  run: |
    # Check static files
    if ! python manage.py collectstatic --dry-run --noinput; then
      echo "::error file=anihpj/settings.py,title=Static Files::JobPost static file collection failed"
      exit 1
    fi
    
    # Check templates
    if ! python manage.py check --tag templates; then
      echo "::error file=jobpost/templates,title=Template Error::JobPost template validation failed"
      exit 1
    fi
    
    # Run integration tests
    if ! python manage.py test jobpost.tests.integration --failfast; then
      echo "::error file=jobpost/tests/integration,title=Integration Tests::JobPost integration tests failed"
      python manage.py test jobpost.tests.integration --verbosity=2
      exit 1
    fi
    
    echo "::notice::All JobPost build and integration checks passed"
    ❌ JobPost build validation with detailed error reporting

    Grouping log lines

    Docs: Log grouping

    Create expandable groups in workflow logs to organize output and improve readability.

    Full description

    Log grouping creates collapsible sections in your workflow logs, making long outputs more organized and easier to navigate. Groups are collapsed by default in the Actions UI, allowing users to expand only the sections they need to inspect. This is especially useful for verbose operations like dependency installation, extensive test suites, or deployment sequences for your JobPost application. Groups improve log readability and help identify specific stages of complex workflows.

    Syntax

    ::group::{title}
# Log lines to group
::endgroup::

    Parameters description

    title: The group name displayed in logs (required)
    Collapsible: Groups are collapsed by default in the UI
    Nesting: Groups can contain other groups for hierarchical organization
    Best practice: Use groups for verbose operations to keep logs clean

    Return value

    Creates a collapsible section in workflow logs. All output between ::group:: and ::endgroup:: appears within the expandable section.

    Examples

    1. JobPost dependency installation

    - name: Install JobPost dependencies
  run: |
    echo "::group::Installing Python packages"
    pip install -r requirements.txt
    echo "::endgroup::"
    
    echo "::group::Installing system dependencies"
    sudo apt-get update
    sudo apt-get install -y postgresql-client
    echo "::endgroup::"
    📦 Grouped JobPost dependency installation logs

    2. JobPost test execution

    - name: Run JobPost test suite
  run: |
    echo "::group::JobPost Model Tests"
    python manage.py test jobpost.tests.test_models --verbosity=2
    echo "::endgroup::"
    
    echo "::group::JobPost View Tests"
    python manage.py test jobpost.tests.test_views --verbosity=2
    echo "::endgroup::"
    
    echo "::group::JobPost API Tests"
    python manage.py test jobpost.tests.test_api --verbosity=2
    echo "::endgroup::"
    🧪 Organized JobPost test output with groups

    3. JobPost deployment steps

    - name: Deploy JobPost
  run: |
    echo "::group::Pre-deployment checks"
    python manage.py check --deploy
    echo "::endgroup::"
    
    echo "::group::Database migration"
    python manage.py migrate --noinput
    echo "::endgroup::"
    
    echo "::group::Static files collection"
    python manage.py collectstatic --noinput
    echo "::endgroup::"
    
    echo "::group::Cache warming"
    python manage.py warm_cache
    echo "::endgroup::"
    🚀 Structured JobPost deployment with log groups

    4. JobPost build process with nested groups

    - name: Build JobPost
  run: |
    echo "::group::JobPost Build Process"
    
    echo "::group::Frontend Build"
    npm install
    npm run build
    echo "::endgroup::"
    
    echo "::group::Backend Build"
    pip install -e .
    python setup.py build
    echo "::endgroup::"
    
    echo "::group::Docker Image Build"
    docker build -t jobpost:latest .
    echo "::endgroup::"
    
    echo "::endgroup::"
    🏗️ Hierarchical JobPost build process with nested groups

    5. JobPost complete CI/CD pipeline with groups

    - name: JobPost full pipeline
  run: |
    echo "::group::Environment Setup"
    python -m venv venv
    source venv/bin/activate
    pip install -r requirements.txt
    echo "::endgroup::"
    
    echo "::group::Code Quality"
    flake8 jobpost/
    black --check jobpost/
    mypy jobpost/
    echo "::endgroup::"
    
    echo "::group::Security Scan"
    bandit -r jobpost/
    safety check
    echo "::endgroup::"
    
    echo "::group::Testing"
    pytest jobpost/tests/ --cov=jobpost
    echo "::endgroup::"
    
    echo "::group::Build & Package"
    python setup.py sdist bdist_wheel
    echo "::endgroup::"
    🎯 Complete JobPost CI/CD pipeline organized with groups

    Masking a value in a log

    Docs: Value masking

    Mask sensitive values in logs by replacing them with asterisks (***) to prevent exposure.

    Full description

    Masking prevents sensitive information from appearing in workflow logs. Once a value is masked, all subsequent occurrences in the log output will be replaced with ***. This is essential for protecting secrets, API keys, passwords, tokens, and other confidential data in your JobPost workflows. Masking should be applied before the sensitive value is used in commands to ensure it never appears in plain text. Note that masking is case-sensitive and must match the exact value.

    Syntax

    ::add-mask::{value}

    Parameters description

    value: The sensitive value to mask (required)
    Case-sensitive: Masking matches exact values only
    Scope: Applies to all subsequent log output in the workflow
    Best practice: Mask values immediately after retrieving from secrets

    Return value

    No visible output. The specified value will appear as *** in all subsequent log output.

    Examples

    1. Masking JobPost API keys

    - name: Setup JobPost API
  run: |
    API_KEY="${{ secrets.JOBPOST_API_KEY }}"
    echo "::add-mask::$API_KEY"
    
    echo "JobPost API Key configured: $API_KEY"
    # API key will appear as *** in logs
    
    curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $API_KEY" https://api.jobpost.com/deploy
    🔒 Mask JobPost API key in logs

    2. Masking JobPost database credentials

    - name: Configure JobPost database
  run: |
    DB_PASSWORD="${{ secrets.DB_PASSWORD }}"
    echo "::add-mask::$DB_PASSWORD"
    
    DB_USER="${{ secrets.DB_USER }}"
    echo "::add-mask::$DB_USER"
    
    # Both credentials will be masked
    echo "JobPost database connection: postgresql://$DB_USER:$DB_PASSWORD@localhost/jobpost"
    
    # Safe to use in connection string
    export DATABASE_URL="postgresql://$DB_USER:$DB_PASSWORD@db.jobpost.com/jobpost"
    🔒 Mask JobPost database credentials

    3. Masking dynamic JobPost secrets

    - name: Generate JobPost tokens
  run: |
    # Generate temporary token
    TOKEN=$(openssl rand -hex 32)
    echo "::add-mask::$TOKEN"
    
    # Generate deployment key
    DEPLOY_KEY=$(python -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_urlsafe(32))")
    echo "::add-mask::$DEPLOY_KEY"
    
    # Use tokens in JobPost API call
    curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
         -H "X-Deploy-Key: $DEPLOY_KEY" \
         https://api.jobpost.com/deploy
    
    echo "JobPost deployment token: $TOKEN"
    echo "JobPost deploy key: $DEPLOY_KEY"
    # Both will be masked in output
    🔒 Mask dynamically generated JobPost tokens

    4. Masking JobPost OAuth tokens

    - name: JobPost OAuth flow
