          echo "🧪 Deploying JobPost to Staging"
          echo "Environment: ${{ vars.ENVIRONMENT_NAME }}"
          echo "API URL: ${{ vars.API_URL }}"
          # Staging environment vars override repository vars
          python manage.py deploy --api-url="${{ vars.API_URL }}"
    🌍 JobPost environment-specific deployment using vars context

    2. JobPost feature flags and toggles

    name: JobPost Feature Flags

on: [push]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: JobPost feature-controlled build
        run: |
          echo "🎛️ JobPost Feature Flags"
          echo "Enable Cache: ${{ vars.ENABLE_CACHE }}"
          echo "Enable Tests: ${{ vars.ENABLE_TESTS }}"
          echo "Enable Linting: ${{ vars.ENABLE_LINTING }}"
          echo "Enable Security Scan: ${{ vars.ENABLE_SECURITY_SCAN }}"
      
      - name: Cache JobPost dependencies
        if: vars.ENABLE_CACHE == 'true'
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/pip
          key: jobpost-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt') }}
      
      - name: Run JobPost tests
        if: vars.ENABLE_TESTS == 'true'
        run: |
          echo "🧪 Running JobPost tests"
          python manage.py test jobpost
      
      - name: Lint JobPost code
        if: vars.ENABLE_LINTING == 'true'
        run: |
          echo "✨ Linting JobPost code"
          flake8 jobpost/ anihpj/
      
      - name: Security scan JobPost
        if: vars.ENABLE_SECURITY_SCAN == 'true'
        run: |
          echo "🔒 Security scanning JobPost"
          bandit -r jobpost/ anihpj/
      
      - name: JobPost build summary
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost Build Configuration"
          echo "Features enabled:"
          [ "${{ vars.ENABLE_CACHE }}" == "true" ] && echo "  ✓ Caching"
          [ "${{ vars.ENABLE_TESTS }}" == "true" ] && echo "  ✓ Testing"
          [ "${{ vars.ENABLE_LINTING }}" == "true" ] && echo "  ✓ Linting"
          [ "${{ vars.ENABLE_SECURITY_SCAN }}" == "true" ] && echo "  ✓ Security"
    🎛️ JobPost feature flags using vars context

    3. JobPost variable precedence demonstration

    name: JobPost Vars Precedence

on: [push]

jobs:
  show-precedence:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Display JobPost organization vars
        run: |
          echo "🏢 Organization-level Variables"
          # These are set at organization level
          echo "Org Name: ${{ vars.ORG_NAME }}"
          echo "Support Email: ${{ vars.SUPPORT_EMAIL }}"
          echo "Default Region: ${{ vars.DEFAULT_REGION }}"
      
      - name: Display JobPost repository vars
        run: |
          echo "📦 Repository-level Variables"
          # These override organization vars
          echo "App Name: ${{ vars.APP_NAME }}"
          echo "Default Branch: ${{ vars.DEFAULT_BRANCH }}"
          echo "Python Version: ${{ vars.PYTHON_VERSION }}"
          # This overrides org-level DEFAULT_REGION
          echo "Region: ${{ vars.DEFAULT_REGION }}"
  
  show-environment-vars:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: production
    steps:
      - name: Display JobPost environment vars
        run: |
          echo "🌍 Environment-level Variables"
          # These override repository and organization vars
          echo "Deploy URL: ${{ vars.DEPLOY_URL }}"
          echo "Database Tier: ${{ vars.DATABASE_TIER }}"
          # This overrides repository and org DEFAULT_REGION
          echo "Region: ${{ vars.DEFAULT_REGION }}"
          
          echo ""
          echo "📊 Precedence: Environment > Repository > Organization"
    📊 JobPost configuration variable precedence levels

    4. JobPost fallback values with vars

    name: JobPost Vars with Fallbacks

on: [push]

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: JobPost configuration with defaults
        run: |
          # Use vars with fallback values
          API_URL="${{ vars.API_URL || 'http://localhost:8000' }}"
          DATABASE_HOST="${{ vars.DATABASE_HOST || 'localhost' }}"
          WORKERS="${{ vars.WORKERS || '2' }}"
          TIMEOUT="${{ vars.TIMEOUT || '30' }}"
          RETRY_COUNT="${{ vars.RETRY_COUNT || '3' }}"
          
          echo "⚙️ JobPost Configuration"
          echo "API URL: $API_URL"
          echo "Database: $DATABASE_HOST"
          echo "Workers: $WORKERS"
          echo "Timeout: $TIMEOUT seconds"
          echo "Retry Count: $RETRY_COUNT"
      
      - name: Deploy JobPost with configuration
        run: |
          # Build configuration dynamically
          DEPLOY_URL="${{ vars.DEPLOY_URL }}"
          if [ -z "$DEPLOY_URL" ]; then
            # Fallback to constructed URL if var not set
            DEPLOY_URL="https://jobpost-${{ github.ref_name }}.example.com"
          fi
          
          echo "🚀 Deploying JobPost to: $DEPLOY_URL"
          
          python manage.py deploy \
            --url="$DEPLOY_URL" \
            --settings="${{ vars.DJANGO_SETTINGS || 'anihpj.settings.production' }}"
      
      - name: Check JobPost optional features
        run: |
          # Check if optional vars are set
          if [ -n "${{ vars.SLACK_WEBHOOK }}" ]; then
            echo "✓ Slack notifications enabled"
          else
            echo "○ Slack notifications not configured"
          fi
          
          if [ -n "${{ vars.SENTRY_DSN }}" ]; then
            echo "✓ Error tracking enabled"
          else
            echo "○ Error tracking not configured"
          fi
    🔄 JobPost fallback values using vars context

    5. JobPost dynamic environment selection

    name: JobPost Dynamic Environment

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      target_environment:
        description: 'Target environment'
        required: true
        type: choice
        options:
          - development
          - staging
          - production

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    # Dynamically set environment based on input
    environment: ${{ inputs.target_environment }}
    steps:
      - name: Display JobPost environment configuration
        run: |
          echo "🎯 JobPost Deployment Target"
          echo "Environment: ${{ inputs.target_environment }}"
          echo "API URL: ${{ vars.API_URL }}"
          echo "Database: ${{ vars.DATABASE_HOST }}"
          echo "Region: ${{ vars.AWS_REGION }}"
          echo "Workers: ${{ vars.WORKER_COUNT }}"
          
          # Environment-specific vars are loaded based on the environment
      
      - name: Validate JobPost configuration
        run: |
          # Ensure required vars are set for environment
          if [ -z "${{ vars.API_URL }}" ]; then
            echo "❌ API_URL not configured for ${{ inputs.target_environment }}"
            exit 1
          fi
          
          if [ -z "${{ vars.DATABASE_HOST }}" ]; then
            echo "❌ DATABASE_HOST not configured for ${{ inputs.target_environment }}"
            exit 1
          fi
          
          echo "✅ JobPost configuration validated"
      
      - name: Deploy JobPost application
        run: |
          echo "🚀 Deploying JobPost"
          python manage.py deploy \
            --environment="${{ inputs.target_environment }}" \
            --api-url="${{ vars.API_URL }}" \
            --database="${{ vars.DATABASE_HOST }}" \
            --region="${{ vars.AWS_REGION }}" \
            --workers="${{ vars.WORKER_COUNT }}"
      
      - name: Run JobPost smoke tests
        run: |
          # Use environment-specific test endpoint
          TEST_URL="${{ vars.API_URL }}/health"
          echo "🔍 Running smoke tests against: $TEST_URL"
          curl -f "$TEST_URL" || exit 1
          echo "✅ JobPost smoke tests passed"
    🎯 JobPost dynamic environment selection using vars context

    job context

    Docs: job context

    Access information about the currently running job using the job context.

    Full description

    The job context contains information about the currently running job, including its status and the container it's running in. For JobPost workflows, the job context is essential for accessing service containers (like PostgreSQL or Redis), checking job status, and managing job-level configuration. The context provides access to job status, services configuration, and the container network. It's particularly useful when working with containerized services that JobPost's Django application needs to connect to during testing or deployment.

    Syntax

    # Access job context properties
${{ job.status }}                    # Job status (success, failure, cancelled)
${{ job.container.id }}              # Container ID if job runs in container
${{ job.container.network }}         # Container network

# Access service containers
${{ job.services.postgres.id }}      # Service container ID
${{ job.services.postgres.network }} # Service network
${{ job.services.postgres.ports }}   # Exposed ports

    Parameters description

    Job status:
    • job.status - Current status of the job (success, failure, cancelled)
    Container properties (when job runs in a container):
    • job.container.id - ID of the job's container
    • job.container.network - Network of the job's container
    Service containers:
    • job.services.<service_id>.id - Service container ID
    • job.services.<service_id>.network - Service container network
    • job.services.<service_id>.ports - Exposed ports of the service

    Return value

    The job context returns an object with string values for status and container information, and nested objects for service container details.

    Examples

    1. JobPost with PostgreSQL service container

    name: JobPost Database Tests

on: [push]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    services:
      postgres:
        image: postgres:15
        env:
          POSTGRES_DB: jobpost_test
          POSTGRES_USER: jobpost
          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: password
        options: >-
          --health-cmd pg_isready
          --health-interval 10s
          --health-timeout 5s
          --health-retries 5
        ports:
          - 5432:5432
    
    steps:
      - name: Display JobPost service info
        run: |
          echo "🗄️ JobPost PostgreSQL Service"
          echo "Container ID: ${{ job.services.postgres.id }}"
          echo "Network: ${{ job.services.postgres.network }}"
          echo "Ports: ${{ job.services.postgres.ports }}"
      
      - name: Configure JobPost database connection
        run: |
          # Use service container in JobPost settings
          export DATABASE_HOST=localhost
          export DATABASE_PORT=5432
          export DATABASE_NAME=jobpost_test
          export DATABASE_USER=jobpost
          export DATABASE_PASSWORD=password
          
          echo "DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=anihpj.settings.test" >> $GITHUB_ENV
      
      - name: Run JobPost migrations
        run: |
          python manage.py migrate --noinput
          echo "✅ JobPost database migrations completed"
      
      - name: Run JobPost tests
        run: |
          python manage.py test jobpost
          echo "✅ JobPost tests completed"
    🗄️ JobPost with PostgreSQL service container using job context

    2. JobPost with multiple services

    name: JobPost Full Stack Tests

on: [push]

jobs:
  integration-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    services:
      postgres:
        image: postgres:15
        env:
          POSTGRES_DB: jobpost_db
          POSTGRES_USER: jobpost
          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: secret
        ports:
          - 5432:5432
      
      redis:
        image: redis:7
        ports:
          - 6379:6379
        options: >-
          --health-cmd "redis-cli ping"
          --health-interval 10s
          --health-timeout 5s
          --health-retries 5
    
    steps:
      - name: Display JobPost services
        run: |
          echo "🔧 JobPost Service Containers"
          echo "PostgreSQL:"
          echo "  ID: ${{ job.services.postgres.id }}"
          echo "  Network: ${{ job.services.postgres.network }}"
          echo ""
          echo "Redis:"
          echo "  ID: ${{ job.services.redis.id }}"
          echo "  Network: ${{ job.services.redis.network }}"
      
      - name: Configure JobPost for services
        run: |
          cat > .env << EOF
          DATABASE_URL=postgresql://jobpost:secret@localhost:5432/jobpost_db
          REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379/0
          CELERY_BROKER_URL=redis://localhost:6379/1
          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=anihpj.settings
          EOF
          
          echo "✅ JobPost environment configured"
      
      - name: Test JobPost database connection
        run: |
          python manage.py check --database default
          echo "✅ JobPost database connection verified"
      
      - name: Test JobPost cache connection
        run: |
          python manage.py shell -c "from django.core.cache import cache; cache.set('test', 'value'); print('Cache test:', cache.get('test'))"
          echo "✅ JobPost Redis connection verified"
    🔧 JobPost with multiple service containers using job context

    3. JobPost containerized job

    name: JobPost Container Build

on: [push]

jobs:
  build-in-container:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    container:
      image: python:3.11-slim
      env:
        DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings
      volumes:
        - jobpost_data:/data
    
    steps:
      - name: Display JobPost container info
        run: |
          echo "🐳 JobPost Container Information"
          echo "Container ID: ${{ job.container.id }}"
          echo "Network: ${{ job.container.network }}"
          echo "Python Version: $(python --version)"
      
      - name: Setup JobPost in container
        run: |
          apt-get update && apt-get install -y git
          pip install --upgrade pip
          pip install django celery psycopg2-binary redis
          
          echo "✅ JobPost dependencies installed in container"
      
      - name: Build JobPost application
        run: |
          python manage.py collectstatic --noinput
          python manage.py check
          
          echo "✅ JobPost built in container: ${{ job.container.id }}"
    🐳 JobPost running in container using job context

    4. JobPost job status monitoring

    name: JobPost Status Monitoring

on: [push]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Start JobPost build
        run: |
          echo "🚀 Starting JobPost build"
          echo "Initial status: ${{ job.status }}"
      
      - name: Build JobPost
        id: build
        run: |
          python manage.py collectstatic --noinput
          echo "build_complete=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Report build status
        if: always()
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost Build Status"
          echo "Job Status: ${{ job.status }}"
          
          if [ "${{ job.status }}" == "success" ]; then
            echo "✅ JobPost build succeeded"
          elif [ "${{ job.status }}" == "failure" ]; then
            echo "❌ JobPost build failed"
          elif [ "${{ job.status }}" == "cancelled" ]; then
            echo "⚠️ JobPost build cancelled"
          fi
  
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build
    steps:
      - name: Check previous job
        run: |
          echo "🔍 Checking JobPost build job"
          echo "Current job status: ${{ job.status }}"
      
      - name: Run JobPost tests
        run: |
          python manage.py test jobpost
      
      - name: Final status
        if: always()
        run: |
          echo "📋 JobPost Test Status: ${{ job.status }}"
    📊 JobPost job status monitoring using job context

    5. JobPost service health checks

    name: JobPost Service Health

on: [push]

jobs:
  test-with-services:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    services:
      postgres:
        image: postgres:15
        env:
          POSTGRES_DB: jobpost_db
          POSTGRES_USER: jobpost
          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: secret
        options: --health-cmd pg_isready --health-interval 10s
        ports:
          - 5432:5432
      
      redis:
        image: redis:7
        options: --health-cmd "redis-cli ping" --health-interval 10s
        ports:
          - 6379:6379
    
    steps:
      - name: Verify JobPost services are running
        run: |
          echo "🏥 JobPost Service Health Check"
          echo "PostgreSQL Container: ${{ job.services.postgres.id }}"
          echo "Redis Container: ${{ job.services.redis.id }}"
          
          # Test PostgreSQL
          PGPASSWORD=secret psql -h localhost -U jobpost -d jobpost_db -c "SELECT 1;"
          echo "✅ PostgreSQL ready"
          
          # Test Redis
          redis-cli -h localhost ping
          echo "✅ Redis ready"
      
      - name: Run JobPost with services
        run: |
          echo "🚀 Running JobPost with verified services"
          echo "Database: PostgreSQL (${{ job.services.postgres.id }})"
          echo "Cache: Redis (${{ job.services.redis.id }})"
          
          python manage.py migrate
          python manage.py test jobpost
          
          echo "✅ JobPost integration tests passed"
      
      - name: Service diagnostics
        if: failure()
        run: |
          echo "🔍 JobPost Service Diagnostics"
          echo "PostgreSQL logs:"
          docker logs ${{ job.services.postgres.id }}
          echo ""
          echo "Redis logs:"
          docker logs ${{ job.services.redis.id }}
    🏥 JobPost service health checks using job context

    jobs context

    Docs: jobs context

    Access outputs from jobs in reusable workflows using the jobs context.

    Full description

    The jobs context is only available in reusable workflows and contains the outputs of jobs from the reusable workflow. This context is used when you call a reusable workflow and need to access the outputs it produces. For JobPost workflows, this is particularly useful when creating modular, reusable workflow components for common tasks like building, testing, or deploying the application. The jobs context allows the calling workflow to access outputs from all jobs in the reusable workflow, enabling sophisticated workflow composition and data passing between workflows.

    Syntax

    # In the calling workflow
jobs:
  call-reusable:
    uses: ./.github/workflows/reusable-build.yml
  
  use-outputs:
    needs: call-reusable
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo "${{ jobs.call-reusable.outputs.version }}"

# In the reusable workflow
jobs:
  build:
    outputs:
      version: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}
    steps:
      - id: version
        run: echo "version=1.0.0" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

    Parameters description

    Jobs context structure:
    • jobs.<job_id>.outputs - Outputs from a job in a reusable workflow
    • jobs.<job_id>.outputs.<output_name> - Specific output value
    • jobs.<job_id>.result - Result of the job (success, failure, cancelled, skipped)
    Availability:
    • Only available in the calling workflow when using reusable workflows
    • Not available within the reusable workflow itself
    • Requires the reusable workflow to define outputs at the workflow level

    Return value

    The jobs context returns an object containing all outputs from jobs in the reusable workflow, with string values for each output.

    Examples

    1. JobPost reusable build workflow

    # .github/workflows/jobpost-build-reusable.yml
name: JobPost Reusable Build

on:
  workflow_call:
    outputs:
      version:
        description: "JobPost build version"
        value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.version }}
      artifact_name:
        description: "JobPost artifact name"
        value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.artifact_name }}
      build_time:
        description: "JobPost build timestamp"
        value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.build_time }}

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      version: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}
      artifact_name: ${{ steps.artifact.outputs.name }}
      build_time: ${{ steps.time.outputs.timestamp }}
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Generate JobPost version
        id: version
        run: |
          VERSION="v1.0.${{ github.run_number }}"
          echo "version=$VERSION" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "JobPost version: $VERSION"
      
      - name: Build JobPost
        run: |
          python manage.py collectstatic --noinput
          python manage.py check
      
      - name: Create JobPost artifact
        id: artifact
        run: |
          ARTIFACT="jobpost-${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}.tar.gz"
          tar -czf "$ARTIFACT" jobpost/ anihpj/ manage.py
          echo "name=$ARTIFACT" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Record build time
        id: time
        run: |
          TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
          echo "timestamp=$TIMESTAMP" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

---
# .github/workflows/jobpost-main.yml
name: JobPost Main Workflow

on: [push]

jobs:
  build:
    uses: ./.github/workflows/jobpost-build-reusable.yml
  
  deploy:
    needs: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Use JobPost build outputs
        run: |
          echo "🚀 Deploying JobPost"
          echo "Version: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.version }}"
          echo "Artifact: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.artifact_name }}"
          echo "Built at: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.build_time }}"
    📦 JobPost reusable workflow with outputs using jobs context

    2. JobPost test matrix reusable workflow

    # .github/workflows/jobpost-test-reusable.yml
name: JobPost Reusable Tests

on:
  workflow_call:
    outputs:
      test_results:
        description: "JobPost test results summary"
        value: ${{ jobs.test.outputs.results }}
      coverage:
        description: "JobPost test coverage percentage"
        value: ${{ jobs.test.outputs.coverage }}

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      results: ${{ steps.summary.outputs.results }}
      coverage: ${{ steps.coverage.outputs.percentage }}
    
    steps:
      - name: Run JobPost tests
        id: test
        run: |
          python manage.py test jobpost --verbosity=2
          echo "test_status=passed" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Calculate JobPost coverage
        id: coverage
        run: |
          coverage run manage.py test jobpost
          COVERAGE=$(coverage report | grep TOTAL | awk '{print $4}')
          echo "percentage=$COVERAGE" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "JobPost coverage: $COVERAGE"
      
      - name: Generate test summary
        id: summary
        run: |
          SUMMARY="Tests: Passed, Coverage: ${{ steps.coverage.outputs.percentage }}"
          echo "results=$SUMMARY" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

---
# .github/workflows/jobpost-ci.yml
name: JobPost CI

on: [pull_request]

jobs:
  test:
    uses: ./.github/workflows/jobpost-test-reusable.yml
  
  report:
    needs: test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Display JobPost test results
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost Test Report"
          echo "Results: ${{ jobs.test.outputs.test_results }}"
          echo "Coverage: ${{ jobs.test.outputs.coverage }}"
      
      - name: Comment on PR
        run: |
          COMMENT="## JobPost Test Results\n"
          COMMENT="${COMMENT}${{ jobs.test.outputs.test_results }}\n"
          COMMENT="${COMMENT}Coverage: ${{ jobs.test.outputs.coverage }}"
          echo "$COMMENT"
    🧪 JobPost test reusable workflow using jobs context

    3. JobPost multi-stage deployment

    # .github/workflows/jobpost-deploy-reusable.yml
name: JobPost Reusable Deploy

on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      environment:
        required: true
        type: string
    outputs:
      deploy_url:
        description: "JobPost deployment URL"
        value: ${{ jobs.deploy.outputs.url }}
      deploy_status:
        description: "JobPost deployment status"
        value: ${{ jobs.deploy.outputs.status }}

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: ${{ inputs.environment }}
    outputs:
      url: ${{ steps.deploy.outputs.url }}
      status: ${{ steps.deploy.outputs.status }}
    
    steps:
      - name: Deploy JobPost
        id: deploy
        run: |
          URL="https://jobpost-${{ inputs.environment }}.example.com"
          echo "url=$URL" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "status=deployed" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "Deployed to: $URL"

---
# .github/workflows/jobpost-release.yml
name: JobPost Release

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  deploy-staging:
    uses: ./.github/workflows/jobpost-deploy-reusable.yml
    with:
      environment: staging
  
  deploy-production:
    needs: deploy-staging
    uses: ./.github/workflows/jobpost-deploy-reusable.yml
    with:
      environment: production
  
  notify:
    needs: [deploy-staging, deploy-production]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Send JobPost deployment notification
        run: |
          echo "🚀 JobPost Deployment Complete"
          echo "Staging URL: ${{ jobs.deploy-staging.outputs.deploy_url }}"
          echo "Staging Status: ${{ jobs.deploy-staging.outputs.deploy_status }}"
          echo "Production URL: ${{ jobs.deploy-production.outputs.deploy_url }}"
          echo "Production Status: ${{ jobs.deploy-production.outputs.deploy_status }}"
    🚀 JobPost multi-stage deployment using jobs context

    4. JobPost conditional workflow based on outputs

    # .github/workflows/jobpost-check-reusable.yml
name: JobPost Reusable Checks

on:
  workflow_call:
    outputs:
      needs_migration:
        description: "Whether JobPost needs database migration"
        value: ${{ jobs.check.outputs.needs_migration }}
      has_changes:
        description: "Whether JobPost has code changes"
        value: ${{ jobs.check.outputs.has_changes }}
      security_issues:
        description: "Number of security issues found"
        value: ${{ jobs.check.outputs.security_count }}

jobs:
  check:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      needs_migration: ${{ steps.migration.outputs.needed }}
      has_changes: ${{ steps.changes.outputs.detected }}
      security_count: ${{ steps.security.outputs.count }}
    
    steps:
      - name: Check JobPost migrations
        id: migration
        run: |
          # Check for pending migrations
          PENDING=$(python manage.py showmigrations --plan | grep "\[ \]" | wc -l)
          if [ $PENDING -gt 0 ]; then
            echo "needed=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          else
            echo "needed=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi
      
      - name: Check JobPost changes
        id: changes
        run: |
          # Check for changes in jobpost/ directory
          if git diff --name-only ${{ github.event.before }} ${{ github.sha }} | grep -q "^jobpost/"; then
            echo "detected=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          else
            echo "detected=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi
      
      - name: JobPost security scan
        id: security
        run: |
          bandit -r jobpost/ anihpj/ -f json -o scan.json
          COUNT=$(cat scan.json | jq '.results | length')
          echo "count=$COUNT" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

---
# .github/workflows/jobpost-smart-deploy.yml
name: JobPost Smart Deploy

on: [push]

jobs:
  check:
    uses: ./.github/workflows/jobpost-check-reusable.yml
  
  migrate:
    needs: check
    if: jobs.check.outputs.needs_migration == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Run JobPost migrations
        run: |
          echo "🗄️ Running JobPost database migrations"
          python manage.py migrate
  
  deploy:
    needs: check
    if: jobs.check.outputs.has_changes == 'true' && jobs.check.outputs.security_issues == '0'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Deploy JobPost
        run: |
          echo "🚀 Deploying JobPost"
          python manage.py deploy
  
  security-alert:
    needs: check
    if: jobs.check.outputs.security_issues != '0'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Alert security issues
        run: |
          echo "⚠️ JobPost has ${{ jobs.check.outputs.security_issues }} security issues"
          exit 1
    🎯 JobPost conditional workflows based on jobs context outputs

    5. JobPost composite reusable workflow

    # .github/workflows/jobpost-full-ci-reusable.yml
name: JobPost Full CI

on:
  workflow_call:
    outputs:
      build_version:
        value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.version }}
      test_status:
        value: ${{ jobs.test.outputs.status }}
      lint_score:
        value: ${{ jobs.lint.outputs.score }}
      overall_status:
        value: ${{ jobs.report.outputs.status }}

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      version: ${{ steps.version.outputs.ver }}
    steps:
      - name: Build JobPost
        id: version
        run: |
          VERSION="v1.0.${{ github.run_number }}"
          echo "ver=$VERSION" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          python manage.py collectstatic --noinput
  
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      status: ${{ steps.test.outputs.result }}
    steps:
      - name: Test JobPost
        id: test
        run: |
          python manage.py test jobpost
          echo "result=passed" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
  
  lint:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      score: ${{ steps.lint.outputs.score }}
    steps:
      - name: Lint JobPost
        id: lint
        run: |
          SCORE=$(pylint jobpost/ | grep "rated at" | awk '{print $7}' | cut -d/ -f1)
          echo "score=$SCORE" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
  
  report:
    needs: [build, test, lint]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      status: ${{ steps.report.outputs.overall }}
    steps:
      - name: Generate report
        id: report
        run: |
          if [ "${{ needs.test.outputs.status }}" == "passed" ]; then
            echo "overall=success" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          else
            echo "overall=failed" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi

---
# .github/workflows/jobpost-main.yml
name: JobPost Main

on: [push]

jobs:
  ci:
    uses: ./.github/workflows/jobpost-full-ci-reusable.yml
  
  summary:
    needs: ci
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Display JobPost CI summary
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost CI Summary"
          echo "Version: ${{ jobs.ci.outputs.build_version }}"
          echo "Tests: ${{ jobs.ci.outputs.test_status }}"
          echo "Lint Score: ${{ jobs.ci.outputs.lint_score }}"
          echo "Overall: ${{ jobs.ci.outputs.overall_status }}"
    📊 JobPost composite reusable workflow using jobs context

    steps context

    Docs: steps context

    Access information about steps that have already run in the current job using the steps context.

    Full description

    The steps context contains information about the steps in the current job that have already been completed and have an id. For JobPost workflows, this is essential for passing data between steps, checking step outcomes, and building complex multi-step workflows where later steps depend on earlier step results. Each step must have an id to be accessible in the steps context. The context provides access to step outputs, conclusions, and outcomes, enabling sophisticated workflow logic and data flow through the CI/CD pipeline.

    Syntax

    # Access step outputs
${{ steps.step_id.outputs.output_name }}

# Access step status
${{ steps.step_id.conclusion }}  # success, failure, cancelled, skipped
${{ steps.step_id.outcome }}     # success, failure, cancelled, skipped

# Define step with id and outputs
- name: Build JobPost
  id: build
  run: |
    echo "version=1.0.0" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    echo "artifact=jobpost.tar.gz" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

# Use step outputs
- name: Deploy JobPost
  run: |
    echo "Version: ${{ steps.build.outputs.version }}"
    echo "Artifact: ${{ steps.build.outputs.artifact }}"

    Parameters description

    Step outputs:
    • steps.<step_id>.outputs.<output_name> - Value set by the step using $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    Step status:
    • steps.<step_id>.conclusion - Final result after continue-on-error is applied
    • steps.<step_id>.outcome - Result before continue-on-error is applied
    Status values:
    • success - Step completed successfully
    • failure - Step failed
    • cancelled - Workflow was cancelled
    • skipped - Step was skipped due to conditional

    Return value

    The steps context returns an object with nested objects for each step that has an id, containing string values for outputs, conclusion, and outcome.

    Examples

    1. JobPost build information sharing

    name: JobPost Build Pipeline

on: [push]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Generate JobPost version
        id: version
        run: |
          VERSION="v1.0.${{ github.run_number }}"
          BUILD_TIME=$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
          COMMIT_SHORT="${{ github.sha }}"
          COMMIT_SHORT="${COMMIT_SHORT:0:7}"
          
          echo "version=$VERSION" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "build_time=$BUILD_TIME" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "commit=$COMMIT_SHORT" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          
          echo "Generated JobPost version: $VERSION"
      
      - name: Build JobPost application
        id: build
        run: |
          echo "🏗️ Building JobPost ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}"
          
          python manage.py collectstatic --noinput
          STATIC_SIZE=$(du -sh staticfiles | cut -f1)
          
          echo "static_size=$STATIC_SIZE" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "build_status=success" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Create JobPost artifact
        id: artifact
        run: |
          ARTIFACT_NAME="jobpost-${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}-${{ steps.version.outputs.build_time }}.tar.gz"
          
          tar -czf "$ARTIFACT_NAME" \
            jobpost/ \
            anihpj/ \
            manage.py \
            staticfiles/
          
          ARTIFACT_SIZE=$(du -h "$ARTIFACT_NAME" | cut -f1)
          
          echo "name=$ARTIFACT_NAME" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "size=$ARTIFACT_SIZE" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Generate JobPost build report
        run: |
          echo "📦 JobPost Build Report"
          echo "======================"
          echo "Version: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}"
          echo "Build Time: ${{ steps.version.outputs.build_time }}"
          echo "Commit: ${{ steps.version.outputs.commit }}"
          echo "Static Size: ${{ steps.build.outputs.static_size }}"
          echo "Artifact: ${{ steps.artifact.outputs.name }}"
          echo "Artifact Size: ${{ steps.artifact.outputs.size }}"
          echo "Build Status: ${{ steps.build.outputs.build_status }}"
    📦 JobPost build information sharing using steps context

    2. JobPost conditional step execution

    name: JobPost Conditional Steps

on: [push]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Run JobPost unit tests
        id: unit_tests
        continue-on-error: true
        run: |
          python manage.py test jobpost.tests.unit
          echo "passed=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Run JobPost integration tests
        id: integration_tests
        if: steps.unit_tests.outcome == 'success'
        continue-on-error: true
        run: |
          python manage.py test jobpost.tests.integration
          echo "passed=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Run JobPost E2E tests
        id: e2e_tests
        if: |
          steps.unit_tests.outcome == 'success' &&
          steps.integration_tests.outcome == 'success'
        run: |
          python manage.py test jobpost.tests.e2e
          echo "passed=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Generate JobPost test report
        if: always()
        run: |
          echo "🧪 JobPost Test Report"
          echo "====================="
          
          echo "Unit Tests:"
          echo "  Outcome: ${{ steps.unit_tests.outcome }}"
          echo "  Conclusion: ${{ steps.unit_tests.conclusion }}"
          
          if [ "${{ steps.integration_tests.outcome }}" != "" ]; then
            echo "Integration Tests:"
            echo "  Outcome: ${{ steps.integration_tests.outcome }}"
            echo "  Conclusion: ${{ steps.integration_tests.conclusion }}"
          else
            echo "Integration Tests: Skipped"
          fi
          
          if [ "${{ steps.e2e_tests.outcome }}" != "" ]; then
            echo "E2E Tests:"
            echo "  Outcome: ${{ steps.e2e_tests.outcome }}"
            echo "  Conclusion: ${{ steps.e2e_tests.conclusion }}"
          else
            echo "E2E Tests: Skipped"
          fi
      
      - name: Fail if critical tests failed
        if: |
          steps.unit_tests.outcome == 'failure' ||
          steps.integration_tests.outcome == 'failure'
        run: |
          echo "❌ JobPost critical tests failed"
          exit 1
    🎯 JobPost conditional steps using steps context

    3. JobPost deployment with rollback

    name: JobPost Deploy with Rollback

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Backup current JobPost deployment
        id: backup
        run: |
          BACKUP_ID="backup-$(date +%s)"
          echo "Creating JobPost backup: $BACKUP_ID"
          
          # Create backup
          python manage.py backup --id="$BACKUP_ID"
          
          echo "backup_id=$BACKUP_ID" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "backup_status=success" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Deploy new JobPost version
        id: deploy
        continue-on-error: true
        run: |
          echo "🚀 Deploying JobPost"
          python manage.py deploy --production
          echo "deploy_status=success" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Run JobPost smoke tests
        id: smoke_tests
        if: steps.deploy.outcome == 'success'
        continue-on-error: true
        run: |
          echo "🔍 Running JobPost smoke tests"
          python manage.py test jobpost.tests.smoke
          echo "smoke_status=passed" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Rollback JobPost deployment
        if: |
          steps.deploy.outcome == 'failure' ||
          steps.smoke_tests.outcome == 'failure'
        run: |
          echo "⚠️ Rolling back JobPost deployment"
          echo "Backup ID: ${{ steps.backup.outputs.backup_id }}"
          
          python manage.py restore --id="${{ steps.backup.outputs.backup_id }}"
          
          echo "❌ JobPost deployment failed and rolled back"
          exit 1
      
      - name: Cleanup JobPost backup
        if: |
          steps.deploy.outcome == 'success' &&
          steps.smoke_tests.outcome == 'success'
        run: |
          echo "✅ JobPost deployment successful"
          echo "Cleaning up backup: ${{ steps.backup.outputs.backup_id }}"
          python manage.py cleanup-backup --id="${{ steps.backup.outputs.backup_id }}"
      
      - name: Deployment summary
        if: always()
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost Deployment Summary"
          echo "Backup: ${{ steps.backup.outputs.backup_status }}"
          echo "Deploy: ${{ steps.deploy.outcome }}"
          echo "Smoke Tests: ${{ steps.smoke_tests.outcome }}"
    🔄 JobPost deployment with rollback using steps context

    4. JobPost multi-step data pipeline

    name: JobPost Data Pipeline

on: [push]

jobs:
  process:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Extract JobPost data
        id: extract
        run: |
          echo "📥 Extracting JobPost data"
          RECORD_COUNT=1234
          EXTRACT_TIME=$(date +%s)
          
          echo "record_count=$RECORD_COUNT" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "extract_time=$EXTRACT_TIME" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "extract_status=complete" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Transform JobPost data
        id: transform
        if: steps.extract.outputs.extract_status == 'complete'
        run: |
          echo "🔄 Transforming JobPost data"
          echo "Processing ${{ steps.extract.outputs.record_count }} records"
          
          TRANSFORM_TIME=$(date +%s)
          START_TIME=${{ steps.extract.outputs.extract_time }}
          DURATION=$((TRANSFORM_TIME - START_TIME))
          PROCESSED_COUNT=${{ steps.extract.outputs.record_count }}
          
          echo "duration=$DURATION" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "processed=$PROCESSED_COUNT" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "transform_status=complete" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Load JobPost data
        id: load
        if: steps.transform.outputs.transform_status == 'complete'
        run: |
          echo "📤 Loading JobPost data"
          echo "Loading ${{ steps.transform.outputs.processed }} records"
          
          LOAD_TIME=$(date +%s)
          START_TIME=${{ steps.extract.outputs.extract_time }}
          TOTAL_DURATION=$((LOAD_TIME - START_TIME))
          
          echo "load_time=$LOAD_TIME" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "total_duration=$TOTAL_DURATION" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "load_status=complete" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Validate JobPost data
        id: validate
        if: steps.load.outputs.load_status == 'complete'
        run: |
          echo "✅ Validating JobPost data"
          VALIDATION_ERRORS=0
          VALIDATION_WARNINGS=5
          
          echo "errors=$VALIDATION_ERRORS" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "warnings=$VALIDATION_WARNINGS" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Generate JobPost pipeline report
        if: always()
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost Data Pipeline Report"
          echo "================================"
          echo "Extract:"
          echo "  Status: ${{ steps.extract.outputs.extract_status }}"
          echo "  Records: ${{ steps.extract.outputs.record_count }}"
          echo ""
          echo "Transform:"
          echo "  Status: ${{ steps.transform.outputs.transform_status }}"
          echo "  Duration: ${{ steps.transform.outputs.duration }}s"
          echo "  Processed: ${{ steps.transform.outputs.processed }}"
          echo ""
          echo "Load:"
          echo "  Status: ${{ steps.load.outputs.load_status }}"
          echo "  Total Duration: ${{ steps.load.outputs.total_duration }}s"
          echo ""
          echo "Validation:"
          echo "  Errors: ${{ steps.validate.outputs.errors }}"
          echo "  Warnings: ${{ steps.validate.outputs.warnings }}"
    📊 JobPost data pipeline using steps context

    5. JobPost performance benchmarking

    name: JobPost Performance Benchmark

on: [push]

jobs:
  benchmark:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Benchmark JobPost database queries
        id: db_benchmark
        run: |
          echo "🗄️ Benchmarking JobPost database"
          
          START=$(date +%s%N)
          python manage.py benchmark db_queries
          END=$(date +%s%N)
          
          DB_TIME=$(( (END - START) / 1000000 ))
          
          echo "time_ms=$DB_TIME" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "queries=150" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Benchmark JobPost API endpoints
        id: api_benchmark
        run: |
          echo "🌐 Benchmarking JobPost API"
          
          START=$(date +%s%N)
          python manage.py benchmark api_endpoints
          END=$(date +%s%N)
          
          API_TIME=$(( (END - START) / 1000000 ))
          
          echo "time_ms=$API_TIME" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "endpoints=25" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "requests=1000" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Benchmark JobPost template rendering
        id: template_benchmark
        run: |
          echo "📄 Benchmarking JobPost templates"
          
          START=$(date +%s%N)
          python manage.py benchmark templates
          END=$(date +%s%N)
          
          TEMPLATE_TIME=$(( (END - START) / 1000000 ))
          
          echo "time_ms=$TEMPLATE_TIME" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "templates=50" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Calculate JobPost performance score
        id: score
        run: |
          DB_TIME=${{ steps.db_benchmark.outputs.time_ms }}
          API_TIME=${{ steps.api_benchmark.outputs.time_ms }}
          TEMPLATE_TIME=${{ steps.template_benchmark.outputs.time_ms }}
          
          TOTAL_TIME=$((DB_TIME + API_TIME + TEMPLATE_TIME))
          
          # Calculate score (lower time = higher score)
          SCORE=$((10000 - (TOTAL_TIME / 10)))
          
          echo "total_time=$TOTAL_TIME" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "score=$SCORE" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Compare with baseline
        run: |
          BASELINE_SCORE=8500
          CURRENT_SCORE=${{ steps.score.outputs.score }}
          
          DIFF=$((CURRENT_SCORE - BASELINE_SCORE))
          PERCENT=$(( (DIFF * 100) / BASELINE_SCORE ))
          
          echo "📊 JobPost Performance Report"
          echo "=============================="
          echo "Database:"
          echo "  Time: ${{ steps.db_benchmark.outputs.time_ms }}ms"
          echo "  Queries: ${{ steps.db_benchmark.outputs.queries }}"
          echo ""
          echo "API:"
          echo "  Time: ${{ steps.api_benchmark.outputs.time_ms }}ms"
          echo "  Endpoints: ${{ steps.api_benchmark.outputs.endpoints }}"
          echo "  Requests: ${{ steps.api_benchmark.outputs.requests }}"
          echo ""
          echo "Templates:"
          echo "  Time: ${{ steps.template_benchmark.outputs.time_ms }}ms"
          echo "  Templates: ${{ steps.template_benchmark.outputs.templates }}"
          echo ""
          echo "Overall:"
          echo "  Total Time: ${{ steps.score.outputs.total_time }}ms"
          echo "  Score: $CURRENT_SCORE / 10000"
          echo "  Baseline: $BASELINE_SCORE"
          echo "  Difference: ${DIFF} (${PERCENT}%)"
          
          if [ $CURRENT_SCORE -lt $BASELINE_SCORE ]; then
            echo "⚠️ Performance regression detected!"
          else
            echo "✅ Performance meets or exceeds baseline"
          fi
    ⚡ JobPost performance benchmarking using steps context

    runner context

    Docs: runner context

    Access information about the runner executing the current job using the runner context.

    Full description

    The runner context contains information about the runner that is executing the current job, including the operating system, architecture, temporary directories, and tool cache location. For JobPost workflows, this context is crucial for writing portable workflows that adapt to different runner environments, managing temporary files, accessing pre-installed tools, and optimizing workflows based on the runner's capabilities. The runner context helps ensure JobPost workflows work correctly across different operating systems (Linux, macOS, Windows) and architectures (x64, ARM).

    Syntax

    # Access runner properties
${{ runner.os }}              # Operating system (Linux, Windows, macOS)
${{ runner.arch }}            # Architecture (X64, ARM, ARM64)
${{ runner.name }}            # Runner name
${{ runner.temp }}            # Temporary directory path
${{ runner.tool_cache }}      # Tool cache directory path
${{ runner.workspace }}       # Default working directory path

    Parameters description

    Runner properties:
    • runner.os - Operating system of the runner (Linux, Windows, macOS)
    • runner.arch - Architecture of the runner (X64, ARM, ARM64)
    • runner.name - Name of the runner
    • runner.environment - Environment type (github-hosted, self-hosted)
    Directory paths:
    • runner.temp - Path to temporary directory (cleaned between jobs)
    • runner.tool_cache - Path to tool cache directory (persists across jobs)
    • runner.workspace - Path to default working directory

    Return value

    The runner context returns an object with string values for operating system, architecture, name, and directory paths.

    Examples

    1. JobPost cross-platform workflows

    name: JobPost Cross-Platform

on: [push]

jobs:
  build:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest]
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    
    steps:
      - name: Display JobPost runner info
        run: |
          echo "🖥️ JobPost Runner Information"
          echo "OS: ${{ runner.os }}"
          echo "Architecture: ${{ runner.arch }}"
          echo "Runner Name: ${{ runner.name }}"
          echo "Environment: ${{ runner.environment }}"
      
      - name: Set JobPost platform-specific variables
        run: |
          if [ "${{ runner.os }}" == "Linux" ]; then
            echo "PLATFORM=linux" >> $GITHUB_ENV
            echo "PYTHON_CMD=python3" >> $GITHUB_ENV
            echo "PKG_MANAGER=apt-get" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          elif [ "${{ runner.os }}" == "macOS" ]; then
            echo "PLATFORM=macos" >> $GITHUB_ENV
            echo "PYTHON_CMD=python3" >> $GITHUB_ENV
            echo "PKG_MANAGER=brew" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          elif [ "${{ runner.os }}" == "Windows" ]; then
            echo "PLATFORM=windows" >> $GITHUB_ENV
            echo "PYTHON_CMD=python" >> $GITHUB_ENV
            echo "PKG_MANAGER=choco" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          fi
        shell: bash
      
      - name: Build JobPost for platform
        run: |
          echo "🏗️ Building JobPost for ${{ runner.os }}"
          ${{ env.PYTHON_CMD }} manage.py collectstatic --noinput
          
          ARTIFACT="jobpost-${{ env.PLATFORM }}-${{ runner.arch }}.tar.gz"
          echo "Artifact: $ARTIFACT"
        shell: bash
      
      - name: Run JobPost platform-specific tests
        run: |
          echo "🧪 Running JobPost tests on ${{ runner.os }}"
          ${{ env.PYTHON_CMD }} manage.py test jobpost
        shell: bash
    🖥️ JobPost cross-platform builds using runner context

    2. JobPost temporary file management

    name: JobPost Temp Files

on: [push]

jobs:
  process:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Display JobPost directories
        run: |
          echo "📁 JobPost Directory Information"
          echo "Temp: ${{ runner.temp }}"
          echo "Tool Cache: ${{ runner.tool_cache }}"
          echo "Workspace: ${{ runner.workspace }}"
      
      - name: Generate JobPost temporary files
        run: |
          TEMP_DIR="${{ runner.temp }}/jobpost"
          mkdir -p "$TEMP_DIR"
          
          echo "Creating JobPost temporary files in: $TEMP_DIR"
          
          # Generate temporary data
          python manage.py dumpdata jobpost > "$TEMP_DIR/data.json"
          python manage.py collectstatic --noinput -c --noinput \
            --output="$TEMP_DIR/static"
          
          # Create build artifacts in temp
          tar -czf "$TEMP_DIR/jobpost-build.tar.gz" \
            jobpost/ anihpj/ manage.py
          
          echo "TEMP_DIR=$TEMP_DIR" >> $GITHUB_ENV
      
      - name: Process JobPost temporary files
        run: |
          echo "📦 Processing JobPost temp files from: ${{ env.TEMP_DIR }}"
          
          ls -lh "${{ env.TEMP_DIR }}"
          
          # Process files
          FILE_COUNT=$(find "${{ env.TEMP_DIR }}" -type f | wc -l)
          TOTAL_SIZE=$(du -sh "${{ env.TEMP_DIR }}" | cut -f1)
          
          echo "Files: $FILE_COUNT"
          echo "Total Size: $TOTAL_SIZE"
      
      - name: Store JobPost artifacts in cache directory
        run: |
          CACHE_DIR="${{ runner.tool_cache }}/jobpost-cache"
          mkdir -p "$CACHE_DIR"
          
          echo "💾 Storing JobPost artifacts in: $CACHE_DIR"
          
          # Move important artifacts to cache (persists across jobs)
          cp "${{ env.TEMP_DIR }}/jobpost-build.tar.gz" "$CACHE_DIR/"
          
          echo "✅ JobPost artifacts cached"
      
      - name: Cleanup JobPost temp files
        if: always()
        run: |
          echo "🧹 Cleaning up JobPost temp files"
          rm -rf "${{ env.TEMP_DIR }}"
          echo "✅ Temp directory cleaned"
    📁 JobPost temporary file management using runner context

    3. JobPost runner-optimized workflows

    name: JobPost Optimized Workflow

on: [push]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Detect JobPost runner capabilities
        id: capabilities
        run: |
          echo "🔍 Detecting JobPost runner capabilities"
          
          # Check CPU cores
          CORES=$(nproc)
          echo "cores=$CORES" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          
          # Check memory
          MEMORY=$(free -g | awk '/^Mem:/{print $2}')
          echo "memory_gb=$MEMORY" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          
          # Check disk space
          DISK=$(df -h ${{ runner.workspace }} | awk 'NR==2{print $4}')
          echo "disk_available=$DISK" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          
          echo "Cores: $CORES"
          echo "Memory: ${MEMORY}GB"
          echo "Disk: $DISK"
      
      - name: Configure JobPost for runner
        run: |
          CORES=${{ steps.capabilities.outputs.cores }}
          
          echo "⚙️ Configuring JobPost for $CORES cores"
          
          # Set parallel workers based on cores
          if [ $CORES -ge 4 ]; then
            WORKERS=4
          elif [ $CORES -ge 2 ]; then
            WORKERS=2
          else
            WORKERS=1
          fi
          
          echo "WORKERS=$WORKERS" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          
          # Configure Django settings
          cat >> anihpj/settings.py << EOF
          
          # CI Runner optimizations
          DATABASES['default']['CONN_MAX_AGE'] = 600
          DATABASES['default']['OPTIONS'] = {'MAX_CONNECTIONS': $WORKERS}
          EOF
      
      - name: Run JobPost parallel tests
        run: |
          echo "🧪 Running JobPost tests with ${{ env.WORKERS }} workers"
          
          python manage.py test jobpost \
            --parallel=${{ env.WORKERS }} \
            --verbosity=2
      
      - name: Build JobPost with optimization
        run: |
          echo "🏗️ Building JobPost (optimized for ${{ runner.os }}/${{ runner.arch }})"
          
          # Use runner-specific optimizations
          if [ "${{ runner.arch }}" == "ARM64" ]; then
            export CFLAGS="-march=armv8-a"
          else
            export CFLAGS="-march=x86-64"
          fi
          
          python manage.py collectstatic --noinput
          
          echo "✅ JobPost build optimized for runner"
    ⚡ JobPost runner-optimized workflows using runner context

    4. JobPost self-hosted vs GitHub-hosted runners

    name: JobPost Runner Type Detection

on: [push]

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Detect JobPost runner type
        id: runner_type
        run: |
          echo "🏃 JobPost Runner Type Detection"
          echo "Runner: ${{ runner.name }}"
          echo "Environment: ${{ runner.environment }}"
          
          if [ "${{ runner.environment }}" == "github-hosted" ]; then
            echo "type=github-hosted" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
            echo "Using GitHub-hosted runner"
          else
            echo "type=self-hosted" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
            echo "Using self-hosted runner"
          fi
      
      - name: Configure JobPost for GitHub-hosted
        if: steps.runner_type.outputs.type == 'github-hosted'
        run: |
          echo "⚙️ Configuring JobPost for GitHub-hosted runner"
          
          # Use public package indexes
          echo "PYPI_URL=https://pypi.org/simple" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          
          # Use public Docker registry
          echo "DOCKER_REGISTRY=docker.io" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          
          # Configure for external services
          echo "DATABASE_HOST=external-db.example.com" >> $GITHUB_ENV
      
      - name: Configure JobPost for self-hosted
        if: steps.runner_type.outputs.type == 'self-hosted'
        run: |
          echo "⚙️ Configuring JobPost for self-hosted runner"
          
          # Use internal package mirror
          echo "PYPI_URL=https://internal-pypi.corp.local/simple" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          
          # Use internal Docker registry
          echo "DOCKER_REGISTRY=registry.corp.local" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          
          # Use internal database
          echo "DATABASE_HOST=internal-db.corp.local" >> $GITHUB_ENV
      
      - name: Build JobPost with runner-specific config
        run: |
          echo "🏗️ Building JobPost"
          echo "PyPI: ${{ env.PYPI_URL }}"
          echo "Docker: ${{ env.DOCKER_REGISTRY }}"
          echo "Database: ${{ env.DATABASE_HOST }}"
          
          pip install --index-url="${{ env.PYPI_URL }}" -r requirements.txt
          python manage.py collectstatic --noinput
      
      - name: Deploy JobPost
        run: |
          echo "🚀 Deploying JobPost from ${{ steps.runner_type.outputs.type }} runner"
          
          if [ "${{ steps.runner_type.outputs.type }}" == "self-hosted" ]; then
            # Direct deployment for self-hosted
            python manage.py deploy --internal
          else
            # Remote deployment for GitHub-hosted
            python manage.py deploy --external
          fi
    🏃 JobPost self-hosted vs GitHub-hosted runner detection

    5. JobPost workspace and path management

    name: JobPost Workspace Management

on: [push]

jobs:
  organize:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Display JobPost workspace info
        run: |
          echo "📂 JobPost Workspace Information"
          echo "Workspace: ${{ runner.workspace }}"
          echo "Temp: ${{ runner.temp }}"
          echo "Tool Cache: ${{ runner.tool_cache }}"
          echo "Current Dir: $(pwd)"
      
      - name: Create JobPost directory structure
        run: |
          BASE="${{ runner.workspace }}/jobpost-build"
          
          echo "🏗️ Creating JobPost directory structure in: $BASE"
          
          mkdir -p "$BASE"/{source,build,dist,logs,cache}
          
          echo "BASE_DIR=$BASE" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          
          tree -L 2 "$BASE" || ls -R "$BASE"
      
      - name: Clone JobPost to custom location
        run: |
          echo "📥 Cloning JobPost to: ${{ env.BASE_DIR }}/source"
          
          cd "${{ env.BASE_DIR }}/source"
          git clone https://github.com/user/jobpost.git .
          
          echo "SOURCE_DIR=${{ env.BASE_DIR }}/source" >> $GITHUB_ENV
      
      - name: Build JobPost in custom directories
        run: |
          echo "🏗️ Building JobPost"
          
          cd "${{ env.SOURCE_DIR }}"
          
          # Build to custom output directory
          python manage.py collectstatic \
            --noinput \
            --output="${{ env.BASE_DIR }}/build/static"
          
          # Create distribution
          tar -czf "${{ env.BASE_DIR }}/dist/jobpost.tar.gz" \
            -C "${{ env.SOURCE_DIR }}" \
            jobpost/ anihpj/ manage.py
          
          # Store logs
          python manage.py check > "${{ env.BASE_DIR }}/logs/check.log" 2>&1
      
      - name: Use JobPost temp for intermediate files
        run: |
          TEMP_BUILD="${{ runner.temp }}/jobpost-temp"
          mkdir -p "$TEMP_BUILD"
          
          echo "🔧 Using temp for intermediate files: $TEMP_BUILD"
          
          # Generate large intermediate files in temp
          python manage.py dumpdata > "$TEMP_BUILD/data.json"
          
          # Process and move final output
          python scripts/process_data.py \
            --input="$TEMP_BUILD/data.json" \
            --output="${{ env.BASE_DIR }}/dist/data.processed.json"
          
          echo "✅ Intermediate files processed"
      
      - name: Cache JobPost dependencies
        run: |
          CACHE_DIR="${{ runner.tool_cache }}/jobpost"
          mkdir -p "$CACHE_DIR"
          
          echo "💾 Caching JobPost dependencies to: $CACHE_DIR"
          
          # Cache pip packages
          cp -r ~/.cache/pip "$CACHE_DIR/pip-cache"
          
          # Cache built wheels
          pip wheel -r requirements.txt -w "$CACHE_DIR/wheels"
          
          echo "✅ Dependencies cached"
      
      - name: Generate JobPost workspace report
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost Workspace Report"
          echo "=========================="
          echo "Workspace root: ${{ runner.workspace }}"
          echo "Base directory: ${{ env.BASE_DIR }}"
          echo ""
          echo "Directory sizes:"
          du -sh "${{ env.BASE_DIR }}"/* | sort -h
          echo ""
          echo "Total workspace usage:"
          du -sh "${{ runner.workspace }}"
    📂 JobPost workspace and path management using runner context

    secrets context

    Docs: secrets context

    Access secrets configured at repository, environment, or organization level using the secrets context.

    Full description

    The secrets context contains sensitive information like API keys, passwords, tokens, and certificates that you've configured in GitHub. Secrets are encrypted and should never be printed to logs. For JobPost workflows, secrets are essential for authentication with external services, database connections, deployment credentials, and third-party API integrations. Secrets follow a precedence order: environment-level secrets override repository-level, which override organization-level secrets. The GITHUB_TOKEN secret is automatically available in every workflow for authenticating with the GitHub API.

    Syntax

    # Access secrets
${{ secrets.SECRET_NAME }}
${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}  # Automatically available

# Use in environment variables
env:
  DATABASE_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.DB_PASSWORD }}
  API_KEY: ${{ secrets.API_KEY }}

# Pass to actions
- uses: some-action@v1
  with:
    token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
    api_key: ${{ secrets.API_KEY }}

    Parameters description

    Secret levels:
    • Organization-level - Available to all repositories in the organization
    • Repository-level - Available to all workflows in the repository
    • Environment-level - Available only when job specifies that environment
    Automatic secrets:
    • secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN - Automatically provided token for GitHub API authentication
    Security notes:
    • Secrets are automatically redacted from logs
    • Never print secrets directly to output
    • Secrets are not passed to workflows from forked repositories
    • Maximum 100 secrets per repository, 100 per organization, 100 per environment

    Return value

    The secrets context returns an object with string values for each secret name. Secret values are encrypted and redacted in logs.

    Examples

    1. JobPost database and service authentication

    name: JobPost with Secrets

on: [push]

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: production
    
    steps:
      - name: Configure JobPost database
        env:
          DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.DATABASE_URL }}
          DATABASE_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.DB_PASSWORD }}
        run: |
          echo "🗄️ Configuring JobPost database"
          
          # Create Django settings with secrets
          cat > anihpj/settings_prod.py << EOF
          from .settings import *
          
          DATABASES = {
              'default': {
                  'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.postgresql',
                  'NAME': 'jobpost_prod',
                  'USER': 'jobpost',
                  'PASSWORD': '$DATABASE_PASSWORD',
                  'HOST': 'db.example.com',
                  'PORT': '5432',
              }
          }
          
          SECRET_KEY = '${{ secrets.DJANGO_SECRET_KEY }}'
          DEBUG = False
          ALLOWED_HOSTS = ['jobpost.com', 'www.jobpost.com']
          EOF
          
          echo "✅ JobPost configured with production secrets"
      
      - name: Run JobPost migrations
        env:
          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings_prod
          DATABASE_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.DB_PASSWORD }}
        run: |
          echo "📊 Running JobPost database migrations"
          python manage.py migrate --noinput
      
      - name: Configure JobPost cache
        env:
          REDIS_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.REDIS_PASSWORD }}
        run: |
          echo "💾 Configuring JobPost Redis cache"
          
          # Configure Redis with authentication
          cat >> anihpj/settings_prod.py << EOF
          
          CACHES = {
              'default': {
                  'BACKEND': 'django.core.cache.backends.redis.RedisCache',
                  'LOCATION': 'redis://redis.example.com:6379/0',
                  'OPTIONS': {
                      'PASSWORD': '$REDIS_PASSWORD',
                  }
              }
          }
          EOF
    🔐 JobPost database authentication using secrets context

    2. JobPost third-party API integrations

    name: JobPost API Integrations

on: [push]

jobs:
  integrate:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Configure JobPost external services
        env:
          STRIPE_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.STRIPE_API_KEY }}
          SENDGRID_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.SENDGRID_API_KEY }}
          AWS_ACCESS_KEY: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
          AWS_SECRET_KEY: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
          SENTRY_DSN: ${{ secrets.SENTRY_DSN }}
        run: |
          echo "🔌 Configuring JobPost external integrations"
          
          # Create environment file with secrets
          cat > .env.production << EOF
          # Payment processing
          STRIPE_API_KEY=$STRIPE_API_KEY
          STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=${{ secrets.STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET }}
          
          # Email service
          SENDGRID_API_KEY=$SENDGRID_API_KEY
          
          # Cloud storage
          AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=$AWS_ACCESS_KEY
          AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=$AWS_SECRET_KEY
          AWS_STORAGE_BUCKET_NAME=jobpost-media
          
          # Error tracking
          SENTRY_DSN=$SENTRY_DSN
          EOF
          
          echo "✅ JobPost external services configured"
      
      - name: Test JobPost Stripe integration
        env:
          STRIPE_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.STRIPE_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "💳 Testing JobPost Stripe integration"
          python manage.py test jobpost.tests.test_payments
      
      - name: Send JobPost deployment notification
        env:
          SLACK_WEBHOOK: ${{ secrets.SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL }}
        run: |
          echo "📢 Sending JobPost deployment notification"
          
          curl -X POST "$SLACK_WEBHOOK" \
               -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
               -d "{
                 \"text\": \"JobPost deployed to production\",
                 \"username\": \"GitHub Actions\",
                 \"icon_emoji\": \":rocket:\"
               }"
    🔌 JobPost third-party API integrations using secrets context

    3. JobPost deployment with SSH and certificates

    name: JobPost SSH Deploy

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Setup JobPost SSH key
        env:
          SSH_PRIVATE_KEY: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_SSH_KEY }}
          SSH_KNOWN_HOSTS: ${{ secrets.SSH_KNOWN_HOSTS }}
        run: |
          echo "🔑 Setting up JobPost SSH authentication"
          
          mkdir -p ~/.ssh
          chmod 700 ~/.ssh
          
          # Install SSH private key
          echo "$SSH_PRIVATE_KEY" > ~/.ssh/id_rsa
          chmod 600 ~/.ssh/id_rsa
          
          # Install known hosts
          echo "$SSH_KNOWN_HOSTS" > ~/.ssh/known_hosts
          chmod 644 ~/.ssh/known_hosts
          
          echo "✅ SSH key configured"
      
      - name: Deploy JobPost via SSH
        env:
          DEPLOY_HOST: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }}
          DEPLOY_USER: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_USER }}
        run: |
          echo "🚀 Deploying JobPost to production server"
          
          # Copy files to server
          scp -r jobpost/ anihpj/ manage.py requirements.txt \
              "$DEPLOY_USER@$DEPLOY_HOST":/var/www/jobpost/
          
          # Run deployment commands on server
          ssh "$DEPLOY_USER@$DEPLOY_HOST" << 'EOF'
            cd /var/www/jobpost
            source venv/bin/activate
            pip install -r requirements.txt
            python manage.py migrate --noinput
            python manage.py collectstatic --noinput
            sudo systemctl restart jobpost
          EOF
          
          echo "✅ JobPost deployed successfully"
      
      - name: Configure JobPost SSL certificates
        env:
          SSL_CERTIFICATE: ${{ secrets.SSL_CERTIFICATE }}
          SSL_PRIVATE_KEY: ${{ secrets.SSL_PRIVATE_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "🔒 Configuring JobPost SSL certificates"
          
          # Note: In practice, store these on the server securely
          echo "SSL certificate and key configured for HTTPS"
    🔑 JobPost SSH deployment using secrets context

    4. JobPost with GITHUB_TOKEN

    name: JobPost GitHub Integration

on: [pull_request]

jobs:
  check:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      
      - name: Run JobPost tests
        run: |
          echo "🧪 Running JobPost tests"
          python manage.py test jobpost
      
      - name: Comment JobPost test results on PR
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
        run: |
          echo "💬 Commenting JobPost test results on PR #$PR_NUMBER"
          
          COMMENT="## JobPost Test Results\n\n"
          COMMENT="${COMMENT}✅ All tests passed!\n\n"
          COMMENT="${COMMENT}- Tests run: 42\n"
          COMMENT="${COMMENT}- Duration: 3m 24s\n"
          COMMENT="${COMMENT}- Coverage: 87%\n"
          
          # Post comment using GitHub API
          curl -X POST \
               -H "Authorization: Bearer $GITHUB_TOKEN" \
               -H "Accept: application/vnd.github.v3+json" \
               https://api.github.com/repos/${{ github.repository }}/issues/$PR_NUMBER/comments \
               -d "{\"body\": \"$COMMENT\"}"
      
      - name: Update JobPost PR labels
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
        run: |
          echo "🏷️ Updating JobPost PR labels"
          
          # Add "tests-passed" label
          curl -X POST \
               -H "Authorization: Bearer $GITHUB_TOKEN" \
               -H "Accept: application/vnd.github.v3+json" \
               https://api.github.com/repos/${{ github.repository }}/issues/$PR_NUMBER/labels \
               -d '{"labels": ["tests-passed", "ready-for-review"]}'
      
      - name: Create JobPost deployment
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          echo "🚀 Creating JobPost deployment"
          
          # Create deployment via GitHub API
          curl -X POST \
               -H "Authorization: Bearer $GITHUB_TOKEN" \
               -H "Accept: application/vnd.github.v3+json" \
               https://api.github.com/repos/${{ github.repository }}/deployments \
               -d "{
                 \"ref\": \"${{ github.sha }}\",
                 \"environment\": \"staging\",
                 \"description\": \"JobPost staging deployment\",
                 \"auto_merge\": false
               }"
    🔗 JobPost GitHub API integration using GITHUB_TOKEN

    5. JobPost environment-specific secrets

    name: JobPost Multi-Environment

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, staging, develop]

jobs:
  deploy-production:
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: production
    steps:
      - name: Deploy JobPost to production
        env:
          DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.DATABASE_URL }}
          API_KEY: ${{ secrets.API_KEY }}
          REDIS_URL: ${{ secrets.REDIS_URL }}
        run: |
          echo "🚀 Deploying JobPost to Production"
          # Production secrets are used here
          python manage.py deploy \
            --database="$DATABASE_URL" \
            --cache="$REDIS_URL"
  
  deploy-staging:
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/staging'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: staging
    steps:
      - name: Deploy JobPost to staging
        env:
          DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.DATABASE_URL }}
          API_KEY: ${{ secrets.API_KEY }}
          REDIS_URL: ${{ secrets.REDIS_URL }}
        run: |
          echo "🧪 Deploying JobPost to Staging"
          # Staging secrets override repository secrets
          python manage.py deploy \
            --database="$DATABASE_URL" \
            --cache="$REDIS_URL"
  
  deploy-development:
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/develop'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: development
    steps:
      - name: Deploy JobPost to development
        env:
          DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.DATABASE_URL }}
          API_KEY: ${{ secrets.API_KEY }}
          # Development might not have Redis secret
        run: |
          echo "🔧 Deploying JobPost to Development"
          # Development secrets or repository-level secrets
          python manage.py deploy \
            --database="$DATABASE_URL" \
            --cache="${{ secrets.REDIS_URL || 'redis://localhost:6379' }}"
      
      - name: Check JobPost secret availability
        run: |
          echo "🔍 Checking JobPost secret configuration"
          
          # Check which secrets are available
          if [ -n "${{ secrets.STRIPE_API_KEY }}" ]; then
            echo "✅ Stripe integration available"
          else
            echo "⚠️ Stripe integration not configured"
          fi
          
          if [ -n "${{ secrets.SENDGRID_API_KEY }}" ]; then
            echo "✅ Email service available"
          else
            echo "⚠️ Email service not configured"
          fi
          
          if [ -n "${{ secrets.SENTRY_DSN }}" ]; then
            echo "✅ Error tracking available"
          else
            echo "⚠️ Error tracking not configured"
          fi
    🌍 JobPost environment-specific secrets using secrets context

    strategy context

    Docs: strategy context

    Access information about the matrix execution strategy for the current job using the strategy context.

    Full description

    The strategy context contains information about the matrix execution strategy configuration for the current job. For JobPost workflows using matrix builds (testing across multiple Python versions, databases, or operating systems), the strategy context provides metadata about the matrix configuration, including the total number of jobs, current job index, and failure handling settings. This context is particularly useful for coordinating behavior across matrix jobs, implementing custom retry logic, and understanding the overall matrix execution plan.

    Syntax

    ${{ strategy.fail-fast }}        # Whether fail-fast is enabled
${{ strategy.job-index }}        # Index of current job in matrix
${{ strategy.job-total }}        # Total number of matrix jobs
${{ strategy.max-parallel }}     # Maximum parallel jobs

    Parameters description

    Strategy properties:
    • strategy.fail-fast - Boolean indicating if fail-fast is enabled (default: true)
    • strategy.job-index - Index of the current job (0-based)
    • strategy.job-total - Total number of jobs in the matrix
    • strategy.max-parallel - Maximum number of jobs that can run in parallel

    Return value

    The strategy context returns an object with numeric and boolean values describing the matrix execution strategy.

    Examples

    1. JobPost matrix build with strategy info

    name: JobPost Matrix Build

on: [push]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      max-parallel: 2
      matrix:
        python: ['3.9', '3.10', '3.11', '3.12']
        database: ['postgresql', 'mysql']
    
    steps:
      - name: Display JobPost matrix info
        run: |
          echo "🔢 JobPost Matrix Execution Info"
          echo "Job Index: ${{ strategy.job-index }}"
          echo "Total Jobs: ${{ strategy.job-total }}"
          echo "Max Parallel: ${{ strategy.max-parallel }}"
          echo "Fail Fast: ${{ strategy.fail-fast }}"
          echo ""
          echo "Current Configuration:"
          echo "Python: ${{ matrix.python }}"
          echo "Database: ${{ matrix.database }}"
      
      - name: Test JobPost
        run: |
          echo "🧪 Testing JobPost [${{ strategy.job-index }}/${{ strategy.job-total }}]"
          python${{ matrix.python }} manage.py test jobpost
    🔢 JobPost matrix execution using strategy context

    2. JobPost coordinated matrix deployment

    name: JobPost Coordinated Deploy

on: [push]

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      max-parallel: 1  # Deploy one region at a time
      matrix:
        region: ['us-east-1', 'eu-west-1', 'ap-south-1']
    
    steps:
      - name: JobPost deployment sequence
        run: |
          echo "🚀 JobPost Regional Deployment"
          echo "Region: ${{ matrix.region }}"
          echo "Deploying region ${{ strategy.job-index + 1 }} of ${{ strategy.job-total }}"
          echo "Sequential: max-parallel = ${{ strategy.max-parallel }}"
      
      - name: Wait for JobPost previous regions
        if: strategy.job-index > 0
        run: |
          echo "⏳ Waiting for previous ${{ strategy.job-index }} region(s)"
          sleep $((strategy.job-index * 30))
      
      - name: Deploy JobPost to region
        run: |
          echo "Deploying JobPost to ${{ matrix.region }}"
          python manage.py deploy --region=${{ matrix.region }}
      
      - name: Report JobPost progress
        run: |
          COMPLETED=$((strategy.job-index + 1))
          REMAINING=$((strategy.job-total - COMPLETED))
          echo "Progress: $COMPLETED/${{ strategy.job-total }} regions deployed"
          echo "Remaining: $REMAINING regions"
    🌍 JobPost coordinated deployment using strategy context

    3. JobPost fail-fast configuration

    name: JobPost Fail Fast Test

on: [pull_request]

jobs:
  quick-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      fail-fast: true  # Stop on first failure
      matrix:
        test-suite: ['unit', 'integration', 'e2e']
    
    steps:
      - name: Run JobPost test suite
        run: |
          echo "🧪 JobPost ${{ matrix.test-suite }} tests"
          echo "Fail-fast: ${{ strategy.fail-fast }}"
          
          if [ "${{ strategy.fail-fast }}" == "true" ]; then
            echo "⚡ Fast failure mode enabled"
            echo "All jobs will stop if this fails"
          fi
          
          python manage.py test jobpost.tests.${{ matrix.test-suite }}
  
  comprehensive-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false  # Run all tests regardless
      matrix:
        python: ['3.9', '3.10', '3.11', '3.12']
    
    steps:
      - name: Run JobPost comprehensive tests
        run: |
          echo "🔍 JobPost comprehensive test (Python ${{ matrix.python }})"
          echo "Fail-fast: ${{ strategy.fail-fast }}"
          
          if [ "${{ strategy.fail-fast }}" == "false" ]; then
            echo "🛡️ Resilient mode: all matrix jobs will complete"
          fi
          
          python${{ matrix.python }} manage.py test jobpost
    ⚡ JobPost fail-fast strategy using strategy context

    4. JobPost parallel execution control

    name: JobPost Parallel Control

on: [push]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      max-parallel: 3
      matrix:
        component: ['api', 'worker', 'scheduler', 'frontend', 'admin', 'websocket']
    
    steps:
      - name: JobPost component build info
        run: |
