on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
      - staging

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: ${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && 'production' || 'staging' }}
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup SSH key
        env:
          SSH_PRIVATE_KEY: ${{ secrets.SSH_PRIVATE_KEY }}
          SSH_KNOWN_HOSTS: ${{ secrets.SSH_KNOWN_HOSTS }}
        run: |
          echo "🔐 Setting up SSH authentication"
          
          # Create SSH directory
          mkdir -p ~/.ssh
          chmod 700 ~/.ssh
          
          # Add private key
          echo "$SSH_PRIVATE_KEY" > ~/.ssh/deploy_key
          chmod 600 ~/.ssh/deploy_key
          
          # Add known hosts
          echo "$SSH_KNOWN_HOSTS" > ~/.ssh/known_hosts
          chmod 644 ~/.ssh/known_hosts
          
          echo "✅ SSH configured"
      
      - name: Deploy JobPost application
        env:
          DEPLOY_SERVER: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_SERVER }}
          DEPLOY_USER: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_USER }}
          DEPLOY_PATH: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_PATH }}
        run: |
          ENV="${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && 'production' || 'staging' }}"
          echo "🚀 Deploying JobPost to $ENV"
          
          # Sync files
          rsync -avz \
            --delete \
            --exclude='*.pyc' \
            --exclude='__pycache__' \
            --exclude='.git' \
            --exclude='env/' \
            --exclude='staticfiles/' \
            -e "ssh -i ~/.ssh/deploy_key -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no" \
            ./ $DEPLOY_USER@$DEPLOY_SERVER:$DEPLOY_PATH/
          
          echo "✅ Files synchronized"
      
      - name: Run remote JobPost commands
        env:
          DEPLOY_SERVER: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_SERVER }}
          DEPLOY_USER: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_USER }}
          DEPLOY_PATH: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_PATH }}
          DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.DATABASE_URL }}
        run: |
          echo "⚙️ Running JobPost deployment commands"
          
          ssh -i ~/.ssh/deploy_key \
            -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no \
            $DEPLOY_USER@$DEPLOY_SERVER << 'ENDSSH'
            
            cd ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_PATH }}
            
            # Activate virtual environment
            source env/bin/activate
            
            # Install dependencies
            pip install -r requirements.txt
            
            # Run migrations
            export DATABASE_URL="${{ secrets.DATABASE_URL }}"
            python manage.py migrate --noinput
            
            # Collect static files
            python manage.py collectstatic --noinput
            
            # Restart JobPost
            sudo systemctl restart jobpost
            
ENDSSH
          
          echo "✅ Remote commands executed"
      
      - name: Verify JobPost deployment
        env:
          APP_URL: ${{ secrets.APP_URL }}
          HEALTH_CHECK_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HEALTH_CHECK_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          echo "🔍 Verifying JobPost deployment"
          
          # Wait for application to start
          sleep 15
          
          # Health check
          RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "%{http_code}" \
            -H "Authorization: Bearer $HEALTH_CHECK_TOKEN" \
            $APP_URL/health)
          
          HTTP_CODE="${RESPONSE: -3}"
          
          if [[ "$HTTP_CODE" == "200" ]]; then
            echo "✅ JobPost is healthy"
          else
            echo "❌ Health check failed: HTTP $HTTP_CODE"
            exit 1
          fi
      
      - name: Cleanup SSH credentials
        if: always()
        run: |
          echo "🧹 Cleaning up SSH credentials"
          rm -f ~/.ssh/deploy_key
          rm -f ~/.ssh/known_hosts
    🔐 JobPost secure SSH deployment using environment secrets

    4. JobPost Docker deployment with registry credentials

    name: Deploy JobPost Docker

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]

jobs:
  build-and-deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: ${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && 'production' || 'staging' }}
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Login to Docker Registry
        env:
          DOCKER_REGISTRY: ${{ secrets.DOCKER_REGISTRY }}
          DOCKER_USERNAME: ${{ secrets.DOCKER_USERNAME }}
          DOCKER_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.DOCKER_PASSWORD }}
        run: |
          echo "🔐 Logging into Docker registry"
          
          echo "$DOCKER_PASSWORD" | docker login $DOCKER_REGISTRY \
            -u $DOCKER_USERNAME \
            --password-stdin
          
          echo "✅ Docker login successful"
      
      - name: Build JobPost Docker image
        env:
          DOCKER_REGISTRY: ${{ secrets.DOCKER_REGISTRY }}
          IMAGE_NAME: ${{ secrets.IMAGE_NAME }}
          ENVIRONMENT: ${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && 'production' || 'staging' }}
        run: |
          echo "🏗️ Building JobPost Docker image"
          
          TAG="$DOCKER_REGISTRY/$IMAGE_NAME:$ENVIRONMENT-${{ github.sha }}"
          LATEST="$DOCKER_REGISTRY/$IMAGE_NAME:$ENVIRONMENT-latest"
          
          docker build \
            --build-arg ENVIRONMENT=$ENVIRONMENT \
            --build-arg BUILD_DATE=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") \
            --build-arg VCS_REF=${{ github.sha }} \
            -t $TAG \
            -t $LATEST \
            -f Dockerfile \
            .
          
          echo "✅ Docker image built: $TAG"
      
      - name: Push JobPost image to registry
        env:
          DOCKER_REGISTRY: ${{ secrets.DOCKER_REGISTRY }}
          IMAGE_NAME: ${{ secrets.IMAGE_NAME }}
          ENVIRONMENT: ${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && 'production' || 'staging' }}
        run: |
          echo "📤 Pushing JobPost image to registry"
          
          TAG="$DOCKER_REGISTRY/$IMAGE_NAME:$ENVIRONMENT-${{ github.sha }}"
          LATEST="$DOCKER_REGISTRY/$IMAGE_NAME:$ENVIRONMENT-latest"
          
          docker push $TAG
          docker push $LATEST
          
          echo "✅ Image pushed successfully"
      
      - name: Deploy JobPost container
        env:
          DEPLOY_SERVER: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_SERVER }}
          DEPLOY_USER: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_USER }}
          SSH_PRIVATE_KEY: ${{ secrets.SSH_PRIVATE_KEY }}
          DOCKER_REGISTRY: ${{ secrets.DOCKER_REGISTRY }}
          IMAGE_NAME: ${{ secrets.IMAGE_NAME }}
          ENVIRONMENT: ${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && 'production' || 'staging' }}
          DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.DATABASE_URL }}
          SECRET_KEY: ${{ secrets.SECRET_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "🚀 Deploying JobPost container"
          
          # Setup SSH
          mkdir -p ~/.ssh
          echo "$SSH_PRIVATE_KEY" > ~/.ssh/deploy_key
          chmod 600 ~/.ssh/deploy_key
          
          # Deploy via SSH
          IMAGE="$DOCKER_REGISTRY/$IMAGE_NAME:$ENVIRONMENT-${{ github.sha }}"
          
          ssh -i ~/.ssh/deploy_key \
            -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no \
            $DEPLOY_USER@$DEPLOY_SERVER << ENDSSH
            
            # Pull new image
            docker pull $IMAGE
            
            # Stop old container
            docker stop jobpost-$ENVIRONMENT || true
            docker rm jobpost-$ENVIRONMENT || true
            
            # Run new container
            docker run -d \
              --name jobpost-$ENVIRONMENT \
              --restart unless-stopped \
              -p 8000:8000 \
              -e DATABASE_URL="${{ secrets.DATABASE_URL }}" \
              -e SECRET_KEY="${{ secrets.SECRET_KEY }}" \
              -e DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=anihpj.settings.$ENVIRONMENT \
              $IMAGE
            
ENDSSH
          
          echo "✅ JobPost container deployed"
      
      - name: Docker deployment verification
        env:
          APP_URL: ${{ secrets.APP_URL }}
        run: |
          echo "🔍 Verifying JobPost container"
          
          sleep 10
          
          # Health check
          curl -f $APP_URL/health || exit 1
          
          echo "✅ JobPost container is healthy"
    🐳 JobPost Docker deployment using environment-specific registry credentials

    5. JobPost secret rotation workflow

    name: Rotate JobPost Secrets

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      environment:
        description: 'Environment for secret rotation'
        required: true
        type: choice
        options:
          - production
          - staging
      secret_type:
        description: 'Type of secret to rotate'
        required: true
        type: choice
        options:
          - database
          - api_keys
          - ssh_keys
          - all

jobs:
  rotate-secrets:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: ${{ inputs.environment }}
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Rotate database credentials
        if: inputs.secret_type == 'database' || inputs.secret_type == 'all'
        env:
          DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.DATABASE_URL }}
          CURRENT_DB_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.DATABASE_PASSWORD }}
          VAULT_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.VAULT_TOKEN }}
          VAULT_URL: ${{ secrets.VAULT_URL }}
        run: |
          echo "🔄 Rotating JobPost database credentials"
          
          # Generate new password
          NEW_PASSWORD=$(openssl rand -base64 32)
          
          # Update database password
          psql "$DATABASE_URL" -c "
            ALTER USER jobpost_user PASSWORD '$NEW_PASSWORD';
          "
          
          # Store in Vault
          curl -X POST \
            -H "X-Vault-Token: $VAULT_TOKEN" \
            -d "{\"password\": \"$NEW_PASSWORD\"}" \
            $VAULT_URL/v1/secret/data/${{ inputs.environment }}/database
          
          echo "✅ Database credentials rotated"
      
      - name: Rotate API keys
        if: inputs.secret_type == 'api_keys' || inputs.secret_type == 'all'
        env:
          SENDGRID_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.SENDGRID_API_KEY }}
          STRIPE_SECRET_KEY: ${{ secrets.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY }}
          VAULT_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.VAULT_TOKEN }}
          VAULT_URL: ${{ secrets.VAULT_URL }}
        run: |
          echo "🔄 Rotating JobPost API keys"
          
          # Note: Actual rotation would use provider APIs
          echo "Rotating SendGrid API key..."
          # NEW_SENDGRID_KEY=$(curl -X POST https://api.sendgrid.com/v3/api_keys ...)
          
          echo "Rotating Stripe API key..."
          # NEW_STRIPE_KEY=$(curl -X POST https://api.stripe.com/v1/keys ...)
          
          echo "✅ API keys rotation initiated"
      
      - name: Rotate SSH keys
        if: inputs.secret_type == 'ssh_keys' || inputs.secret_type == 'all'
        env:
          DEPLOY_SERVER: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_SERVER }}
          DEPLOY_USER: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_USER }}
          SSH_PRIVATE_KEY: ${{ secrets.SSH_PRIVATE_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "🔄 Rotating JobPost SSH keys"
          
          # Generate new SSH key pair
          ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f ~/.ssh/new_deploy_key -N "" -C "jobpost-${{ inputs.environment }}"
          
          # Add new key to server
          mkdir -p ~/.ssh
          echo "$SSH_PRIVATE_KEY" > ~/.ssh/old_deploy_key
          chmod 600 ~/.ssh/old_deploy_key
          
          ssh -i ~/.ssh/old_deploy_key \
            -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no \
            $DEPLOY_USER@$DEPLOY_SERVER \
            "cat >> ~/.ssh/authorized_keys" \
            < ~/.ssh/new_deploy_key.pub
          
          echo "✅ New SSH key added to server"
          echo "⚠️  Update GitHub secret SSH_PRIVATE_KEY with new key"
          echo "⚠️  Remove old key from server after verification"
      
      - name: Verify JobPost still works
        env:
          APP_URL: ${{ secrets.APP_URL }}
        run: |
          echo "🔍 Verifying JobPost after secret rotation"
          
          # Test application
          curl -f $APP_URL/health || {
            echo "❌ Health check failed after rotation"
            exit 1
          }
          
          echo "✅ JobPost is healthy after secret rotation"
      
      - name: Secret rotation summary
        run: |
          echo "📋 JobPost Secret Rotation Summary"
          echo "===================================="
          echo "Environment: ${{ inputs.environment }}"
          echo "Secret Type: ${{ inputs.secret_type }}"
          echo "Rotated by: ${{ github.actor }}"
          echo "Time: $(date)"
          echo ""
          echo "⚠️  Next Steps:"
          echo "1. Update GitHub environment secrets with new values"
          echo "2. Verify all services are functioning"
          echo "3. Remove old credentials after verification period"
    🔄 JobPost secret rotation workflow using environment secrets

    Environment variables

    Docs: Environment variables for deployments

    Variables stored in an environment are only available to workflow jobs that reference the environment.

    Full description

    Environment variables in the context of deployments allow you to configure environment-specific settings that are not sensitive (unlike secrets). These variables can be used to store environment URLs, configuration flags, deployment targets, and other non-secret configuration data. For the JobPost Django application, environment variables are perfect for storing deployment settings like server hostnames, API endpoints, feature flags, debug modes, and environment-specific configuration that varies between production, staging, and development environments. Environment variables complement environment secrets by providing non-sensitive configuration that can be more easily viewed and managed.

    Official Environment Variables Documentation

    What are Environment Variables?

    Variables stored in an environment are only available to workflow jobs that reference the environment. These variables are only accessible using the vars context.

    For more information, see Store information in variables.
    📝 Availability Note:
    Environment variables are available for all public repositories. For users on GitHub Pro or GitHub Team plans, environment variables are also available for private repositories.

    Key Differences: Environment Variables vs Environment Secrets

    Feature Environment Variables (vars) Environment Secrets (secrets)
    Context Access vars context secrets context
    Visibility Visible in workflow logs Masked in workflow logs
    Use Case Non-sensitive configuration (URLs, flags, settings) Sensitive credentials (passwords, tokens, keys)
    Approval Gate Available immediately when environment referenced Available after required reviewer approval
    Plan Availability (Public Repos) All plans (Free, Pro, Team, Enterprise) All plans (Free, Pro, Team, Enterprise)
    Plan Availability (Private Repos) Pro, Team, Enterprise plans only Free plan: Not available

    JobPost Environment Variables Examples

    1. JobPost multi-environment deployment configuration

    name: Deploy JobPost with Environment Config

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      environment:
        description: 'Target environment'
        required: true
        type: choice
        options:
          - production
          - staging
          - development

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: ${{ inputs.environment }}
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Display environment configuration
        run: |
          echo "🔧 JobPost Environment Configuration"
          echo "====================================="
          echo "Environment: ${{ inputs.environment }}"
          echo "Server URL: ${{ vars.SERVER_URL }}"
          echo "API Endpoint: ${{ vars.API_ENDPOINT }}"
          echo "Debug Mode: ${{ vars.DEBUG_MODE }}"
          echo "Log Level: ${{ vars.LOG_LEVEL }}"
          echo "Max Workers: ${{ vars.MAX_WORKERS }}"
          echo "Cache Backend: ${{ vars.CACHE_BACKEND }}"
          echo "Static URL: ${{ vars.STATIC_URL }}"
          echo "Media URL: ${{ vars.MEDIA_URL }}"
      
      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
      
      - name: Configure JobPost settings
        env:
          ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.environment }}
          SERVER_URL: ${{ vars.SERVER_URL }}
          DEBUG_MODE: ${{ vars.DEBUG_MODE }}
          LOG_LEVEL: ${{ vars.LOG_LEVEL }}
          STATIC_URL: ${{ vars.STATIC_URL }}
          MEDIA_URL: ${{ vars.MEDIA_URL }}
          CACHE_BACKEND: ${{ vars.CACHE_BACKEND }}
          MAX_WORKERS: ${{ vars.MAX_WORKERS }}
        run: |
          echo "⚙️ Generating JobPost environment settings"
          
          cat > anihpj/settings/environment.py << EOF
import os

# Environment configuration
ENVIRONMENT = '$ENVIRONMENT'
DEBUG = $DEBUG_MODE
LOG_LEVEL = '$LOG_LEVEL'

# Server configuration
ALLOWED_HOSTS = ['$SERVER_URL']
CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS = ['https://$SERVER_URL']

# Static files
STATIC_URL = '$STATIC_URL'
MEDIA_URL = '$MEDIA_URL'

# Cache configuration
CACHES = {
    'default': {
        'BACKEND': '$CACHE_BACKEND',
    }
}

# Worker configuration
CELERY_WORKER_CONCURRENCY = $MAX_WORKERS

# Logging configuration
LOGGING = {
    'version': 1,
    'disable_existing_loggers': False,
    'handlers': {
        'console': {
            'class': 'logging.StreamHandler',
        },
    },
    'root': {
        'handlers': ['console'],
        'level': '$LOG_LEVEL',
    },
}
EOF
          
          echo "✅ Environment settings generated"
      
      - name: Install JobPost dependencies
        run: |
          pip install -r requirements.txt
      
      - name: Deploy JobPost application
        env:
          DEPLOY_TARGET: ${{ vars.DEPLOY_TARGET }}
          DEPLOY_METHOD: ${{ vars.DEPLOY_METHOD }}
          SERVER_URL: ${{ vars.SERVER_URL }}
          DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.DATABASE_URL }}
        run: |
          echo "🚀 Deploying JobPost to $DEPLOY_TARGET"
          
          if [[ "$DEPLOY_METHOD" == "docker" ]]; then
            echo "Deploying via Docker..."
            docker build -t jobpost:${{ inputs.environment }} .
            docker push ${{ vars.DOCKER_REGISTRY }}/jobpost:${{ inputs.environment }}
          elif [[ "$DEPLOY_METHOD" == "ansible" ]]; then
            echo "Deploying via Ansible..."
            ansible-playbook -i inventory/${{ inputs.environment }} deploy.yml
          elif [[ "$DEPLOY_METHOD" == "ssh" ]]; then
            echo "Deploying via SSH..."
            rsync -avz ./ deploy@$SERVER_URL:/var/www/jobpost/
          fi
          
          echo "✅ JobPost deployed to $SERVER_URL"
      
      - name: Verify deployment
        env:
          SERVER_URL: ${{ vars.SERVER_URL }}
          HEALTH_CHECK_ENDPOINT: ${{ vars.HEALTH_CHECK_ENDPOINT }}
        run: |
          echo "🔍 Verifying JobPost deployment"
          
          sleep 5
          
          # Health check
          curl -f https://$SERVER_URL$HEALTH_CHECK_ENDPOINT || exit 1
          
          echo "✅ JobPost is healthy at https://$SERVER_URL"
    🔧 JobPost deployment using environment-specific configuration variables

    2. JobPost feature flag deployment

    name: Deploy JobPost with Feature Flags

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: ${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && 'production' || 'staging' }}
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Configure JobPost features
        env:
          # Feature flags from environment variables
          ENABLE_ASYNC_VIEWS: ${{ vars.ENABLE_ASYNC_VIEWS }}
          ENABLE_CACHING: ${{ vars.ENABLE_CACHING }}
          ENABLE_CELERY: ${{ vars.ENABLE_CELERY }}
          ENABLE_WEBSOCKETS: ${{ vars.ENABLE_WEBSOCKETS }}
          ENABLE_ELASTICSEARCH: ${{ vars.ENABLE_ELASTICSEARCH }}
          ENABLE_API_RATE_LIMITING: ${{ vars.ENABLE_API_RATE_LIMITING }}
          ENABLE_EMAIL_NOTIFICATIONS: ${{ vars.ENABLE_EMAIL_NOTIFICATIONS }}
          ENABLE_SOCIAL_AUTH: ${{ vars.ENABLE_SOCIAL_AUTH }}
        run: |
          echo "🎛️ JobPost Feature Configuration"
          echo "================================"
          echo "Async Views: $ENABLE_ASYNC_VIEWS"
          echo "Caching: $ENABLE_CACHING"
          echo "Celery: $ENABLE_CELERY"
          echo "WebSockets: $ENABLE_WEBSOCKETS"
          echo "Elasticsearch: $ENABLE_ELASTICSEARCH"
          echo "Rate Limiting: $ENABLE_API_RATE_LIMITING"
          echo "Email Notifications: $ENABLE_EMAIL_NOTIFICATIONS"
          echo "Social Auth: $ENABLE_SOCIAL_AUTH"
          
          # Generate feature flags configuration
          cat > jobpost/feature_flags.py << EOF
# JobPost Feature Flags

FEATURES = {
    'async_views': $ENABLE_ASYNC_VIEWS,
    'caching': $ENABLE_CACHING,
    'celery': $ENABLE_CELERY,
    'websockets': $ENABLE_WEBSOCKETS,
    'elasticsearch': $ENABLE_ELASTICSEARCH,
    'api_rate_limiting': $ENABLE_API_RATE_LIMITING,
    'email_notifications': $ENABLE_EMAIL_NOTIFICATIONS,
    'social_auth': $ENABLE_SOCIAL_AUTH,
}

def is_enabled(feature_name):
    return FEATURES.get(feature_name, False)
EOF
          
          echo "✅ Feature flags configured"
      
      - name: Apply conditional configuration
        env:
          ENABLE_CACHING: ${{ vars.ENABLE_CACHING }}
          CACHE_TTL: ${{ vars.CACHE_TTL }}
          ENABLE_ELASTICSEARCH: ${{ vars.ENABLE_ELASTICSEARCH }}
          ELASTICSEARCH_INDEX: ${{ vars.ELASTICSEARCH_INDEX }}
        run: |
          echo "⚙️ Applying conditional JobPost configuration"
          
          # Configure caching if enabled
          if [[ "$ENABLE_CACHING" == "true" ]]; then
            echo "Configuring Redis cache..."
            cat >> anihpj/settings/cache.py << EOF
CACHES = {
    'default': {
        'BACKEND': 'django_redis.cache.RedisCache',
        'LOCATION': '${{ secrets.REDIS_URL }}',
        'OPTIONS': {
            'CLIENT_CLASS': 'django_redis.client.DefaultClient',
        },
        'TIMEOUT': $CACHE_TTL,
    }
}
EOF
          fi
          
          # Configure Elasticsearch if enabled
          if [[ "$ENABLE_ELASTICSEARCH" == "true" ]]; then
            echo "Configuring Elasticsearch..."
            cat >> anihpj/settings/search.py << EOF
ELASTICSEARCH_DSL = {
    'default': {
        'hosts': '${{ secrets.ELASTICSEARCH_URL }}'
    },
}
ELASTICSEARCH_INDEX = '$ELASTICSEARCH_INDEX'
EOF
          fi
          
          echo "✅ Conditional configuration applied"
      
      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
      
      - name: Install JobPost dependencies
        run: |
          pip install -r requirements.txt
          
          # Install optional dependencies based on features
          if [[ "${{ vars.ENABLE_CELERY }}" == "true" ]]; then
            pip install celery redis
          fi
          
          if [[ "${{ vars.ENABLE_ELASTICSEARCH }}" == "true" ]]; then
            pip install elasticsearch-dsl
          fi
      
      - name: Deploy JobPost
        env:
          SERVER_URL: ${{ vars.SERVER_URL }}
          DEPLOY_KEY: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "🚀 Deploying JobPost with configured features"
          
          # Deploy application
          ./deploy.sh ${{ vars.SERVER_URL }}
          
          echo "✅ JobPost deployed with feature flags"
    🎛️ JobPost deployment with environment-specific feature flags

    3. JobPost performance-tuned deployment

    name: Deploy JobPost with Performance Config

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  deploy-production:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: production
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Configure JobPost performance settings
        env:
          # Performance configuration from environment variables
          WORKER_PROCESSES: ${{ vars.WORKER_PROCESSES }}
          WORKER_THREADS: ${{ vars.WORKER_THREADS }}
          WORKER_TIMEOUT: ${{ vars.WORKER_TIMEOUT }}
          MAX_CONNECTIONS: ${{ vars.MAX_CONNECTIONS }}
          CONNECTION_POOL_SIZE: ${{ vars.CONNECTION_POOL_SIZE }}
          CACHE_TTL: ${{ vars.CACHE_TTL }}
          STATIC_FILE_CDN: ${{ vars.STATIC_FILE_CDN }}
          MEDIA_FILE_CDN: ${{ vars.MEDIA_FILE_CDN }}
          ENABLE_GZIP: ${{ vars.ENABLE_GZIP }}
          ENABLE_HTTP2: ${{ vars.ENABLE_HTTP2 }}
        run: |
          echo "⚡ JobPost Performance Configuration"
          echo "===================================="
          echo "Worker Processes: $WORKER_PROCESSES"
          echo "Worker Threads: $WORKER_THREADS"
          echo "Worker Timeout: $WORKER_TIMEOUT seconds"
          echo "Max Connections: $MAX_CONNECTIONS"
          echo "Connection Pool: $CONNECTION_POOL_SIZE"
          echo "Cache TTL: $CACHE_TTL seconds"
          echo "Static CDN: $STATIC_FILE_CDN"
          echo "Media CDN: $MEDIA_FILE_CDN"
          echo "GZIP: $ENABLE_GZIP"
          echo "HTTP/2: $ENABLE_HTTP2"
          
          # Generate Gunicorn configuration
          cat > gunicorn.conf.py << EOF
import multiprocessing

# Server socket
bind = '0.0.0.0:8000'
backlog = $MAX_CONNECTIONS

# Worker processes
workers = $WORKER_PROCESSES
worker_class = 'sync'
worker_connections = $CONNECTION_POOL_SIZE
threads = $WORKER_THREADS
timeout = $WORKER_TIMEOUT
keepalive = 5

# Logging
accesslog = '-'
errorlog = '-'
loglevel = 'info'

# Process naming
proc_name = 'jobpost'

# Server mechanics
daemon = False
pidfile = None
umask = 0
user = None
group = None
tmp_upload_dir = None
EOF
          
          echo "✅ Gunicorn configuration generated"
      
      - name: Configure NGINX
        env:
          SERVER_URL: ${{ vars.SERVER_URL }}
          STATIC_FILE_CDN: ${{ vars.STATIC_FILE_CDN }}
          MEDIA_FILE_CDN: ${{ vars.MEDIA_FILE_CDN }}
          ENABLE_GZIP: ${{ vars.ENABLE_GZIP }}
          ENABLE_HTTP2: ${{ vars.ENABLE_HTTP2 }}
        run: |
          echo "🔧 Generating NGINX configuration"
          
          cat > nginx.conf << EOF
upstream jobpost_app {
    server 127.0.0.1:8000;
}

server {
    listen 80;
    listen [::]:80;
EOF
          
          if [[ "$ENABLE_HTTP2" == "true" ]]; then
            cat >> nginx.conf << EOF
    listen 443 ssl http2;
    listen [::]:443 ssl http2;
EOF
          fi
          
          cat >> nginx.conf << EOF
    
    server_name $SERVER_URL;
    
    client_max_body_size 10M;
    
EOF
          
          if [[ "$ENABLE_GZIP" == "true" ]]; then
            cat >> nginx.conf << EOF
    # GZIP compression
    gzip on;
    gzip_vary on;
    gzip_min_length 1024;
    gzip_types text/plain text/css text/xml text/javascript
               application/x-javascript application/xml+rss
               application/json application/javascript;
    
EOF
          fi
          
          cat >> nginx.conf << EOF
    # Static files
    location /static/ {
        alias /var/www/jobpost/staticfiles/;
        expires 1y;
        add_header Cache-Control "public, immutable";
    }
    
    # Media files
    location /media/ {
        alias /var/www/jobpost/media/;
        expires 30d;
        add_header Cache-Control "public";
    }
    
    # Proxy to application
    location / {
        proxy_pass http://jobpost_app;
        proxy_set_header Host \$host;
        proxy_set_header X-Real-IP \$remote_addr;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For \$proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto \$scheme;
        proxy_connect_timeout ${{ vars.WORKER_TIMEOUT }}s;
        proxy_send_timeout ${{ vars.WORKER_TIMEOUT }}s;
        proxy_read_timeout ${{ vars.WORKER_TIMEOUT }}s;
    }
}
EOF
          
          echo "✅ NGINX configuration generated"
      
      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
      
      - name: Install JobPost dependencies
        run: |
          pip install -r requirements.txt
          pip install gunicorn
      
      - name: Deploy JobPost
        env:
          SERVER_URL: ${{ vars.SERVER_URL }}
          DEPLOY_KEY: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "🚀 Deploying performance-tuned JobPost"
          
          # Upload configuration files
          scp -i ~/.ssh/deploy_key \
            gunicorn.conf.py nginx.conf \
            deploy@$SERVER_URL:/tmp/
          
          # Deploy application
          ssh -i ~/.ssh/deploy_key deploy@$SERVER_URL << 'ENDSSH'
            sudo mv /tmp/nginx.conf /etc/nginx/sites-available/jobpost
            sudo mv /tmp/gunicorn.conf.py /var/www/jobpost/
            sudo systemctl restart nginx
            sudo systemctl restart jobpost
ENDSSH
          
          echo "✅ JobPost deployed with optimized configuration"
      
      - name: Performance verification
        env:
          SERVER_URL: ${{ vars.SERVER_URL }}
        run: |
          echo "📊 Running JobPost performance checks"
          
          # Check response time
          TIME=$(curl -o /dev/null -s -w '%{time_total}' https://$SERVER_URL/)
          echo "Response time: ${TIME}s"
          
          # Check HTTP/2 if enabled
          if [[ "${{ vars.ENABLE_HTTP2 }}" == "true" ]]; then
            curl -I --http2 https://$SERVER_URL/ | grep "HTTP/2"
          fi
          
          # Check GZIP if enabled
          if [[ "${{ vars.ENABLE_GZIP }}" == "true" ]]; then
            curl -H "Accept-Encoding: gzip" -I https://$SERVER_URL/ | grep "gzip"
          fi
          
          echo "✅ Performance verification complete"
    ⚡ JobPost performance-tuned deployment using environment variables

    4. JobPost environment-specific integrations

    name: Deploy JobPost with Integrations

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      environment:
        description: 'Target environment'
        required: true
        type: choice
        options:
          - production
          - staging

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: ${{ inputs.environment }}
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Configure third-party integrations
        env:
          # API endpoints (non-sensitive URLs)
          SENDGRID_API_URL: ${{ vars.SENDGRID_API_URL }}
          STRIPE_API_URL: ${{ vars.STRIPE_API_URL }}
          AWS_S3_REGION: ${{ vars.AWS_S3_REGION }}
          AWS_S3_BUCKET: ${{ vars.AWS_S3_BUCKET }}
          ELASTICSEARCH_HOST: ${{ vars.ELASTICSEARCH_HOST }}
          REDIS_HOST: ${{ vars.REDIS_HOST }}
          SENTRY_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ vars.SENTRY_ENVIRONMENT }}
          
          # Integration settings
          EMAIL_FROM_NAME: ${{ vars.EMAIL_FROM_NAME }}
          STRIPE_WEBHOOK_ENDPOINT: ${{ vars.STRIPE_WEBHOOK_ENDPOINT }}
          S3_STORAGE_CLASS: ${{ vars.S3_STORAGE_CLASS }}
          ELASTICSEARCH_INDEX_PREFIX: ${{ vars.ELASTICSEARCH_INDEX_PREFIX }}
        run: |
          echo "🔌 JobPost Integration Configuration"
          echo "===================================="
          echo "Environment: ${{ inputs.environment }}"
          echo ""
          echo "Email Service:"
          echo "  API URL: $SENDGRID_API_URL"
          echo "  From Name: $EMAIL_FROM_NAME"
          echo ""
          echo "Payment Gateway:"
          echo "  API URL: $STRIPE_API_URL"
          echo "  Webhook: $STRIPE_WEBHOOK_ENDPOINT"
          echo ""
          echo "Storage:"
          echo "  Region: $AWS_S3_REGION"
          echo "  Bucket: $AWS_S3_BUCKET"
          echo "  Storage Class: $S3_STORAGE_CLASS"
          echo ""
          echo "Search:"
          echo "  Host: $ELASTICSEARCH_HOST"
          echo "  Index Prefix: $ELASTICSEARCH_INDEX_PREFIX"
          echo ""
          echo "Cache:"
          echo "  Host: $REDIS_HOST"
          echo ""
          echo "Monitoring:"
          echo "  Environment: $SENTRY_ENVIRONMENT"
          
          # Generate integration configuration
          cat > anihpj/integrations.py << EOF
# JobPost Third-Party Integration Configuration

# Email configuration
EMAIL_BACKEND = 'sendgrid_backend.SendgridBackend'
SENDGRID_API_URL = '$SENDGRID_API_URL'
DEFAULT_FROM_EMAIL = '$EMAIL_FROM_NAME '

# Payment configuration
STRIPE_API_VERSION = '2023-10-16'
STRIPE_API_URL = '$STRIPE_API_URL'
STRIPE_WEBHOOK_ENDPOINT = '$STRIPE_WEBHOOK_ENDPOINT'

# Storage configuration
DEFAULT_FILE_STORAGE = 'storages.backends.s3boto3.S3Boto3Storage'
AWS_S3_REGION_NAME = '$AWS_S3_REGION'
AWS_STORAGE_BUCKET_NAME = '$AWS_S3_BUCKET'
AWS_S3_OBJECT_PARAMETERS = {
    'StorageClass': '$S3_STORAGE_CLASS'
}

# Search configuration
ELASTICSEARCH_DSL = {
    'default': {
        'hosts': '$ELASTICSEARCH_HOST'
    },
}
ELASTICSEARCH_INDEX_PREFIX = '$ELASTICSEARCH_INDEX_PREFIX'

# Cache configuration
CACHES = {
    'default': {
        'BACKEND': 'django_redis.cache.RedisCache',
        'LOCATION': 'redis://$REDIS_HOST:6379/1',
    }
}

# Monitoring configuration
import sentry_sdk
from sentry_sdk.integrations.django import DjangoIntegration

sentry_sdk.init(
    integrations=[DjangoIntegration()],
    environment='$SENTRY_ENVIRONMENT',
    traces_sample_rate=1.0 if '${{ inputs.environment }}' == 'staging' else 0.1,
)
EOF
          
          echo "✅ Integration configuration generated"
      
      - name: Test integrations
        env:
          SENDGRID_API_URL: ${{ vars.SENDGRID_API_URL }}
          STRIPE_API_URL: ${{ vars.STRIPE_API_URL }}
          ELASTICSEARCH_HOST: ${{ vars.ELASTICSEARCH_HOST }}
          REDIS_HOST: ${{ vars.REDIS_HOST }}
        run: |
          echo "🧪 Testing JobPost integrations"
          
          # Test SendGrid endpoint
          curl -f $SENDGRID_API_URL/v3/api_keys \
            -H "Authorization: Bearer ${{ secrets.SENDGRID_API_KEY }}" \
            > /dev/null
          echo "✅ SendGrid: Connected"
          
          # Test Stripe endpoint
          curl -f $STRIPE_API_URL/v1/account \
            -u ${{ secrets.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY }}: \
            > /dev/null
          echo "✅ Stripe: Connected"
          
          # Test Elasticsearch
          curl -f $ELASTICSEARCH_HOST/_cluster/health > /dev/null
          echo "✅ Elasticsearch: Connected"
          
          # Test Redis
          redis-cli -h $REDIS_HOST ping | grep PONG
          echo "✅ Redis: Connected"
      
      - name: Deploy JobPost
        env:
          SERVER_URL: ${{ vars.SERVER_URL }}
          DEPLOY_KEY: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "🚀 Deploying JobPost with integrations"
          
          # Deploy application with integration config
          rsync -avz \
            --include='anihpj/integrations.py' \
            -e "ssh -i ~/.ssh/deploy_key" \
            ./ deploy@${{ vars.SERVER_URL }}:/var/www/jobpost/
          
          # Restart application
          ssh -i ~/.ssh/deploy_key deploy@${{ vars.SERVER_URL }} \
            'sudo systemctl restart jobpost'
          
          echo "✅ JobPost deployed with configured integrations"
    🔌 JobPost deployment with environment-specific integration configuration

    5. JobPost blue-green deployment with environment variables

    name: JobPost Blue-Green Deployment

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      target_slot:
        description: 'Deployment slot (blue/green)'
        required: true
        type: choice
        options:
          - blue
          - green

jobs:
  deploy-slot:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: production
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Determine slot configuration
        id: config
        env:
          BLUE_SERVER: ${{ vars.BLUE_SERVER }}
          GREEN_SERVER: ${{ vars.GREEN_SERVER }}
          BLUE_PORT: ${{ vars.BLUE_PORT }}
          GREEN_PORT: ${{ vars.GREEN_PORT }}
          LOAD_BALANCER_URL: ${{ vars.LOAD_BALANCER_URL }}
        run: |
          TARGET_SLOT="${{ inputs.target_slot }}"
          
          if [[ "$TARGET_SLOT" == "blue" ]]; then
            echo "server=$BLUE_SERVER" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
            echo "port=$BLUE_PORT" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
            echo "inactive_server=$GREEN_SERVER" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
            echo "inactive_port=$GREEN_PORT" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          else
            echo "server=$GREEN_SERVER" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
            echo "port=$GREEN_PORT" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
            echo "inactive_server=$BLUE_SERVER" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
            echo "inactive_port=$BLUE_PORT" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi
          
          echo "load_balancer=$LOAD_BALANCER_URL" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          
          echo "🎯 Deploying to $TARGET_SLOT slot"
          echo "Target: ${{ steps.config.outputs.server }}:${{ steps.config.outputs.port }}"
      
      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
      
      - name: Deploy to target slot
        env:
          TARGET_SERVER: ${{ steps.config.outputs.server }}
          TARGET_PORT: ${{ steps.config.outputs.port }}
          DEPLOY_KEY: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "🚀 Deploying JobPost to ${{ inputs.target_slot }} slot"
          
          # Deploy application
          rsync -avz \
            --exclude='.git' \
            --exclude='env/' \
            -e "ssh -i ~/.ssh/deploy_key" \
            ./ deploy@$TARGET_SERVER:/var/www/jobpost-${{ inputs.target_slot }}/
          
          # Start application on target port
          ssh -i ~/.ssh/deploy_key deploy@$TARGET_SERVER << EOF
            cd /var/www/jobpost-${{ inputs.target_slot }}
            source env/bin/activate
            
            # Run migrations
            python manage.py migrate --noinput
            
            # Collect static files
            python manage.py collectstatic --noinput
            
            # Start Gunicorn on target port
            pkill -f "gunicorn.*$TARGET_PORT" || true
            gunicorn anihpj.wsgi:application \
              --bind 0.0.0.0:$TARGET_PORT \
              --workers 4 \
              --daemon
EOF
          
          echo "✅ JobPost deployed to ${{ inputs.target_slot }} slot"
      
      - name: Warm up target slot
        env:
          TARGET_SERVER: ${{ steps.config.outputs.server }}
          TARGET_PORT: ${{ steps.config.outputs.port }}
        run: |
          echo "🔥 Warming up JobPost on ${{ inputs.target_slot }} slot"
          
          # Wait for application to start
          sleep 10
          
          # Make warmup requests
          for i in {1..10}; do
            curl -s http://$TARGET_SERVER:$TARGET_PORT/ > /dev/null
            curl -s http://$TARGET_SERVER:$TARGET_PORT/api/jobs > /dev/null
            curl -s http://$TARGET_SERVER:$TARGET_PORT/api/categories > /dev/null
          done
          
          echo "✅ Warmup complete"
      
      - name: Run smoke tests
        env:
          TARGET_SERVER: ${{ steps.config.outputs.server }}
          TARGET_PORT: ${{ steps.config.outputs.port }}
        run: |
          echo "🧪 Running smoke tests on ${{ inputs.target_slot }} slot"
          
          # Health check
          curl -f http://$TARGET_SERVER:$TARGET_PORT/health || exit 1
          
          # API tests
          curl -f http://$TARGET_SERVER:$TARGET_PORT/api/jobs || exit 1
          curl -f http://$TARGET_SERVER:$TARGET_PORT/api/categories || exit 1
          
          # Check response time
          TIME=$(curl -o /dev/null -s -w '%{time_total}' http://$TARGET_SERVER:$TARGET_PORT/)
          echo "Response time: ${TIME}s"
          
          if (( $(echo "$TIME > 2" | bc -l) )); then
            echo "❌ Response time too high: ${TIME}s"
            exit 1
          fi
          
          echo "✅ Smoke tests passed"
      
      - name: Switch traffic to new slot
        env:
          LOAD_BALANCER_URL: ${{ steps.config.outputs.load_balancer }}
          TARGET_SERVER: ${{ steps.config.outputs.server }}
          TARGET_PORT: ${{ steps.config.outputs.port }}
          LB_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.LB_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "🔄 Switching traffic to ${{ inputs.target_slot }} slot"
          
          # Update load balancer configuration
          curl -X POST $LOAD_BALANCER_URL/api/update \
            -H "Authorization: Bearer $LB_API_KEY" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d '{
              "backend": "'$TARGET_SERVER:$TARGET_PORT'",
              "slot": "'${{ inputs.target_slot }}'"
            }'
          
          echo "✅ Traffic switched to ${{ inputs.target_slot }} slot"
      
      - name: Monitor new slot
        env:
          LOAD_BALANCER_URL: ${{ steps.config.outputs.load_balancer }}
        run: |
          echo "📊 Monitoring JobPost on ${{ inputs.target_slot }} slot"
          
          # Monitor for 2 minutes
          for i in {1..24}; do
            STATUS=$(curl -s $LOAD_BALANCER_URL/health | jq -r '.status')
            
            if [[ "$STATUS" != "healthy" ]]; then
              echo "⚠️ Health check warning at $(date)"
            fi
            
            sleep 5
          done
          
          echo "✅ Monitoring complete, deployment successful"
      
      - name: Deployment summary
        env:
          TARGET_SERVER: ${{ steps.config.outputs.server }}
          INACTIVE_SERVER: ${{ steps.config.outputs.inactive_server }}
        run: |
          echo "📋 JobPost Blue-Green Deployment Summary"
          echo "========================================"
          echo "Active Slot: ${{ inputs.target_slot }}"
          echo "Active Server: $TARGET_SERVER"
          echo "Inactive Slot: ${{ inputs.target_slot == 'blue' && 'green' || 'blue' }}"
          echo "Inactive Server: $INACTIVE_SERVER"
          echo "Deployed by: ${{ github.actor }}"
          echo "Version: ${{ github.sha }}"
          echo ""
          echo "✅ Blue-green deployment complete"
          echo "💡 To rollback, deploy to the inactive slot and switch traffic"
    🔄 JobPost blue-green deployment using environment configuration variables

    cache action usage

    Docs: Caching dependencies

    The cache action will attempt a sequence of restore operations to find and restore caches.

    Full description

    The actions/cache action allows you to cache dependencies and build outputs to improve workflow execution time. By caching dependencies, you can avoid downloading and installing the same packages on every workflow run. For the JobPost Django application, caching is essential for storing pip packages, node_modules, Python virtual environments, database fixtures, and compiled assets. The cache action significantly reduces workflow run time, especially for jobs that install many dependencies, making CI/CD pipelines faster and more efficient.

    Official Cache Action Documentation

    Cache Restore Sequence

    The cache action will attempt the following sequence when restoring a cache:

    1. First, it searches for an exact match to your provided key.
    2. If no exact match is found, it will search for partial matches of the key.
    3. If there is still no match found, and you've provided restore-keys, these keys will be checked sequentially for partial matches. For more information, see Cache key matching.

    If there is an exact match to the provided key, this is considered a cache hit. If no cache exactly matches the provided key, this is considered a cache miss. On a cache miss, the action automatically creates a new cache if the job completes successfully. The new cache will use the key you provided and contains the files you specify in path. For more information about how this is handled, see Cache hits and misses.
    📝 Important:
    You cannot change the contents of an existing cache. Instead, you can create a new cache with a new key.

    Input parameters for the cache action

    key: Required

    The key created when saving a cache and the key used to search for a cache. It can be any combination of variables, context values, static strings, and functions.

    Maximum length: 512 characters
    Keys longer than the maximum length will cause the action to fail.

    path: Required

    The path(s) on the runner to cache or restore.

    You can specify a single path, or you can add multiple paths on separate lines. For example:

    - name: Cache Gradle packages
  uses: actions/cache@v4
  with:
    path: |
      ~/.gradle/caches
      ~/.gradle/wrapper
    You can specify either directories or single files, and glob patterns are supported.

    You can specify absolute paths, or paths relative to the workspace directory.

    restore-keys: Optional

    A string containing alternative restore keys, with each restore key placed on a new line. If no cache hit occurs for key, these restore keys are used sequentially in the order provided to find and restore a cache. For example:

    restore-keys: |
  npm-feature-${{ hashFiles('package-lock.json') }}
  npm-feature-
  npm-

    enableCrossOsArchive: Optional

    A boolean value that when enabled, allows Windows runners to save or restore caches independent of the operating system the cache was created on. If this parameter is not set, it defaults to false. For more information, see Cross OS cache in the Actions Cache documentation.
    ⚠️ Security Warning:

    We recommend that you don't store any sensitive information, such as access tokens or login credentials, in files in the cache path. Anyone with read access can create a pull request on a repository and access the contents of a cache. Additionally, forks of a repository can create pull requests on the base branch and access caches on the base branch.

    Output parameters for the cache action

    cache-hit: A boolean value to indicate an exact match was found for the key.

    Cache hits and misses

    Scenario Description
    Cache Hit When key exactly matches an existing cache, it's called a cache hit, and the action restores the cached files to the path directory.
    Cache Miss When key doesn't match an existing cache, it's called a cache miss, and a new cache is automatically created if the job completes successfully.
    When a cache miss occurs:

    The action also searches your specified restore-keys for any matches:

    • If you provide restore-keys, the cache action sequentially searches for any caches that match the list of restore-keys.
    • When there is an exact match, the action restores the files in the cache to the path directory.
    • If there are no exact matches, the action searches for partial matches of the restore keys. When the action finds a partial match, the most recent cache is restored to the path directory.
    • The cache action completes and the next step in the job runs.
    • If the job completes successfully, the action automatically creates a new cache with the contents of the path directory.

    For a more detailed explanation of the cache matching process, see Cache key matching.

    Example using the cache action

    Official Example from GitHub

    This example creates a new cache when the packages in package-lock.json file change, or when the runner's operating system changes. The cache key uses contexts and expressions to generate a key that includes the runner's operating system and a SHA-256 hash of the package-lock.json file. 
    name: Caching with npm
on: push
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5

      - name: Cache node modules
        id: cache-npm
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        env:
          cache-name: cache-node-modules
        with:
          # npm cache files are stored in `~/.npm` on Linux/macOS
          path: ~/.npm
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-build-${{ env.cache-name }}-${{ hashFiles('**/package-lock.json') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-build-${{ env.cache-name }}-
            ${{ runner.os }}-build-
            ${{ runner.os }}-

      - if: ${{ steps.cache-npm.outputs.cache-hit != 'true' }}
        name: List the state of node modules
        continue-on-error: true
        run: npm list

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build

      - name: Test
        run: npm test

    Using contexts to create cache keys

    A cache key can include any of the contexts, functions, literals, and operators supported by GitHub Actions. For more information, see Contexts reference and Evaluate expressions in workflows and actions.

    Using expressions to create a key allows you to automatically create a new cache when dependencies change.

    For example, you can create a key using an expression that calculates the hash of an npm package-lock.json file. So, when the dependencies that make up the package-lock.json file change, the cache key changes and a new cache is automatically created.

    npm-${{ hashFiles('package-lock.json') }}
    GitHub evaluates the expression hash "package-lock.json" to derive the final key.

    npm-d5ea0750

    Using the output of the cache action

    You can use the output of the cache action to do something based on whether a cache hit or miss occurred. When an exact match is found for a cache for the specified key, the cache-hit output is set to true.

    In the example workflow above, there is a step that lists the state of the Node modules if a cache miss occurred:

    - if: ${{ steps.cache-npm.outputs.cache-hit != 'true' }}
  name: List the state of node modules
  continue-on-error: true
  run: npm list

    JobPost Dependency Caching Examples

    1. JobPost Python dependencies caching

    name: Test JobPost with Pip Cache

on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
      
      - name: Cache pip packages
        id: cache-pip
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/pip
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-pip-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-pip-
      
      - name: Install JobPost dependencies
        if: steps.cache-pip.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
        run: |
          echo "📦 Installing JobPost dependencies (cache miss)"
          pip install -r requirements.txt
      
      - name: Use cached dependencies
        if: steps.cache-pip.outputs.cache-hit == 'true'
        run: |
          echo "✅ Using cached JobPost dependencies"
          pip list
      
      - name: Run JobPost tests
        env:
          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.test
        run: |
          echo "🧪 Running JobPost tests"
          python manage.py test jobpost
      
      - name: Cache statistics
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost Cache Statistics"
          echo "Cache hit: ${{ steps.cache-pip.outputs.cache-hit }}"
          echo "Cache key: ${{ runner.os }}-pip-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt') }}"
          echo "Pip cache size: $(du -sh ~/.cache/pip | cut -f1)"
    ⚡ JobPost testing with pip cache for faster dependency installation

    2. JobPost virtual environment caching

    name: JobPost with Venv Cache

on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
      
      - name: Cache virtual environment
        id: cache-venv
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: env/
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-venv-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt') }}-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements-dev.txt') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-venv-
      
      - name: Create JobPost virtual environment
        if: steps.cache-venv.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
        run: |
          echo "🔨 Creating JobPost virtual environment"
          python -m venv env
          source env/bin/activate
          pip install --upgrade pip
          pip install -r requirements.txt
          pip install -r requirements-dev.txt
          echo "✅ Virtual environment created and cached"
      
      - name: Use cached virtual environment
        if: steps.cache-venv.outputs.cache-hit == 'true'
        run: |
          echo "✅ Using cached JobPost virtual environment"
          source env/bin/activate
          python --version
          pip list
      
      - name: Run JobPost migrations
        run: |
          source env/bin/activate
          export DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=anihpj.settings.test
          python manage.py migrate
      
      - name: Run JobPost tests
        run: |
          source env/bin/activate
          export DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=anihpj.settings.test
          
          # Run tests with coverage
          coverage run --source='jobpost,anihpj' manage.py test
          coverage report
      
      - name: Cache summary
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost Virtual Environment Cache"
          echo "Cache hit: ${{ steps.cache-venv.outputs.cache-hit }}"
          echo "Venv size: $(du -sh env/ | cut -f1)"
          echo "Python packages: $(source env/bin/activate && pip list | wc -l)"
    🔨 JobPost with complete virtual environment caching for maximum speed

    3. JobPost multi-path caching (Python + Node.js)

    name: JobPost Full Stack Cache

on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
      
      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '18'
      
      - name: Cache JobPost dependencies
        id: cache-deps
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cache/pip
            ~/.npm
            node_modules
            jobpost/static/dist
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-jobpost-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt', '**/package-lock.json') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-jobpost-
      
      - name: Install Python dependencies
        if: steps.cache-deps.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
        run: |
          echo "📦 Installing JobPost Python dependencies"
          pip install -r requirements.txt
      
      - name: Install Node.js dependencies
        if: steps.cache-deps.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
        run: |
          echo "📦 Installing JobPost Node.js dependencies"
          npm ci
      
      - name: Build JobPost frontend assets
        if: steps.cache-deps.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
        run: |
          echo "🏗️ Building JobPost frontend assets"
          npm run build
          echo "✅ Assets built and cached"
      
      - name: Use cached assets
        if: steps.cache-deps.outputs.cache-hit == 'true'
        run: |
          echo "✅ Using cached JobPost dependencies and assets"
          ls -lh jobpost/static/dist/
      
      - name: Run Django migrations
        env:
          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.test
        run: |
          python manage.py migrate
      
      - name: Collect static files
        env:
          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.test
        run: |
          python manage.py collectstatic --noinput
      
      - name: Run JobPost tests
        env:
          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.test
        run: |
          # Backend tests
          python manage.py test jobpost
          
          # Frontend tests
          npm test
      
      - name: Cache summary
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost Full Stack Cache Report"
          echo "===================================="
          echo "Cache hit: ${{ steps.cache-deps.outputs.cache-hit }}"
          echo ""
          echo "Python packages:"
          echo "  Location: ~/.cache/pip"
          echo "  Size: $(du -sh ~/.cache/pip | cut -f1)"
          echo ""
          echo "Node.js packages:"
          echo "  Location: node_modules"
          echo "  Size: $(du -sh node_modules | cut -f1)"
          echo "  Packages: $(ls node_modules | wc -l)"
          echo ""
          echo "Built assets:"
          echo "  Location: jobpost/static/dist"
          echo "  Size: $(du -sh jobpost/static/dist | cut -f1)"
          echo "  Files: $(find jobpost/static/dist -type f | wc -l)"
    🎨 JobPost full stack caching for Python and Node.js dependencies

    4. JobPost database fixtures caching

    name: JobPost Test with Fixtures Cache

on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    services:
      postgres:
        image: postgres:15
        env:
          POSTGRES_DB: jobpost_test
          POSTGRES_USER: jobpost
          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: password
        options: >-
          --health-cmd pg_isready
          --health-interval 10s
          --health-timeout 5s
          --health-retries 5
        ports:
          - 5432:5432
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
      
      - name: Cache pip dependencies
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/pip
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-pip-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-pip-
      
      - name: Install JobPost dependencies
        run: |
          pip install -r requirements.txt
          pip install psycopg2-binary
      
      - name: Generate fixtures cache key
        id: fixtures-key
        run: |
          # Create hash of fixture files
          FIXTURES_HASH=$(find fixtures/ -type f -exec sha256sum {} \; | sha256sum | cut -d' ' -f1)
          echo "hash=$FIXTURES_HASH" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "📋 Fixtures hash: $FIXTURES_HASH"
      
      - name: Cache JobPost test fixtures
        id: cache-fixtures
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            fixtures/
            test_database_dump.json
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-fixtures-${{ steps.fixtures-key.outputs.hash }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-fixtures-
      
      - name: Run JobPost migrations
        env:
          DATABASE_URL: postgresql://jobpost:password@localhost:5432/jobpost_test
          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.test
        run: |
          echo "🔄 Running JobPost migrations"
          python manage.py migrate
      
      - name: Load JobPost fixtures
        if: steps.cache-fixtures.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
        env:
          DATABASE_URL: postgresql://jobpost:password@localhost:5432/jobpost_test
          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.test
        run: |
          echo "📦 Loading JobPost test fixtures"
          
          # Load fixtures
          python manage.py loaddata \
            fixtures/categories.json \
            fixtures/users.json \
            fixtures/jobs.json \
            fixtures/applications.json
          
          # Create database dump for cache
          python manage.py dumpdata \
            --natural-foreign \
            --natural-primary \
            -e contenttypes \
            -e auth.Permission \
            > test_database_dump.json
          
          echo "✅ Fixtures loaded and cached"
      
      - name: Restore JobPost fixtures from cache
        if: steps.cache-fixtures.outputs.cache-hit == 'true'
        env:
          DATABASE_URL: postgresql://jobpost:password@localhost:5432/jobpost_test
          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.test
        run: |
          echo "✅ Restoring JobPost fixtures from cache"
          python manage.py loaddata test_database_dump.json
      
      - name: Run JobPost tests
        env:
          DATABASE_URL: postgresql://jobpost:password@localhost:5432/jobpost_test
          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.test
        run: |
          echo "🧪 Running JobPost tests with fixtures"
          
          # Run tests
          python manage.py test jobpost
          
          # Verify fixture data
          python manage.py shell << EOF
from jobpost.models import Job, Category, Application
print(f"✅ Categories: {Category.objects.count()}")
print(f"✅ Jobs: {Job.objects.count()}")
print(f"✅ Applications: {Application.objects.count()}")
EOF
      
      - name: Fixtures cache report
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost Fixtures Cache Report"
          echo "================================="
          echo "Cache hit: ${{ steps.cache-fixtures.outputs.cache-hit }}"
          echo "Fixtures hash: ${{ steps.fixtures-key.outputs.hash }}"
          echo "Dump size: $(du -sh test_database_dump.json | cut -f1 || echo 'N/A')"
          echo "Fixture files: $(find fixtures/ -type f -name '*.json' | wc -l)"
    📦 JobPost test fixtures caching for faster test database setup

    5. JobPost Docker layer caching

    name: JobPost Docker Build with Cache

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Set up Docker Buildx
        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3
      
      - name: Cache Docker layers
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: /tmp/.buildx-cache
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-buildx-${{ github.sha }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-buildx-
      
      - name: Login to Docker Hub
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          username: ${{ secrets.DOCKER_USERNAME }}
          password: ${{ secrets.DOCKER_PASSWORD }}
      
      - name: Build JobPost Docker image
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: .
          push: false
          tags: jobpost:latest
          cache-from: type=local,src=/tmp/.buildx-cache
          cache-to: type=local,dest=/tmp/.buildx-cache-new,mode=max
          build-args: |
            PYTHON_VERSION=3.11
            DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=anihpj.settings.production
      
      - name: Test JobPost Docker image
        run: |
          echo "🧪 Testing JobPost Docker image"
          
          # Run container
          docker run -d \
            --name jobpost-test \
            -e DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=anihpj.settings.test \
            -e DATABASE_URL=sqlite:///db.sqlite3 \
            jobpost:latest
          
          # Wait for startup
          sleep 5
          
          # Test health endpoint
          docker exec jobpost-test python manage.py check
          
          # Run tests
          docker exec jobpost-test python manage.py test jobpost
          
          # Cleanup
          docker stop jobpost-test
          docker rm jobpost-test
          
          echo "✅ Docker image tests passed"
      
      - name: Push JobPost image
        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
        run: |
          echo "📤 Pushing JobPost Docker image"
          docker tag jobpost:latest ${{ secrets.DOCKER_USERNAME }}/jobpost:latest
          docker push ${{ secrets.DOCKER_USERNAME }}/jobpost:latest
      
      - name: Move Docker cache
        run: |
          # Move cache to avoid growing cache size
          rm -rf /tmp/.buildx-cache
          mv /tmp/.buildx-cache-new /tmp/.buildx-cache
      
      - name: Docker cache report
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost Docker Cache Report"
          echo "==============================="
          echo "Cache location: /tmp/.buildx-cache"
          echo "Cache size: $(du -sh /tmp/.buildx-cache | cut -f1)"
          echo "Image size: $(docker images jobpost:latest --format '{{.Size}}')"
          echo "Layers: $(docker history jobpost:latest | wc -l)"
    🐳 JobPost Docker build with layer caching for faster image builds

    Cache key matching

    Docs: Cache key matching

    The cache action searches for cache hits using a specific sequence for key and restore-keys.

    Full description

    Cache key matching determines which cached data is restored during workflow execution. GitHub Actions first attempts to find an exact match for the cache key. If no exact match is found, it searches through restore-keys in order, looking for the most recent cache with a matching prefix. For the JobPost Django application, proper cache key strategies ensure that caches are reused when dependencies haven't changed, but new caches are created when requirements.txt is updated. Understanding key matching helps optimize cache hit rates, reduce cache storage usage, and ensure workflows use the most appropriate cached data.

    Official Cache Key Matching Documentation

    Cache Search Sequence

    The cache action first searches for cache hits for key and the cache version in the branch containing the workflow run. If there is no hit, it searches for prefix-matches for key, and if there is still no hit, it searches for restore-keys and the version. If there are still no hits in the current branch, the cache action retries the same steps on the default branch. Please note that the scope restrictions apply during the search. For more information, see Restrictions for accessing a cache.
    📝 Cache Version:

    Cache version is a way to stamp a cache with metadata of the path and the compression tool used while creating the cache. This ensures that the consuming workflow run uniquely matches a cache it can actually decompress and use. For more information, see Cache Version in the Actions Cache documentation.

    restore-keys

    restore-keys allows you to specify a list of alternate restore keys to use when there is a cache miss on key. You can create multiple restore keys ordered from the most specific to least specific. The cache action searches the restore-keys in sequential order. When a key doesn't match directly, the action searches for keys prefixed with the restore key. If there are multiple partial matches for a restore key, the action returns the most recently created cache.

    Example using multiple restore keys

    restore-keys: |
  npm-feature-${{ hashFiles('package-lock.json') }}
  npm-feature-
  npm-
    The runner evaluates the expressions, which resolve to these restore-keys:

    restore-keys: |
  npm-feature-d5ea0750
  npm-feature-
  npm-
    The restore key npm-feature- matches any key that starts with the string npm-feature-. For example, both of the keys npm-feature-fd3052de and npm-feature-a9b253ff match the restore key. The cache with the most recent creation date would be used. The keys in this example are searched in the following order:

    1. npm-feature-d5ea0750 matches a specific hash.
    2. npm-feature- matches cache keys prefixed with npm-feature-.
    3. npm- matches any keys prefixed with npm-.

    Example of search priority

    key:
  npm-feature-d5ea0750
restore-keys: |
  npm-feature-
  npm-
    Search Order Example

    For example, if a pull request contains a feature branch and targets the default branch (main), the action searches for key and restore-keys in the following order:

    1. Key npm-feature-d5ea0750 in the feature branch
    2. Key npm-feature- in the feature branch
    3. Key npm- in the feature branch
    4. Key npm-feature-d5ea0750 in the main branch
    5. Key npm-feature- in the main branch
    6. Key npm- in the main branch

    JobPost Cache Key Matching Examples

    1. JobPost requirements-based cache key

    name: JobPost Smart Cache Key

on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
      
      - name: Display cache key strategy
        run: |
          echo "📋 JobPost Cache Key Strategy"
          echo "=============================="
          echo "Primary key pattern:"
          echo "  Format: OS-pip-HASH(requirements.txt)"
          echo "  Example: Linux-pip-abc123def456..."
          echo ""
          echo "Restore keys (in order):"
          echo "  1. Linux-pip- (any pip cache for Linux)"
          echo "  2. Linux- (any Linux cache)"
          echo ""
          echo "Key components:"
          echo "  OS: ${{ runner.os }}"
          echo "  Files: requirements.txt"
          echo "  Hash: ${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt') }}"
      
      - name: Cache JobPost dependencies
        id: cache
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/pip
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-pip-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-pip-
            ${{ runner.os }}-
      
      - name: Analyze cache result
        run: |
          echo "🔍 Cache Match Analysis"
          echo "======================="
          
          if [[ "${{ steps.cache.outputs.cache-hit }}" == "true" ]]; then
            echo "✅ EXACT MATCH FOUND"
            echo "The cache key matched exactly, indicating:"
            echo "  • requirements.txt has not changed"
            echo "  • No need to reinstall dependencies"
            echo "  • Maximum time savings"
          else
            echo "⚠️  EXACT MATCH NOT FOUND"
            echo "Falling back to restore-keys:"
            echo "  • Checking for '${{ runner.os }}-pip-' prefix"
            echo "  • Will restore most recent pip cache"
            echo "  • New cache will be created after installation"
          fi
          
          echo ""
          echo "Cache key used: ${{ runner.os }}-pip-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt') }}"
      
      - name: Install JobPost dependencies
        if: steps.cache.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
        run: |
          echo "📦 Installing JobPost dependencies"
          pip install -r requirements.txt
          echo "✅ Dependencies installed, cache will be saved"
      
      - name: Verify cached dependencies
        run: |
          echo "📋 JobPost Installed Packages"
          pip list | grep -E "(Django|celery|redis|psycopg2)"
      
      - name: Run JobPost tests
        env:
          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.test
        run: |
          python manage.py test jobpost
    🔑 JobPost cache with requirements.txt-based key for intelligent caching

    2. JobPost multi-file cache key

    name: JobPost Multi-File Cache Key

on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
      
      - name: Generate multi-file hash
        id: hash
        run: |
          echo "🔐 Generating JobPost multi-file hash"
          
          # Calculate individual hashes
          REQ_HASH=$(sha256sum requirements.txt | cut -d' ' -f1 | cut -c1-8)
          DEV_HASH=$(sha256sum requirements-dev.txt | cut -d' ' -f1 | cut -c1-8)
          SETUP_HASH=$(sha256sum setup.py | cut -d' ' -f1 | cut -c1-8)
          
          echo "requirements.txt hash: $REQ_HASH"
          echo "requirements-dev.txt hash: $DEV_HASH"
          echo "setup.py hash: $SETUP_HASH"
          
          # Combined hash
          COMBINED=$(echo "$REQ_HASH$DEV_HASH$SETUP_HASH" | sha256sum | cut -d' ' -f1 | cut -c1-16)
          echo "combined=$COMBINED" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          
          echo "Combined hash: $COMBINED"
      
      - name: Cache JobPost dependencies
        id: cache
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cache/pip
            env/
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-jobpost-${{ hashFiles('requirements.txt', 'requirements-dev.txt', 'setup.py') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-jobpost-${{ hashFiles('requirements.txt') }}
            ${{ runner.os }}-jobpost-
            ${{ runner.os }}-
      
      - name: Cache matching analysis
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost Multi-File Cache Analysis"
          echo "====================================="
          echo "Exact key: ${{ runner.os }}-jobpost-${{ hashFiles('requirements.txt', 'requirements-dev.txt', 'setup.py') }}"
          echo ""
          echo "Restore key hierarchy:"
          echo "  1. Same OS + production deps (requirements.txt)"
          echo "  2. Same OS + any jobpost cache"
          echo "  3. Same OS + any cache"
          echo ""
          echo "Cache hit: ${{ steps.cache.outputs.cache-hit }}"
          echo ""
          
          if [[ "${{ steps.cache.outputs.cache-hit }}" == "true" ]]; then
            echo "✅ All dependency files unchanged"
          else
            echo "⚠️  At least one dependency file changed:"
            echo "  • requirements.txt"
            echo "  • requirements-dev.txt"
            echo "  • setup.py"
            echo "New cache will be created with updated dependencies"
          fi
      
      - name: Install JobPost dependencies
        if: steps.cache.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
        run: |
          echo "📦 Installing JobPost dependencies"
          python -m venv env
          source env/bin/activate
          pip install --upgrade pip
          pip install -r requirements.txt
          pip install -r requirements-dev.txt
          pip install -e .
      
      - name: Run JobPost tests
        run: |
          source env/bin/activate
          export DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=anihpj.settings.test
          
          # Run tests with dev dependencies
          pytest jobpost/tests/ \
            --cov=jobpost \
            --cov-report=html \
            -v
    🗂️ JobPost cache with multi-file key for comprehensive dependency tracking

    3. JobPost version-specific cache key

    name: JobPost Version-Specific Cache

on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest]
        python-version: ['3.10', '3.11', '3.12']
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Python ${{ matrix.python-version }}
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: ${{ matrix.python-version }}
      
      - name: Generate version-specific cache key
        id: cache-key
        run: |
          echo "🔑 Generating JobPost version-specific cache key"
          
          PYTHON_VERSION="${{ matrix.python-version }}"
          OS="${{ runner.os }}"
          ARCH="${{ runner.arch }}"
          DEPS_HASH="${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt') }}"
          
          echo "python=$PYTHON_VERSION" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "os=$OS" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "arch=$ARCH" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          
          echo "Cache key components:"
          echo "  OS: $OS"
          echo "  Architecture: $ARCH"
          echo "  Python: $PYTHON_VERSION"
          echo "  Dependencies hash: ${DEPS_HASH:0:8}..."
        shell: bash
      
      - name: Cache JobPost dependencies
        id: cache
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/pip
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ runner.arch }}-py${{ matrix.python-version }}-pip-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-${{ runner.arch }}-py${{ matrix.python-version }}-pip-
            ${{ runner.os }}-${{ runner.arch }}-py${{ matrix.python-version }}-
            ${{ runner.os }}-${{ runner.arch }}-
        
      - name: Cache key breakdown
        run: |
          echo "📋 JobPost Cache Key Strategy"
          echo "=============================="
          echo ""
          echo "Full key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ runner.arch }}-py${{ matrix.python-version }}-pip-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt') }}"
          echo ""
          echo "Restore keys (most specific to least specific):"
          echo "  1. Same OS + arch + Python version + pip packages"
          echo "  2. Same OS + arch + Python version (any packages)"
          echo "  3. Same OS + arch (any Python version)"
          echo ""
          echo "This ensures:"
          echo "  ✓ Caches are Python version specific"
          echo "  ✓ Platform-specific packages cached correctly"
          echo "  ✓ Architecture differences handled (x64 vs ARM)"
        shell: bash
      
      - name: Install JobPost dependencies
        if: steps.cache.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
        run: |
          echo "📦 Installing JobPost for Python ${{ matrix.python-version }}"
          pip install -r requirements.txt
        shell: bash
      
      - name: Run JobPost tests
        env:
          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.test
        run: |
          python manage.py test jobpost
        shell: bash
      
      - name: Matrix cache summary
        run: |
          echo "📊 Cache Summary for Matrix Job"
          echo "================================"
          echo "OS: ${{ runner.os }}"
          echo "Architecture: ${{ runner.arch }}"
          echo "Python: ${{ matrix.python-version }}"
          echo "Cache hit: ${{ steps.cache.outputs.cache-hit }}"
        shell: bash
    🎯 JobPost version-specific cache keys for matrix builds

    4. JobPost time-based cache key rotation

    name: JobPost Time-Based Cache Rotation

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 0 * * 0'  # Weekly on Sunday
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
      
      - name: Generate time-based cache key
        id: date
        run: |
          # Get current week number for weekly rotation
          WEEK=$(date +%U)
          YEAR=$(date +%Y)
          
          echo "week=$WEEK" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "year=$YEAR" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          
          echo "📅 JobPost Cache Rotation Schedule"
          echo "==================================="
          echo "Year: $YEAR"
          echo "Week: $WEEK"
          echo "Cache will rotate weekly to prevent stale dependencies"
      
      - name: Cache JobPost dependencies with rotation
        id: cache
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/pip
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-pip-${{ steps.date.outputs.year }}-week${{ steps.date.outputs.week }}-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-pip-${{ steps.date.outputs.year }}-week${{ steps.date.outputs.week }}-
            ${{ runner.os }}-pip-${{ steps.date.outputs.year }}-
            ${{ runner.os }}-pip-
      
      - name: Explain rotation strategy
        run: |
          echo "🔄 JobPost Cache Rotation Strategy"
          echo "==================================="
          echo ""
          echo "Key components:"
          echo "  • Year: ${{ steps.date.outputs.year }}"
          echo "  • Week: ${{ steps.date.outputs.week }}"
          echo "  • Requirements hash: ${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt') }}"
          echo ""
          echo "Rotation benefits:"
          echo "  ✓ Weekly cache refresh ensures up-to-date dependencies"
          echo "  ✓ Security updates included automatically"
          echo "  ✓ Prevents indefinite cache reuse of outdated packages"
          echo "  ✓ Old caches automatically evicted after 7 days"
          echo ""
          echo "Current cache key:"
