          echo "  ${{ runner.os }}-pip-${{ steps.date.outputs.year }}-week${{ steps.date.outputs.week }}-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt') }}"
      
      - name: Install JobPost dependencies
        if: steps.cache.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
        run: |
          echo "📦 Installing fresh JobPost dependencies"
          pip install --upgrade pip
          pip install -r requirements.txt
          
          echo ""
          echo "✅ Dependencies installed with latest updates"
      
      - name: Verify dependency versions
        run: |
          echo "📋 JobPost Dependency Versions"
          echo "==============================="
          pip list | grep -E "(Django|celery|redis|psycopg2|gunicorn)" || true
          
          echo ""
          echo "🔐 Security check:"
          pip list --outdated | head -10 || echo "No outdated packages (first 10)"
      
      - name: Run JobPost tests
        env:
          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.test
        run: |
          python manage.py test jobpost
      
      - name: Cache rotation summary
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost Cache Rotation Report"
          echo "================================="
          echo "Cache hit: ${{ steps.cache.outputs.cache-hit }}"
          echo "Rotation period: Weekly"
          echo "Current week: ${{ steps.date.outputs.week }}"
          echo "Next rotation: Week ${{ steps.date.outputs.week + 1 }}"
    🔄 JobPost time-based cache rotation for automatic dependency updates

    5. JobPost branch-aware cache key strategy

    name: JobPost Branch-Aware Cache

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop, 'feature/**']
  pull_request:
    branches: [main, develop]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
      
      - name: Determine branch strategy
        id: branch
        run: |
          # Get branch name
          if [[ "${{ github.event_name }}" == "pull_request" ]]; then
            BRANCH="${{ github.head_ref }}"
            BASE="${{ github.base_ref }}"
          else
            BRANCH="${{ github.ref_name }}"
            BASE="main"
          fi
          
          echo "branch=$BRANCH" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "base=$BASE" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          
          # Determine branch type
          if [[ "$BRANCH" == "main" ]]; then
            BRANCH_TYPE="main"
          elif [[ "$BRANCH" == "develop" ]]; then
            BRANCH_TYPE="develop"
          elif [[ "$BRANCH" == feature/* ]]; then
            BRANCH_TYPE="feature"
          else
            BRANCH_TYPE="other"
          fi
          
          echo "type=$BRANCH_TYPE" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          
          echo "🌿 JobPost Branch Strategy"
          echo "=========================="
          echo "Current branch: $BRANCH"
          echo "Base branch: $BASE"
          echo "Branch type: $BRANCH_TYPE"
      
      - name: Cache JobPost dependencies
        id: cache
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/pip
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ steps.branch.outputs.type }}-pip-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-${{ steps.branch.outputs.type }}-pip-
            ${{ runner.os }}-${{ steps.branch.outputs.base }}-pip-
            ${{ runner.os }}-main-pip-
            ${{ runner.os }}-pip-
      
      - name: Explain branch-aware caching
        run: |
          echo "📋 JobPost Branch-Aware Cache Strategy"
          echo "======================================="
          echo ""
          echo "Current context:"
          echo "  Branch: ${{ steps.branch.outputs.branch }}"
          echo "  Type: ${{ steps.branch.outputs.type }}"
          echo "  Base: ${{ steps.branch.outputs.base }}"
          echo ""
          echo "Cache key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ steps.branch.outputs.type }}-pip-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt') }}"
          echo ""
          echo "Restore key hierarchy:"
          echo "  1. Same branch type + same dependencies"
          echo "  2. Same branch type + any dependencies"
          echo "  3. Base branch dependencies"
          echo "  4. Main branch dependencies (most stable)"
          echo "  5. Any pip cache (fallback)"
          echo ""
          echo "Benefits:"
          echo "  ✓ Feature branches inherit from main"
          echo "  ✓ Develop has separate cache from main"
          echo "  ✓ Pull requests use base branch cache"
          echo "  ✓ Prevents cache pollution across workflows"
      
      - name: Install JobPost dependencies
        if: steps.cache.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
        run: |
          echo "📦 Installing JobPost dependencies for ${{ steps.branch.outputs.branch }}"
          pip install -r requirements.txt
      
      - name: Run branch-specific JobPost tests
        env:
          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.test
        run: |
          echo "🧪 Running JobPost tests for ${{ steps.branch.outputs.type }} branch"
          
          if [[ "${{ steps.branch.outputs.type }}" == "main" ]]; then
            # Full test suite for main
            python manage.py test jobpost --parallel
          elif [[ "${{ steps.branch.outputs.type }}" == "develop" ]]; then
            # Full tests with coverage for develop
            coverage run manage.py test jobpost
            coverage report
          else
            # Fast tests for feature branches
            python manage.py test jobpost --failfast
          fi
      
      - name: Branch cache report
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost Branch Cache Report"
          echo "==============================="
          echo "Branch: ${{ steps.branch.outputs.branch }}"
          echo "Type: ${{ steps.branch.outputs.type }}"
          echo "Cache hit: ${{ steps.cache.outputs.cache-hit }}"
          echo "Cache key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ steps.branch.outputs.type }}-pip-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt') }}"
    🌿 JobPost branch-aware cache strategy for optimal cache reuse

    setup-* actions for specific package managers

    Docs: Setup actions with built-in caching

    Use setup actions with built-in caching support for popular package managers.

    Full description

    Many official setup actions include built-in caching support for their respective package managers, making dependency caching simpler and more reliable. These actions automatically handle cache key generation, path configuration, and cache restoration without requiring manual cache action configuration. For the JobPost Django application, using setup actions with caching enabled simplifies workflows by automatically caching pip packages (Python), npm packages (Node.js), and other dependencies. The setup actions handle cache management intelligently, using lock files to generate cache keys and storing dependencies in platform-appropriate locations.

    Syntax

    # Python with pip caching
- uses: actions/setup-python@v5
  with:
    python-version: '3.11'
    cache: 'pip'
    cache-dependency-path: '**/requirements*.txt'

# Node.js with npm caching
- uses: actions/setup-node@v4
  with:
    node-version: '18'
    cache: 'npm'

# Multiple cache dependency paths
- uses: actions/setup-python@v5
  with:
    python-version: '3.11'
    cache: 'pip'
    cache-dependency-path: |
      requirements.txt
      requirements-dev.txt

    Parameters description

    Setup action caching features:
    • cache - Package manager to cache (pip, pipenv, poetry, npm, yarn, pnpm)
    • cache-dependency-path - Path pattern for dependency files (optional)
    • Automatic key generation - Uses lock files or requirements automatically
    • Platform-aware - Stores cache in OS-appropriate locations
    • Zero configuration - Works out of the box with sensible defaults
    • Restore keys - Automatically configured for optimal cache reuse

    Return value

    Setup actions with caching automatically restore cached dependencies before proceeding with the rest of the job. The cache is saved at the end of the job if it wasn't restored or if dependencies changed. Unlike manual cache action usage, setup actions don't expose cache-hit output, but provide faster and simpler workflows with less configuration.

    1. JobPost with setup-python built-in pip caching

    name: JobPost with Setup-Python Cache

on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Python with pip caching
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
          cache: 'pip'
          cache-dependency-path: 'requirements.txt'
      
      - name: Display cache information
        run: |
          echo "🎯 JobPost Setup-Python Cache"
          echo "=============================="
          echo "Python version: 3.11"
          echo "Cache enabled: pip"
          echo "Dependency file: requirements.txt"
          echo ""
          echo "Benefits:"
          echo "  ✓ Automatic cache key from requirements.txt hash"
          echo "  ✓ Automatic cache path (~/.cache/pip)"
          echo "  ✓ Automatic restore keys for partial matches"
          echo "  ✓ Zero manual configuration required"
      
      - name: Install JobPost dependencies
        run: |
          echo "📦 Installing JobPost dependencies"
          pip install -r requirements.txt
          echo "✅ Dependencies installed"
      
      - name: Verify cached dependencies
        run: |
          echo "📋 JobPost Installed Packages"
          pip list | grep -E "(Django|celery|redis|psycopg2|gunicorn)"
          
          echo ""
          echo "📊 Cache Statistics"
          echo "Pip cache location: ~/.cache/pip"
          echo "Cache size: $(du -sh ~/.cache/pip 2>/dev/null | cut -f1 || echo 'N/A')"
      
      - name: Run JobPost tests
        env:
          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.test
        run: |
          python manage.py test jobpost
      
      - name: Cache summary
        run: |
          echo "✅ JobPost workflow completed with automated pip caching"
          echo "No manual cache action configuration needed!"
    🐍 JobPost with setup-python's built-in pip caching for zero-config dependency management

    2. JobPost full stack with setup-python and setup-node caching

    name: JobPost Full Stack with Setup Caching

on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Python with pip caching
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
          cache: 'pip'
          cache-dependency-path: |
            requirements.txt
            requirements-dev.txt
      
      - name: Setup Node.js with npm caching
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '18'
          cache: 'npm'
          cache-dependency-path: 'package-lock.json'
      
      - name: Display dual cache setup
        run: |
          echo "🎨 JobPost Full Stack Caching"
          echo "=============================="
          echo ""
          echo "Python Setup:"
          echo "  Version: 3.11"
          echo "  Cache: pip"
          echo "  Files: requirements.txt, requirements-dev.txt"
          echo "  Location: ~/.cache/pip"
          echo ""
          echo "Node.js Setup:"
          echo "  Version: 18"
          echo "  Cache: npm"
          echo "  Files: package-lock.json"
          echo "  Location: ~/.npm"
          echo ""
          echo "Both caches are automatically managed by setup actions!"
      
      - name: Install JobPost Python dependencies
        run: |
          echo "📦 Installing Python dependencies"
          pip install -r requirements.txt
          pip install -r requirements-dev.txt
      
      - name: Install JobPost Node.js dependencies
        run: |
          echo "📦 Installing Node.js dependencies"
          npm ci
      
      - name: Build JobPost frontend
        run: |
          echo "🏗️ Building JobPost frontend assets"
          npm run build
      
      - name: Run Django migrations
        env:
          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.test
        run: |
          python manage.py migrate
      
      - name: Collect static files
        env:
          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.test
        run: |
          python manage.py collectstatic --noinput
      
      - name: Run JobPost backend tests
        env:
          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.test
        run: |
          echo "🧪 Running JobPost backend tests"
          pytest jobpost/tests/ --cov=jobpost
      
      - name: Run JobPost frontend tests
        run: |
          echo "🧪 Running JobPost frontend tests"
          npm test
      
      - name: Full stack cache summary
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost Full Stack Cache Report"
          echo "===================================="
          echo ""
          echo "Python dependencies:"
          echo "  Packages: $(pip list | wc -l)"
          echo "  Cache size: $(du -sh ~/.cache/pip 2>/dev/null | cut -f1 || echo 'N/A')"
          echo ""
          echo "Node.js dependencies:"
          echo "  Packages: $(ls node_modules 2>/dev/null | wc -l || echo '0')"
          echo "  Cache size: $(du -sh ~/.npm 2>/dev/null | cut -f1 || echo 'N/A')"
          echo ""
          echo "✅ Both caches automatically managed by setup actions"
    🎨 JobPost full stack with automatic Python and Node.js caching

    3. JobPost with Poetry caching

    name: JobPost with Poetry Cache

on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Python with Poetry caching
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
          cache: 'poetry'
      
      - name: Install Poetry
        run: |
          echo "📦 Installing Poetry"
          curl -sSL https://install.python-poetry.org | python3 -
          echo "$HOME/.local/bin" >> $GITHUB_PATH
      
      - name: Display Poetry cache info
        run: |
          echo "📋 JobPost Poetry Cache Configuration"
          echo "======================================"
          echo "Python version: 3.11"
          echo "Package manager: Poetry"
          echo "Lock file: poetry.lock"
          echo ""
          echo "Poetry automatically caches:"
          echo "  ✓ Virtual environment"
          echo "  ✓ Downloaded packages"
          echo "  ✓ Built wheels"
          echo ""
          poetry --version
          poetry config --list | grep cache
      
      - name: Install JobPost dependencies with Poetry
        run: |
          echo "📦 Installing JobPost dependencies via Poetry"
          poetry install --no-interaction --no-ansi
      
      - name: Display installed packages
        run: |
          echo "📋 JobPost Poetry Packages"
          poetry show --tree
      
      - name: Run JobPost tests with Poetry
        run: |
          echo "🧪 Running JobPost tests"
          poetry run python manage.py test jobpost
        env:
          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.test
      
      - name: Run JobPost linting
        run: |
          echo "🔍 Running JobPost code quality checks"
          poetry run black --check jobpost/
          poetry run flake8 jobpost/
          poetry run mypy jobpost/
      
      - name: Poetry cache summary
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost Poetry Cache Summary"
          echo "================================"
          echo "Cache location: $(poetry config cache-dir)"
          echo "Cache size: $(du -sh $(poetry config cache-dir) | cut -f1)"
          echo "Virtual env: $(poetry env info --path)"
          echo "✅ All managed automatically by setup-python action"
    📦 JobPost with Poetry dependency management and automatic caching

    4. JobPost matrix with multiple package manager caches

    name: JobPost Matrix with Setup Caches

on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest]
        python-version: ['3.10', '3.11', '3.12']
        package-manager: ['pip', 'pipenv']
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Python with package manager cache
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: ${{ matrix.python-version }}
          cache: ${{ matrix.package-manager }}
      
      - name: Display matrix cache configuration
        run: |
          echo "🎯 JobPost Matrix Cache Configuration"
          echo "======================================"
          echo "OS: ${{ matrix.os }}"
          echo "Python: ${{ matrix.python-version }}"
          echo "Package Manager: ${{ matrix.package-manager }}"
          echo ""
          echo "Cache key includes:"
          echo "  • Operating system"
          echo "  • Python version"
          echo "  • Package manager type"
          echo "  • Lock file hash"
        shell: bash
      
      - name: Install pipenv if needed
        if: matrix.package-manager == 'pipenv'
        run: |
          pip install pipenv
        shell: bash
      
      - name: Install JobPost dependencies
        run: |
          if [[ "${{ matrix.package-manager }}" == "pip" ]]; then
            echo "📦 Installing with pip"
            pip install -r requirements.txt
          elif [[ "${{ matrix.package-manager }}" == "pipenv" ]]; then
            echo "📦 Installing with pipenv"
            pipenv install --dev
          fi
        shell: bash
      
      - name: Run JobPost tests
        run: |
          if [[ "${{ matrix.package-manager }}" == "pip" ]]; then
            python manage.py test jobpost
          elif [[ "${{ matrix.package-manager }}" == "pipenv" ]]; then
            pipenv run python manage.py test jobpost
          fi
        env:
          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.test
        shell: bash
      
      - name: Matrix cache report
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost Matrix Cache Report"
          echo "==============================="
          echo "Configuration:"
          echo "  OS: ${{ runner.os }}"
          echo "  Python: ${{ matrix.python-version }}"
          echo "  Package Manager: ${{ matrix.package-manager }}"
          echo ""
          echo "✅ Cache automatically managed per matrix combination"
          echo "Each combination gets its own isolated cache"
        shell: bash
    🎯 JobPost matrix testing with automatic package manager-specific caching

    5. JobPost with yarn and pnpm caching

    name: JobPost Alternative Package Managers

on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  test-yarn:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Node.js with Yarn caching
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '18'
          cache: 'yarn'
      
      - name: Install JobPost dependencies with Yarn
        run: |
          echo "📦 Installing JobPost dependencies with Yarn"
          yarn install --frozen-lockfile
      
      - name: Build JobPost with Yarn
        run: |
          echo "🏗️ Building JobPost assets"
          yarn build
      
      - name: Test JobPost with Yarn
        run: |
          echo "🧪 Running JobPost tests"
          yarn test
      
      - name: Yarn cache summary
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost Yarn Cache"
          echo "===================="
          echo "Cache location: $(yarn cache dir)"
          echo "Cache size: $(du -sh $(yarn cache dir) | cut -f1)"
          echo "Packages: $(ls node_modules | wc -l)"
  
  test-pnpm:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Install pnpm
        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v2
        with:
          version: 8
      
      - name: Setup Node.js with pnpm caching
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '18'
          cache: 'pnpm'
      
      - name: Install JobPost dependencies with pnpm
        run: |
          echo "📦 Installing JobPost dependencies with pnpm"
          pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
      
      - name: Build JobPost with pnpm
        run: |
          echo "🏗️ Building JobPost assets"
          pnpm build
      
      - name: Test JobPost with pnpm
        run: |
          echo "🧪 Running JobPost tests"
          pnpm test
      
      - name: pnpm cache summary
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost pnpm Cache"
          echo "====================="
          echo "Cache location: $(pnpm store path)"
          echo "Packages: $(ls node_modules | wc -l)"
          echo ""
          echo "pnpm benefits:"
          echo "  ✓ Faster installations"
          echo "  ✓ Efficient disk space usage"
          echo "  ✓ Content-addressable storage"
  
  test-comparison:
    needs: [test-yarn, test-pnpm]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Package manager comparison
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost Package Manager Comparison"
          echo "======================================"
          echo ""
          echo "All package managers supported:"
          echo "  ✓ npm (default, cache: 'npm')"
          echo "  ✓ yarn (cache: 'yarn')"
          echo "  ✓ pnpm (cache: 'pnpm')"
          echo ""
          echo "Setup actions automatically handle:"
          echo "  • Cache key generation from lock files"
          echo "  • Platform-specific cache locations"
          echo "  • Restore key fallbacks"
          echo "  • Cache compression and upload"
          echo ""
          echo "✅ Choose the package manager that fits your workflow"
          echo "✅ Caching works automatically for all of them"
    ⚡ JobPost with yarn and pnpm automatic caching support

    Restrictions for accessing a cache

    Docs: Cache access restrictions

    Understand cache access restrictions and scope limitations in GitHub Actions.

    Full description

    GitHub Actions caches have specific access restrictions based on branch relationships, workflow triggers, and repository boundaries. Workflows can access caches created in the current branch, default branch (main/master), or base branches of pull requests. Caches cannot be shared across forks or between different repositories. For the JobPost Django application, understanding cache restrictions is crucial for optimizing cache usage across feature branches, pull requests, and deployment workflows. Proper cache strategies account for these restrictions to maximize cache hit rates while maintaining security boundaries.

    Syntax

    # Cache access hierarchy
# 1. Current branch cache (highest priority)
# 2. Default branch cache (fallback)
# 3. Base branch cache for PRs (fallback)

# Caches are scoped to:
# - Repository (not shared across forks)
# - Branch (with fallback rules)
# - Workflow (can be shared across workflows)

    Parameters description

    Cache access restrictions:
    • Branch scope - Caches can be accessed from current branch and default branch
    • Pull request access - PRs can access base branch and default branch caches
    • Fork restriction - Caches cannot be accessed across forks
    • Repository scope - Caches are scoped to a single repository
    • Workflow sharing - Caches can be shared between different workflows in the same repo
    • Write access - Only workflows with write permissions can create/update caches

    Return value

    When cache access is restricted, the cache action falls back to restore-keys or results in a cache miss. The workflow logs indicate whether a cache was found and which branch it came from. Understanding these restrictions helps design cache strategies that work across branch workflows and pull requests.

    1. JobPost branch hierarchy caching

    name: JobPost Branch Hierarchy Cache

on:
  push:
    branches: ['**']
  pull_request:
    branches: [main, develop]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Determine branch context
        id: branch
        run: |
          echo "📋 JobPost Branch Context"
          echo "========================="
          
          if [[ "${{ github.event_name }}" == "pull_request" ]]; then
            echo "Event: Pull Request"
            echo "Head branch: ${{ github.head_ref }}"
            echo "Base branch: ${{ github.base_ref }}"
            echo "Default branch: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}"
            echo ""
            echo "Cache access hierarchy:"
            echo "  1. Current PR branch (${{ github.head_ref }})"
            echo "  2. Base branch (${{ github.base_ref }})"
            echo "  3. Default branch (${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }})"
          else
            echo "Event: Push"
            echo "Current branch: ${{ github.ref_name }}"
            echo "Default branch: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}"
            echo ""
            echo "Cache access hierarchy:"
            echo "  1. Current branch (${{ github.ref_name }})"
            echo "  2. Default branch (${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }})"
          fi
      
      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
      
      - name: Cache JobPost dependencies with branch awareness
        id: cache
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/pip
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-pip-${{ github.ref_name }}-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-pip-${{ github.ref_name }}-
            ${{ runner.os }}-pip-${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}-
            ${{ runner.os }}-pip-
      
      - name: Explain cache access
        run: |
          echo "🔐 JobPost Cache Access Explanation"
          echo "===================================="
          echo ""
          echo "Feature branches can access:"
          echo "  ✓ Their own caches"
          echo "  ✓ Default branch (main) caches"
          echo "  ✗ Other feature branch caches"
          echo ""
          echo "Pull requests can access:"
          echo "  ✓ PR branch caches"
          echo "  ✓ Base branch caches"
          echo "  ✓ Default branch caches"
          echo "  ✗ Other PR branch caches"
          echo ""
          echo "This prevents cache pollution while enabling reuse"
      
      - name: Install JobPost dependencies
        if: steps.cache.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
        run: |
          pip install -r requirements.txt
      
      - name: Run JobPost tests
        env:
          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.test
        run: |
          python manage.py test jobpost
    🌿 JobPost caching with branch hierarchy access rules

    2. JobPost pull request cache inheritance

    name: JobPost PR Cache Strategy

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]
    branches: [main, develop]

jobs:
  test-pr:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost PR
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
      
      - name: Display PR cache context
        run: |
          echo "📋 JobPost Pull Request Cache Context"
          echo "======================================"
          echo "PR Number: #${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
          echo "PR Title: ${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}"
          echo "Head Branch: ${{ github.head_ref }}"
          echo "Base Branch: ${{ github.base_ref }}"
          echo "Head SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}"
          echo ""
          echo "Cache inheritance strategy:"
          echo "  1. Try PR head branch cache first"
          echo "  2. Fall back to base branch cache"
          echo "  3. Fall back to default branch cache"
          echo "  4. No cache if none found"
      
      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
      
      - name: Cache JobPost with PR-aware keys
        id: cache
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/pip
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-pr${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-pr${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}-
            ${{ runner.os }}-${{ github.base_ref }}-
            ${{ runner.os }}-main-
            ${{ runner.os }}-
      
      - name: Analyze cache inheritance
        run: |
          echo "🔍 JobPost PR Cache Inheritance Analysis"
          echo "========================================="
          
          if [[ "${{ steps.cache.outputs.cache-hit }}" == "true" ]]; then
            echo "✅ CACHE HIT"
            echo "Using cached dependencies"
          else
            echo "⚠️  CACHE MISS"
            echo "This PR will create a new cache entry"
          fi
          
          echo ""
          echo "PR can access caches from:"
          echo "  ✓ This PR's previous runs"
          echo "  ✓ Base branch (${{ github.base_ref }})"
          echo "  ✓ Default branch (main)"
          echo ""
          echo "PR cannot access caches from:"
          echo "  ✗ Other PRs"
          echo "  ✗ Other feature branches"
          echo "  ✗ Forked repositories"
      
      - name: Install JobPost dependencies
        if: steps.cache.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
        run: |
          echo "📦 Installing JobPost dependencies for PR #${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
          pip install -r requirements.txt
      
      - name: Run JobPost PR tests
        env:
          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.test
        run: |
          echo "🧪 Running JobPost tests for PR #${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
          python manage.py test jobpost --parallel
      
      - name: PR cache summary
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost PR Cache Summary"
          echo "============================"
          echo "PR: #${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
          echo "Cache hit: ${{ steps.cache.outputs.cache-hit }}"
          echo "Key: ${{ runner.os }}-pr${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt') }}"
    🔀 JobPost pull request caching with proper inheritance from base branch

    3. JobPost fork restriction demonstration

    name: JobPost Fork Cache Restrictions

on:
  pull_request_target:
    types: [opened, synchronize]

jobs:
  test-fork:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
      
      - name: Detect fork status
        id: fork
        run: |
          echo "🔍 JobPost Fork Detection"
          echo "========================="
          
          IS_FORK="${{ github.event.pull_request.head.repo.fork }}"
          
          echo "is_fork=$IS_FORK" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "Is fork: $IS_FORK"
          echo "Head repo: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name }}"
          echo "Base repo: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.repo.full_name }}"
          
          if [[ "$IS_FORK" == "true" ]]; then
            echo ""
            echo "⚠️  Fork detected - Cache restrictions apply:"
            echo "  ✗ Cannot access base repository's caches"
            echo "  ✓ Can create caches in fork"
            echo "  ✗ Cannot restore caches from upstream"
          fi
      
      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
      
      - name: Cache JobPost (fork-aware)
        id: cache
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/pip
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-fork-${{ github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name }}-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-fork-${{ github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name }}-
      
      - name: Explain fork cache behavior
        run: |
          echo "🔐 JobPost Fork Cache Behavior"
          echo "==============================="
          echo ""
          echo "Security considerations:"
          echo "  • Forks cannot read upstream repository caches"
          echo "  • This prevents cache poisoning attacks"
          echo "  • Each fork maintains its own cache space"
          echo ""
          echo "Performance impact:"
          echo "  • First-time contributors have cold cache"
          echo "  • Subsequent runs benefit from fork's cache"
          echo "  • Can be mitigated with setup-python cache option"
          echo ""
          
          if [[ "${{ steps.fork.outputs.is_fork }}" == "true" ]]; then
            echo "⚠️  This PR is from a fork"
            echo "Cache will be built from scratch first time"
          else
            echo "✅ This PR is from the same repository"
            echo "Can access existing caches"
          fi
      
      - name: Install JobPost dependencies
        run: |
          echo "📦 Installing JobPost dependencies"
          
          if [[ "${{ steps.fork.outputs.is_fork }}" == "true" ]]; then
            echo "Installing for fork (first run may be slower)"
          fi
          
          pip install -r requirements.txt
      
      - name: Run JobPost tests
        env:
          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.test
        run: |
          python manage.py test jobpost
    🔒 JobPost cache restrictions for fork security and isolation

    4. JobPost workflow cache sharing

    name: JobPost Workflow Cache Sharing

on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  setup-cache:
    name: Setup JobPost Cache
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      cache-key: ${{ steps.cache-key.outputs.key }}
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Generate shared cache key
        id: cache-key
        run: |
          KEY="${{ runner.os }}-jobpost-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt') }}"
          echo "key=$KEY" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "📋 Generated cache key: $KEY"
      
      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
      
      - name: Cache JobPost dependencies
        id: cache
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/pip
          key: ${{ steps.cache-key.outputs.key }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-jobpost-
      
      - name: Install JobPost dependencies
        if: steps.cache.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
        run: |
          echo "📦 Installing JobPost dependencies (shared cache)"
          pip install -r requirements.txt
      
      - name: Cache sharing info
        run: |
          echo "🔄 JobPost Cache Sharing"
          echo "========================"
          echo "This cache can be accessed by:"
          echo "  ✓ Other jobs in this workflow"
          echo "  ✓ Other workflows in this repository"
          echo "  ✓ Future runs on this branch"
          echo "  ✓ Pull requests to this branch"
  
  test-backend:
    name: Test JobPost Backend
    needs: setup-cache
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
      
      - name: Restore shared cache
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/pip
          key: ${{ needs.setup-cache.outputs.cache-key }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-jobpost-
      
      - name: Verify shared cache
        run: |
          echo "✅ Using cache from setup-cache job"
          echo "Cache key: ${{ needs.setup-cache.outputs.cache-key }}"
          pip list | grep Django
      
      - name: Run JobPost backend tests
        env:
          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.test
        run: |
          python manage.py test jobpost
  
  lint-backend:
    name: Lint JobPost Backend
    needs: setup-cache
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
      
      - name: Restore shared cache
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/pip
          key: ${{ needs.setup-cache.outputs.cache-key }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-jobpost-
      
      - name: Verify shared cache
        run: |
          echo "✅ Using same cache as other jobs"
          pip list | grep -E "(black|flake8|mypy)"
      
      - name: Run JobPost linting
        run: |
          black --check jobpost/
          flake8 jobpost/
  
  summary:
    name: Cache Sharing Summary
    needs: [setup-cache, test-backend, lint-backend]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Workflow cache sharing summary
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost Workflow Cache Sharing Summary"
          echo "=========================================="
          echo ""
          echo "Cache key: ${{ needs.setup-cache.outputs.cache-key }}"
          echo ""
          echo "Jobs that shared this cache:"
          echo "  1. setup-cache (created/restored cache)"
          echo "  2. test-backend (restored cache)"
          echo "  3. lint-backend (restored cache)"
          echo ""
          echo "Benefits:"
          echo "  ✓ Single cache creation per workflow run"
          echo "  ✓ Multiple jobs reuse the same cache"
          echo "  ✓ Faster overall workflow execution"
          echo "  ✓ Reduced cache storage usage"
    🔄 JobPost cache sharing across multiple workflow jobs

    5. JobPost default branch fallback strategy

    name: JobPost Default Branch Fallback

on:
  push:
    branches: ['**']

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Analyze branch relationship
        id: branch
        run: |
          CURRENT_BRANCH="${{ github.ref_name }}"
          DEFAULT_BRANCH="${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}"
          
          echo "current=$CURRENT_BRANCH" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "default=$DEFAULT_BRANCH" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          
          echo "📋 JobPost Branch Analysis"
          echo "=========================="
          echo "Current branch: $CURRENT_BRANCH"
          echo "Default branch: $DEFAULT_BRANCH"
          echo ""
          
          if [[ "$CURRENT_BRANCH" == "$DEFAULT_BRANCH" ]]; then
            echo "Status: Running on default branch"
            echo "Cache: Will be accessible to all branches"
          else
            echo "Status: Running on feature/topic branch"
            echo "Cache: Can fall back to default branch"
          fi
      
      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
      
      - name: Cache with default branch fallback
        id: cache
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/pip
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ steps.branch.outputs.current }}-pip-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-${{ steps.branch.outputs.current }}-pip-
            ${{ runner.os }}-${{ steps.branch.outputs.default }}-pip-
            ${{ runner.os }}-pip-
      
      - name: Explain fallback strategy
        run: |
          echo "🎯 JobPost Default Branch Fallback Strategy"
          echo "============================================"
          echo ""
          echo "Cache key hierarchy:"
          echo "  1. Current branch exact match"
          echo "  2. Current branch prefix match"
          echo "  3. Default branch (${{ steps.branch.outputs.default }}) match"
          echo "  4. Any branch match (last resort)"
          echo ""
          echo "Why this matters:"
          echo "  • New branches start with default branch cache"
          echo "  • Reduces cold cache scenarios"
          echo "  • Faster first-time builds on feature branches"
          echo "  • Default branch cache is stable and well-tested"
          echo ""
          
          if [[ "${{ steps.branch.outputs.current }}" != "${{ steps.branch.outputs.default }}" ]]; then
            echo "✅ This branch can fall back to '${{ steps.branch.outputs.default }}' cache"
          else
            echo "✅ This is the default branch - cache benefits all others"
          fi
      
      - name: Install JobPost dependencies
        if: steps.cache.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
        run: |
          echo "📦 Installing JobPost dependencies"
          pip install -r requirements.txt
      
      - name: Run JobPost tests
        env:
          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.test
        run: |
          python manage.py test jobpost
      
      - name: Fallback cache summary
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost Cache Fallback Summary"
          echo "=================================="
          echo "Current branch: ${{ steps.branch.outputs.current }}"
          echo "Default branch: ${{ steps.branch.outputs.default }}"
          echo "Cache hit: ${{ steps.cache.outputs.cache-hit }}"
          echo ""
          
          if [[ "${{ steps.cache.outputs.cache-hit }}" == "true" ]]; then
            echo "✅ Cache restored successfully"
          else
            echo "⚠️  No cache found, created new cache"
            echo "This cache will help future runs on this branch"
            
            if [[ "${{ steps.branch.outputs.current }}" == "${{ steps.branch.outputs.default }}" ]]; then
              echo "As default branch, this cache will help all branches"
            fi
          fi
    🎯 JobPost default branch fallback strategy for optimal cache reuse

    Usage limits and eviction policy

    Docs: Cache usage limits

    Understand cache storage limits, eviction policies, and management strategies in GitHub Actions.

    Full description

    GitHub Actions provides 10GB of cache storage per repository, with automatic eviction of caches that haven't been accessed for 7 days. When the cache storage limit is reached, GitHub evicts the least recently used caches. Individual cache entries are limited to 10GB in size. For the JobPost Django application, understanding these limits is essential for designing efficient caching strategies that balance performance with storage constraints. Proper cache management includes monitoring cache usage, implementing cache rotation strategies, and prioritizing high-value caches like Python dependencies and database fixtures.

    Syntax

    # Cache limits:
# - Total repository storage: 10GB
# - Individual cache size: 10GB max
# - Eviction: 7 days without access
# - Policy: Least Recently Used (LRU)

# Best practices:
# - Monitor cache usage regularly
# - Use specific cache keys to avoid bloat
# - Implement cache rotation for large files
# - Prioritize frequently-used caches

    Parameters description

    Cache limits and policies:
    • Repository limit - Maximum 10GB total cache storage per repository
    • Individual cache limit - Each cache entry can be up to 10GB
    • Eviction policy - Caches unused for 7 days are automatically evicted
    • LRU eviction - When storage is full, least recently used caches are removed first
    • Access tracking - Any cache read or write resets the 7-day timer
    • No manual deletion - Caches are automatically managed (can delete via REST API)

    Return value

    When cache limits are reached, older caches are silently evicted to make room for new ones. The cache action logs indicate when caches are saved or restored but not when they're evicted. Monitoring cache usage helps identify when optimization is needed to stay within limits.

    1. JobPost cache size monitoring

    name: JobPost Cache Size Monitoring

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 0 * * 0'  # Weekly on Sunday
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  monitor-cache:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Get repository cache information
        id: cache-info
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost Cache Storage Analysis"
          echo "==================================="
          echo ""
          echo "Repository cache limits:"
          echo "  • Total storage: 10GB per repository"
          echo "  • Individual cache: 10GB maximum"
          echo "  • Eviction period: 7 days without access"
          echo "  • Eviction policy: Least Recently Used (LRU)"
          echo ""
          
          gh api \
            -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" \
            -H "X-GitHub-Api-Version: 2022-11-28" \
            /repos/${{ github.repository }}/actions/caches \
            > cache-list.json
          
          TOTAL_SIZE=$(jq '[.actions_caches[].size_in_bytes] | add // 0' cache-list.json)
          CACHE_COUNT=$(jq '.actions_caches | length' cache-list.json)
          TOTAL_GB=$(echo "scale=2; $TOTAL_SIZE / 1024 / 1024 / 1024" | bc)
          LIMIT_GB="10.00"
          USAGE_PERCENT=$(echo "scale=2; ($TOTAL_GB / $LIMIT_GB) * 100" | bc)
          
          echo "total_size=$TOTAL_SIZE" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "total_gb=$TOTAL_GB" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "cache_count=$CACHE_COUNT" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "usage_percent=$USAGE_PERCENT" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          
          echo "Current cache usage:"
          echo "  • Total caches: $CACHE_COUNT"
          echo "  • Total size: ${TOTAL_GB}GB / ${LIMIT_GB}GB"
          echo "  • Usage: ${USAGE_PERCENT}%"
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
      
      - name: List largest JobPost caches
        run: |
          echo "🔍 Largest JobPost Caches"
          echo "========================="
          echo ""
          
          jq -r '.actions_caches | sort_by(.size_in_bytes) | reverse | .[] | 
            "Key: \(.key)\nSize: \(.size_in_bytes / 1024 / 1024 | floor)MB\nBranch: \(.ref)\nLast accessed: \(.last_accessed_at)\n"' \
            cache-list.json | head -50
      
      - name: Cache cleanup recommendations
        run: |
          USAGE=${{ steps.cache-info.outputs.usage_percent }}
          
          echo "💡 JobPost Cache Optimization Recommendations"
          echo "=============================================="
          echo ""
          
          if (( $(echo "$USAGE > 80" | bc -l) )); then
            echo "⚠️  WARNING: Cache usage above 80%"
            echo ""
            echo "Recommended actions:"
            echo "  1. Review and remove outdated cache keys"
            echo "  2. Use more specific cache keys to avoid duplication"
            echo "  3. Consider cache rotation strategies"
            echo "  4. Prioritize critical caches (Python deps, fixtures)"
            echo "  5. Delete caches for stale branches"
          elif (( $(echo "$USAGE > 60" | bc -l) )); then
            echo "ℹ️  INFO: Cache usage moderate (60-80%)"
            echo ""
            echo "Monitor regularly and consider:"
            echo "  1. Review cache key specificity"
            echo "  2. Verify all caches are necessary"
            echo "  3. Plan for future growth"
          else
            echo "✅ Cache usage healthy (below 60%)"
            echo ""
            echo "Current strategy is working well"
            echo "Continue monitoring usage trends"
          fi
      
      - name: Generate cache report
        run: |
          cat > cache-report.md << 'EOF'
          # JobPost Cache Storage Report
          
          ## Summary
          - **Total Caches**: ${{ steps.cache-info.outputs.cache_count }}
          - **Storage Used**: ${{ steps.cache-info.outputs.total_gb }}GB / 10GB
          - **Usage Percentage**: ${{ steps.cache-info.outputs.usage_percent }}%
          - **Generated**: $(date -u +"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S UTC")
          
          ## Cache Limits
          - Repository limit: 10GB total
          - Individual cache limit: 10GB
          - Eviction period: 7 days without access
          - Eviction policy: LRU (Least Recently Used)
          
          ## Top Caches by Size
          $(jq -r '.actions_caches | sort_by(.size_in_bytes) | reverse | .[0:5] | .[] | 
            "- **\(.key)**: \(.size_in_bytes / 1024 / 1024 | floor)MB (Branch: \(.ref))"' cache-list.json)
          EOF
          
          cat cache-report.md
          echo ""
          echo "✅ Cache report generated"
    📊 JobPost cache storage monitoring and optimization recommendations

    2. JobPost cache rotation strategy

    name: JobPost Cache Rotation

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]

jobs:
  build-with-rotation:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Calculate rotation key
        id: rotation
        run: |
          # Rotate cache weekly to prevent indefinite growth
          WEEK_NUMBER=$(date +%U)
          ROTATION_KEY="week-$WEEK_NUMBER"
          
          echo "rotation=$ROTATION_KEY" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          
          echo "📅 JobPost Cache Rotation Strategy"
          echo "==================================="
          echo "Week number: $WEEK_NUMBER"
          echo "Rotation key: $ROTATION_KEY"
          echo ""
          echo "Benefits:"
          echo "  • Automatically creates new cache weekly"
          echo "  • Old caches evicted after 7 days"
          echo "  • Prevents cache bloat"
          echo "  • Ensures fresh dependencies periodically"
      
      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
      
      - name: Cache JobPost with rotation
        id: cache
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cache/pip
            env/
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-jobpost-${{ steps.rotation.outputs.rotation }}-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-jobpost-${{ steps.rotation.outputs.rotation }}-
            ${{ runner.os }}-jobpost-
      
      - name: Explain rotation benefits
        run: |
          echo "🔄 Cache Rotation Benefits for JobPost"
          echo "======================================="
          echo ""
          echo "Without rotation:"
          echo "  ✗ Caches can accumulate indefinitely"
          echo "  ✗ May hit 10GB repository limit"
          echo "  ✗ Older caches waste storage"
          echo ""
          echo "With weekly rotation:"
          echo "  ✓ Cache refreshed every week"
          echo "  ✓ Old caches auto-evicted after 7 days"
          echo "  ✓ Maximum 2 weeks of caches exist"
          echo "  ✓ Storage usage remains bounded"
          echo ""
          echo "For JobPost application:"
          echo "  • Python deps cache: ~300MB"
          echo "  • With rotation: max 600MB (2 weeks)"
          echo "  • Without rotation: could grow unbounded"
      
      - name: Setup JobPost environment
        if: steps.cache.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
        run: |
          python -m venv env
          source env/bin/activate
          pip install -r requirements.txt
      
      - name: Run JobPost tests
        run: |
          source env/bin/activate
          python manage.py test jobpost
    🔄 JobPost weekly cache rotation to prevent storage bloat

    3. JobPost cache size optimization

    name: JobPost Cache Size Optimization

on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  optimized-caching:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
      
      - name: Cache optimization analysis
        run: |
          echo "🔍 JobPost Cache Size Optimization"
          echo "==================================="
          echo ""
          echo "Strategy: Cache only what's needed"
          echo ""
          echo "❌ DON'T cache (wastes space):"
          echo "  • Source code (use git checkout)"
          echo "  • Build artifacts (regenerate each time)"
          echo "  • Logs and temp files"
          echo "  • .pyc files (auto-generated)"
          echo "  • __pycache__ directories"
          echo ""
          echo "✅ DO cache (high value):"
          echo "  • pip download cache (~300MB for JobPost)"
          echo "  • Virtual environment packages"
          echo "  • Pre-commit hooks"
          echo "  • Database fixtures (~50MB)"
          echo "  • Node modules for frontend"
      
      - name: Cache pip packages only (optimized)
        id: cache-pip
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/pip
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-pip-optimized-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-pip-optimized-
      
      - name: Measure cache effectiveness
        run: |
          echo "📏 Cache Effectiveness Metrics"
          echo "=============================="
          
          PIP_CACHE_SIZE=$(du -sh ~/.cache/pip 2>/dev/null | cut -f1 || echo "0")
          
          echo "Pip cache size: $PIP_CACHE_SIZE"
          echo ""
          echo "Expected sizes for JobPost:"
          echo "  • pip cache alone: ~300MB"
          echo "  • pip cache + venv: ~800MB"
          echo "  • pip cache + venv + node_modules: ~1.5GB"
          echo ""
          echo "Our strategy: Cache pip only"
          echo "  ✓ Saves 70% storage vs full venv"
          echo "  ✓ Still 80% time savings"
          echo "  ✓ More cache hits due to smaller size"
      
      - name: Install JobPost dependencies
        run: |
          pip install -r requirements.txt
      
      - name: Cache efficiency summary
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost Cache Efficiency Summary"
          echo "===================================="
          echo ""
          echo "Cache storage (this workflow):"
          echo "  • Pip cache: ~300MB"
          echo "  • Estimated 10GB limit usage: 3%"
          echo ""
          echo "Time savings:"
          echo "  • Without cache: ~2-3 minutes"
          echo "  • With cache: ~30 seconds"
          echo "  • Time saved: 80-85%"
          echo ""
          echo "Cost-benefit ratio: Excellent ✅"
    ⚡ JobPost cache size optimization for maximum efficiency

    4. JobPost multi-cache prioritization

    name: JobPost Multi-Cache Prioritization

on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  prioritized-caching:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Cache prioritization strategy
        run: |
          echo "🎯 JobPost Cache Prioritization Strategy"
          echo "========================================="
          echo ""
          echo "Priority 1 (High): Frequently accessed, stable"
          echo "  • Python pip cache (~300MB)"
          echo "  • Access: Every workflow run"
          echo "  • Change frequency: Low (dependencies stable)"
          echo ""
          echo "Priority 2 (Medium): Moderate access, moderate change"
          echo "  • Database fixtures (~50MB)"
          echo "  • Access: Test workflows only"
          echo "  • Change frequency: Medium (updated monthly)"
          echo ""
          echo "Priority 3 (Low): Infrequent access, high change"
          echo "  • Built assets (~100MB)"
          echo "  • Access: Deployment workflows"
          echo "  • Change frequency: High (every commit)"
          echo ""
          echo "Strategy: Use specific keys for each priority"
      
      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
      
      - name: Cache Priority 1 - Python dependencies
        id: cache-python
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/pip
          # Very stable key - only changes when requirements change
          key: jobpost-priority1-pip-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt') }}
          restore-keys: |
            jobpost-priority1-pip-
      
      - name: Install JobPost dependencies
        if: steps.cache-python.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
        run: |
          pip install -r requirements.txt
      
      - name: Cache Priority 2 - Database fixtures
        id: cache-fixtures
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: jobpost/fixtures/
          # Semi-stable key - changes monthly
          key: jobpost-priority2-fixtures-${{ hashFiles('jobpost/fixtures/**/*.json') }}
          restore-keys: |
            jobpost-priority2-fixtures-
      
      - name: Load fixtures if needed
        if: steps.cache-fixtures.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
        env:
          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.test
        run: |
          python manage.py migrate
          python manage.py loaddata jobpost/fixtures/initial_data.json
          python manage.py loaddata jobpost/fixtures/test_data.json
      
      - name: Cache Priority 3 - Built assets
        id: cache-assets
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: jobpost/static/dist/
          # Volatile key - changes every commit
          key: jobpost-priority3-assets-${{ github.sha }}
          restore-keys: |
            jobpost-priority3-assets-
      
      - name: Build assets if needed
        if: steps.cache-assets.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
        run: |
          npm run build
      
      - name: Prioritization effectiveness
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost Cache Prioritization Results"
          echo "========================================"
          echo ""
          echo "Priority 1 (Python deps):"
          echo "  Cache hit: ${{ steps.cache-python.outputs.cache-hit }}"
          echo "  Expected hit rate: >95%"
          echo "  Storage impact: Low (stable)"
          echo ""
          echo "Priority 2 (Fixtures):"
          echo "  Cache hit: ${{ steps.cache-fixtures.outputs.cache-hit }}"
          echo "  Expected hit rate: ~80%"
          echo "  Storage impact: Medium"
          echo ""
          echo "Priority 3 (Assets):"
          echo "  Cache hit: ${{ steps.cache-assets.outputs.cache-hit }}"
          echo "  Expected hit rate: ~50%"
          echo "  Storage impact: High (volatile)"
          echo ""
          echo "Total storage: ~450MB"
          echo "10GB limit usage: 4.5%"
          echo "Strategy: Effective ✅"
    🎯 JobPost multi-cache prioritization for optimal storage usage

    5. JobPost cache cleanup automation

    name: JobPost Cache Cleanup

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 2 * * 1'  # Weekly on Monday at 2 AM
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  cleanup-stale-caches:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Cleanup strategy explanation
        run: |
          echo "🧹 JobPost Cache Cleanup Strategy"
          echo "=================================="
          echo ""
          echo "Automatic eviction (GitHub managed):"
          echo "  • Caches not accessed for 7 days"
          echo "  • LRU eviction when storage full"
          echo "  • No manual intervention needed"
          echo ""
          echo "Manual cleanup targets:"
          echo "  1. Caches from deleted branches"
          echo "  2. Caches from old PRs"
          echo "  3. Caches with outdated keys"
          echo "  4. Test/experimental caches"
      
      - name: List all JobPost caches
        id: list-caches
        run: |
          echo "📋 Listing all JobPost caches..."
          
          gh api \
            -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" \
            -H "X-GitHub-Api-Version: 2022-11-28" \
            /repos/${{ github.repository }}/actions/caches \
            --paginate \
            > all-caches.json
          
          TOTAL=$(jq '.actions_caches | length' all-caches.json)
          echo "total=$TOTAL" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "Found $TOTAL caches"
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
      
      - name: Identify stale branch caches
        id: stale
        run: |
          echo "🔍 Identifying stale branch caches..."
          
          # Get all branches
          gh api \
            -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" \
            repos/${{ github.repository }}/branches \
            --paginate \
            | jq -r '.[].name' > active-branches.txt
          
          # Find caches for non-existent branches
          jq -r '.actions_caches[] | select(.ref | startswith("refs/heads/")) | 
            .ref + "|" + .key + "|" + .id' all-caches.json | while IFS='|' read ref key id; do
            
            branch=$(echo "$ref" | sed 's|refs/heads/||')
            
            if ! grep -q "^${branch}$" active-branches.txt; then
              echo "Stale cache found:"
              echo "  Branch: $branch (deleted)"
              echo "  Key: $key"
              echo "  ID: $id"
              echo "$id" >> stale-cache-ids.txt
            fi
          done
          
          if [ -f stale-cache-ids.txt ]; then
            STALE_COUNT=$(wc -l < stale-cache-ids.txt)
            echo "stale_count=$STALE_COUNT" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
            echo "Found $STALE_COUNT stale caches"
          else
            echo "stale_count=0" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
            echo "No stale caches found"
          fi
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
      
      - name: Delete stale caches
        if: steps.stale.outputs.stale_count > 0
        run: |
          echo "🗑️  Deleting stale caches..."
          
          while read cache_id; do
            echo "Deleting cache ID: $cache_id"
            
            gh api \
              --method DELETE \
              -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" \
              -H "X-GitHub-Api-Version: 2022-11-28" \
              /repos/${{ github.repository }}/actions/caches/$cache_id
            
            echo "✅ Deleted cache $cache_id"
          done < stale-cache-ids.txt
          
          echo ""
          echo "Deleted ${{ steps.stale.outputs.stale_count }} stale caches"
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
      
      - name: Cleanup summary
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost Cache Cleanup Summary"
          echo "================================="
          echo ""
          echo "Total caches reviewed: ${{ steps.list-caches.outputs.total }}"
          echo "Stale caches deleted: ${{ steps.stale.outputs.stale_count }}"
          echo ""
          echo "Cleanup criteria:"
          echo "  ✓ Caches from deleted branches"
          echo "  ✓ Manual deletion via API"
          echo ""
          echo "Automatic cleanup:"
          echo "  ✓ 7-day unused caches (GitHub managed)"
          echo "  ✓ LRU eviction when full (GitHub managed)"
          echo ""
          echo "Next cleanup: Next Monday at 2 AM UTC"
      
      - name: Storage impact analysis
        run: |
          echo "💾 Storage Impact Analysis"
          echo "=========================="
          echo ""
          
          DELETED=${{ steps.stale.outputs.stale_count }}
          
          if [ "$DELETED" -gt 0 ]; then
            # Estimate average cache size
            AVG_SIZE="300"  # MB
            FREED=$((DELETED * AVG_SIZE))
            
            echo "Estimated storage freed:"
            echo "  • Caches deleted: $DELETED"
            echo "  • Average size: ${AVG_SIZE}MB"
            echo "  • Total freed: ~${FREED}MB"
            echo ""
            echo "✅ Cleanup successful"
          else
            echo "No storage freed (no stale caches)"
            echo "✅ Cache hygiene is good"
          fi
    🧹 JobPost automated cache cleanup for storage management

    Next steps

    Docs: Caching dependencies

    Learn best practices, optimization strategies, and advanced caching techniques for GitHub Actions workflows.

    Full description

    After understanding cache fundamentals, access restrictions, and storage limits, the next steps involve implementing advanced caching strategies, monitoring cache effectiveness, and optimizing workflows for performance. For the JobPost Django application, this includes implementing intelligent cache warming, matrix-based caching strategies, cache analytics, and integration with deployment pipelines. These advanced techniques help maximize cache hit rates, reduce workflow execution time, and maintain optimal storage usage while supporting complex CI/CD requirements.

    Syntax

    # Advanced caching patterns:
# 1. Cache warming on default branch
# 2. Matrix-aware caching strategies
# 3. Cache analytics and monitoring
# 4. Integration with deployment workflows
# 5. Conditional caching based on workflow needs

    Parameters description

    Best practices for advanced caching:
    • Cache warming - Pre-populate caches on default branch for feature branches
    • Matrix optimization - Use specific keys for different OS/Python combinations
    • Analytics - Monitor cache hit rates and adjust strategies
    • Selective caching - Cache only for relevant workflow types
    • Documentation - Document caching strategy for team awareness
    • Regular review - Audit cache usage and effectiveness periodically

    Return value

    Implementing these advanced strategies results in faster workflows, better cache hit rates, and more efficient use of the 10GB storage limit. Regular monitoring and optimization ensure the caching strategy evolves with the project's needs.

    1. JobPost cache warming strategy

    name: JobPost Cache Warming

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 6 * * 1'  # Monday at 6 AM - warm caches weekly

jobs:
  warm-python-cache:
    name: Warm Python Cache
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        python-version: ['3.9', '3.10', '3.11', '3.12']
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Cache warming explanation
        run: |
          echo "🔥 JobPost Cache Warming Strategy"
          echo "=================================="
          echo ""
          echo "Purpose:"
          echo "  • Pre-populate caches on main branch"
          echo "  • Feature branches inherit warmed caches"
          echo "  • Reduce cold cache scenarios"
          echo "  • Ensure consistent base cache"
          echo ""
          echo "Warming for Python ${{ matrix.python-version }}"
      
      - name: Setup Python ${{ matrix.python-version }}
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: ${{ matrix.python-version }}
          cache: 'pip'
      
      - name: Warm pip cache
        run: |
          echo "📦 Warming pip cache for Python ${{ matrix.python-version }}"
          pip install -r requirements.txt
          pip install -r requirements-dev.txt
          
          echo ""
          echo "✅ Cache warmed for:"
          echo "  • Python ${{ matrix.python-version }}"
          echo "  • All production dependencies"
          echo "  • All development dependencies"
      
      - name: Warm additional caches
        run: |
          # Pre-compile Python files
          python -m compileall jobpost/
          
          # Warm Django system checks
          python manage.py check --deploy
          
          # Warm migration checks
          python manage.py makemigrations --check --dry-run
          
          echo "✅ Additional caches warmed"
      
      - name: Cache warming summary
        run: |
          echo "📊 Cache Warming Summary"
          echo "========================"
          echo ""
          echo "Python version: ${{ matrix.python-version }}"
          echo "Branch: main"
          echo "Cache key: ${{ runner.os }}-pip-py${{ matrix.python-version }}"
          echo ""
          echo "Benefits:"
          echo "  ✓ Feature branches start with warm cache"
          echo "  ✓ Reduced first-run latency"
          echo "  ✓ Consistent baseline performance"
          echo "  ✓ Weekly refresh ensures freshness"

  warm-nodejs-cache:
    name: Warm Node.js Cache
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'npm'
      
      - name: Warm npm cache
        run: |
          echo "📦 Warming npm cache for JobPost frontend"
          npm ci
          
          echo "✅ Node modules cached for feature branches"
      
      - name: Pre-build assets
        run: |
          npm run build
          
          echo "✅ Built assets ready for deployment workflows"

  cache-status-report:
    name: Cache Status Report
    needs: [warm-python-cache, warm-nodejs-cache]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Generate cache warming report
        run: |
          echo "# JobPost Cache Warming Report" > report.md
          echo "" >> report.md
          echo "## Caches Warmed" >> report.md
          echo "- Python 3.9, 3.10, 3.11, 3.12" >> report.md
          echo "- Node.js 20" >> report.md
          echo "- Production dependencies" >> report.md
          echo "- Development dependencies" >> report.md
          echo "" >> report.md
          echo "## Next Scheduled Warming" >> report.md
          echo "Next Monday at 6 AM UTC" >> report.md
          
          cat report.md
          
          echo ""
          echo "✅ Cache warming complete for all supported versions"
    🔥 JobPost cache warming to accelerate feature branch workflows

    2. JobPost cache analytics and monitoring

    name: JobPost Cache Analytics

on:
  workflow_run:
    workflows: ["*"]
    types: [completed]

jobs:
  analyze-cache-performance:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Fetch cache statistics
        id: stats
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost Cache Analytics Dashboard"
          echo "====================================="
          echo ""
          
          # Get all caches
          gh api \
            -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" \
            /repos/${{ github.repository }}/actions/caches \
            > caches.json
          
          TOTAL_CACHES=$(jq '.actions_caches | length' caches.json)
          TOTAL_SIZE=$(jq '[.actions_caches[].size_in_bytes] | add // 0' caches.json)
          TOTAL_GB=$(echo "scale=2; $TOTAL_SIZE / 1024 / 1024 / 1024" | bc)
          
          echo "total_caches=$TOTAL_CACHES" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "total_size_gb=$TOTAL_GB" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          
          echo "Repository cache overview:"
          echo "  • Total caches: $TOTAL_CACHES"
          echo "  • Total size: ${TOTAL_GB}GB / 10GB"
          echo "  • Usage: $(echo "scale=1; $TOTAL_GB * 10" | bc)%"
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
      
      - name: Analyze cache by type
        run: |
          echo ""
          echo "🔍 Cache Breakdown by Type"
          echo "=========================="
          
          # Python caches
          PYTHON_COUNT=$(jq '[.actions_caches[] | select(.key | contains("pip"))] | length' caches.json)
          PYTHON_SIZE=$(jq '[.actions_caches[] | select(.key | contains("pip")) | .size_in_bytes] | add // 0' caches.json)
          PYTHON_MB=$(echo "scale=0; $PYTHON_SIZE / 1024 / 1024" | bc)
          
          echo "Python caches:"
          echo "  • Count: $PYTHON_COUNT"
          echo "  • Size: ${PYTHON_MB}MB"
          
          # Node.js caches
          NODE_COUNT=$(jq '[.actions_caches[] | select(.key | contains("node") or contains("npm"))] | length' caches.json)
          NODE_SIZE=$(jq '[.actions_caches[] | select(.key | contains("node") or contains("npm")) | .size_in_bytes] | add // 0' caches.json)
          NODE_MB=$(echo "scale=0; $NODE_SIZE / 1024 / 1024" | bc)
          
          echo ""
          echo "Node.js caches:"
          echo "  • Count: $NODE_COUNT"
          echo "  • Size: ${NODE_MB}MB"
          
          # Other caches
          OTHER_COUNT=$((TOTAL_CACHES - PYTHON_COUNT - NODE_COUNT))
          OTHER_SIZE=$((TOTAL_SIZE - PYTHON_SIZE - NODE_SIZE))
          OTHER_MB=$(echo "scale=0; $OTHER_SIZE / 1024 / 1024" | bc)
          
          echo ""
          echo "Other caches:"
          echo "  • Count: $OTHER_COUNT"
          echo "  • Size: ${OTHER_MB}MB"
        env:
          TOTAL_CACHES: ${{ steps.stats.outputs.total_caches }}
          TOTAL_SIZE: ${{ steps.stats.outputs.total_size_gb }}
      
      - name: Cache hit rate estimation
        run: |
          echo ""
          echo "📈 Cache Hit Rate Analysis"
          echo "=========================="
          
          # Fetch recent workflow runs
          gh api \
            -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" \
            "/repos/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs?per_page=50" \
            > workflow-runs.json
          
          # Analyze cache usage patterns
          echo "Recent workflow analysis (last 50 runs):"
          
          SUCCESS_COUNT=$(jq '[.workflow_runs[] | select(.conclusion == "success")] | length' workflow-runs.json)
          TOTAL_RUNS=$(jq '.workflow_runs | length' workflow-runs.json)
          
          echo "  • Total runs: $TOTAL_RUNS"
          echo "  • Successful: $SUCCESS_COUNT"
          echo "  • Success rate: $(echo "scale=1; $SUCCESS_COUNT * 100 / $TOTAL_RUNS" | bc)%"
          echo ""
          
          # Estimate cache hit impact
          AVG_RUNTIME=$(jq '[.workflow_runs[].run_duration_ms // 0] | add / length / 1000 / 60' workflow-runs.json)
          
          echo "Average runtime: $(echo "scale=1; $AVG_RUNTIME" | bc) minutes"
          echo ""
          echo "Estimated impact of caching:"
          echo "  • Without caching: ~5-7 minutes"
          echo "  • With caching: ~2-3 minutes"
          echo "  • Time saved: ~50-60%"
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
      
      - name: Generate optimization recommendations
        run: |
          echo ""
          echo "💡 JobPost Cache Optimization Recommendations"
          echo "=============================================="
          
          USAGE=${{ steps.stats.outputs.total_size_gb }}
          
          if (( $(echo "$USAGE > 8" | bc -l) )); then
            echo "🔴 Critical: Cache usage above 80%"
            echo ""
            echo "Immediate actions required:"
            echo "  1. Delete caches for merged/closed PRs"
            echo "  2. Implement cache rotation strategy"
            echo "  3. Review cache key specificity"
            echo "  4. Consider cache size optimization"
          elif (( $(echo "$USAGE > 6" | bc -l) )); then
            echo "🟡 Warning: Cache usage above 60%"
            echo ""
            echo "Recommended actions:"
            echo "  1. Monitor cache growth trends"
            echo "  2. Review stale caches"
            echo "  3. Plan for cache cleanup"
          else
            echo "🟢 Healthy: Cache usage below 60%"
            echo ""
            echo "Continue current strategies:"
            echo "  ✓ Regular cache monitoring"
            echo "  ✓ Automatic 7-day eviction"
            echo "  ✓ Efficient cache key design"
          fi
      
      - name: Create analytics report
        run: |
          cat > cache-analytics.json << EOF
          {
            "timestamp": "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)",
            "total_caches": ${{ steps.stats.outputs.total_caches }},
            "total_size_gb": ${{ steps.stats.outputs.total_size_gb }},
            "usage_percent": $(echo "scale=1; ${{ steps.stats.outputs.total_size_gb }} * 10" | bc),
            "repository": "${{ github.repository }}",
            "recommendations": "See workflow logs"
          }
          EOF
          
          cat cache-analytics.json
          
          echo ""
          echo "✅ Cache analytics report generated"
    📊 JobPost cache analytics for performance monitoring

    3. JobPost conditional caching strategy

    name: JobPost Conditional Caching

on:
  push:
    branches: ['**']
  pull_request:
    branches: [main, develop]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 2  # Need 2 commits to detect changes
      
      - name: Detect changed files
        id: changes
        run: |
          echo "🔍 Detecting JobPost file changes"
          echo "=================================="
          
          if [[ "${{ github.event_name }}" == "pull_request" ]]; then
            BASE_SHA="${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}"
          else
            BASE_SHA="HEAD^"
          fi
          
          # Check for Python changes
          if git diff --name-only $BASE_SHA HEAD | grep -E '\.py$|requirements.*\.txt$'; then
            echo "python_changed=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
            echo "✓ Python files changed"
          else
            echo "python_changed=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
            echo "✗ No Python changes"
          fi
          
          # Check for frontend changes
          if git diff --name-only $BASE_SHA HEAD | grep -E 'package.*\.json$|\.js$|\.jsx$|\.ts$|\.tsx$'; then
            echo "frontend_changed=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
            echo "✓ Frontend files changed"
          else
            echo "frontend_changed=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
            echo "✗ No frontend changes"
          fi
          
          # Check for fixture changes
          if git diff --name-only $BASE_SHA HEAD | grep -E 'fixtures/.*\.json$'; then
            echo "fixtures_changed=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
            echo "✓ Fixture files changed"
          else
            echo "fixtures_changed=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
            echo "✗ No fixture changes"
          fi
      
      - name: Setup Python
        if: steps.changes.outputs.python_changed == 'true'
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
          cache: 'pip'
      
      - name: Cache Python dependencies (conditional)
        if: steps.changes.outputs.python_changed == 'true'
        id: cache-python
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/pip
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-pip-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-pip-
      
      - name: Install JobPost Python dependencies
        if: steps.changes.outputs.python_changed == 'true' && steps.cache-python.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
        run: |
          pip install -r requirements.txt
      
      - name: Setup Node.js
        if: steps.changes.outputs.frontend_changed == 'true'
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'npm'
      
      - name: Cache Node modules (conditional)
        if: steps.changes.outputs.frontend_changed == 'true'
        id: cache-node
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: node_modules
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-node-${{ hashFiles('**/package-lock.json') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-node-
