      - name: Install JobPost frontend dependencies
        if: steps.changes.outputs.frontend_changed == 'true' && steps.cache-node.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
        run: |
          npm ci
      
      - name: Cache fixtures (conditional)
        if: steps.changes.outputs.fixtures_changed == 'true'
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: jobpost/fixtures/
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-fixtures-${{ hashFiles('jobpost/fixtures/**/*.json') }}
      
      - name: Conditional caching summary
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost Conditional Caching Summary"
          echo "======================================="
          echo ""
          echo "Python caching:"
          echo "  Changed: ${{ steps.changes.outputs.python_changed }}"
          
          if [[ "${{ steps.changes.outputs.python_changed }}" == "true" ]]; then
            echo "  Cache hit: ${{ steps.cache-python.outputs.cache-hit }}"
            echo "  Action: Python cache used"
          else
            echo "  Action: Skipped (no Python changes)"
          fi
          
          echo ""
          echo "Frontend caching:"
          echo "  Changed: ${{ steps.changes.outputs.frontend_changed }}"
          
          if [[ "${{ steps.changes.outputs.frontend_changed }}" == "true" ]]; then
            echo "  Cache hit: ${{ steps.cache-node.outputs.cache-hit }}"
            echo "  Action: Node cache used"
          else
            echo "  Action: Skipped (no frontend changes)"
          fi
          
          echo ""
          echo "Benefits of conditional caching:"
          echo "  ✓ Faster workflow (skip unnecessary steps)"
          echo "  ✓ Reduced cache operations"
          echo "  ✓ Lower storage usage"
          echo "  ✓ Smart resource utilization"
    🎯 JobPost conditional caching based on file changes

    4. JobPost matrix caching best practices

    name: JobPost Matrix Caching

on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  test-matrix:
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, windows-latest, macos-latest]
        python-version: ['3.9', '3.10', '3.11', '3.12']
        exclude:
          # Reduce matrix size - skip some combinations
          - os: windows-latest
            python-version: '3.9'
          - os: macos-latest
            python-version: '3.9'
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Matrix cache strategy
        run: |
          echo "🔢 JobPost Matrix Caching Strategy"
          echo "==================================="
          echo ""
          echo "Current matrix combination:"
          echo "  OS: ${{ matrix.os }}"
          echo "  Python: ${{ matrix.python-version }}"
          echo ""
          echo "Cache key strategy:"
          echo "  • Include OS in key (different binaries)"
          echo "  • Include Python version (different deps)"
          echo "  • Include requirements hash (dep changes)"
          echo "  • Share across branches when possible"
      
      - name: Setup Python ${{ matrix.python-version }}
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: ${{ matrix.python-version }}
          cache: 'pip'
          cache-dependency-path: |
            **/requirements.txt
            **/requirements-dev.txt
      
      - name: Additional matrix-aware caching
        id: cache-extras
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cache/pip
            ~/.local/share/virtualenvs
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-py${{ matrix.python-version }}-jobpost-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements*.txt') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-py${{ matrix.python-version }}-jobpost-
            ${{ runner.os }}-py${{ matrix.python-version }}-
      
      - name: Install JobPost dependencies
        run: |
          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
          pip install -r requirements.txt
          pip install -r requirements-dev.txt
      
      - name: Run JobPost tests
        env:
          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.test
        run: |
          python manage.py test jobpost
      
      - name: Matrix cache effectiveness
        run: |
          echo "📊 Matrix Cache Effectiveness"
          echo "============================="
          echo ""
          echo "Configuration: ${{ matrix.os }} / Python ${{ matrix.python-version }}"
          echo "Cache hit: ${{ steps.cache-extras.outputs.cache-hit }}"
          echo ""
          echo "Matrix caching benefits:"
          echo "  ✓ Each OS/Python combo has dedicated cache"
          echo "  ✓ No cache conflicts between combinations"
          echo "  ✓ Optimal cache reuse per configuration"
          echo "  ✓ Parallel jobs don't interfere"
          echo ""
          echo "Storage impact:"
          echo "  • Matrix size: ${{ strategy.job-total }} combinations"
          echo "  • Cache per combo: ~300MB"
          echo "  • Total matrix caches: ~${{ strategy.job-total * 300 }}MB"

  matrix-summary:
    needs: test-matrix
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Matrix caching best practices summary
        run: |
          echo "📚 JobPost Matrix Caching Best Practices"
          echo "========================================="
          echo ""
          echo "1. Key design:"
          echo "   ✓ Include OS and Python version"
          echo "   ✓ Use hashFiles() for dependency tracking"
          echo "   ✓ Implement restore-keys fallback"
          echo ""
          echo "2. Storage optimization:"
          echo "   ✓ Reduce matrix size where possible"
          echo "   ✓ Share caches via restore-keys"
          echo "   ✓ Monitor total cache usage"
          echo ""
          echo "3. Performance:"
          echo "   ✓ Parallel jobs benefit from separate caches"
          echo "   ✓ No cache contention"
          echo "   ✓ Faster overall matrix execution"
          echo ""
          echo "4. Maintenance:"
          echo "   ✓ Regular cleanup of old matrix caches"
          echo "   ✓ Document matrix strategy"
          echo "   ✓ Review matrix necessity periodically"
    🔢 JobPost matrix caching for multi-platform testing

    5. JobPost deployment pipeline caching

    name: JobPost Deployment Pipeline

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    tags: ['v*']

jobs:
  build-and-cache:
    name: Build and Cache Assets
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      cache-key: ${{ steps.cache-key.outputs.key }}
      version: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Determine version
        id: version
        run: |
          if [[ "${{ github.ref }}" == refs/tags/v* ]]; then
            VERSION="${{ github.ref_name }}"
          else
            VERSION="main-${{ github.sha }}"
          fi
          
          echo "version=$VERSION" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "📋 JobPost version: $VERSION"
      
      - name: Generate deployment cache key
        id: cache-key
        run: |
          KEY="jobpost-deploy-${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}"
          echo "key=$KEY" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "🔑 Cache key: $KEY"
      
      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
          cache: 'pip'
      
      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'npm'
      
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          pip install -r requirements.txt
          npm ci
      
      - name: Build JobPost production assets
        run: |
          # Collect Django static files
          python manage.py collectstatic --noinput
          
          # Build frontend assets
          npm run build:production
          
          # Generate version file
          echo "${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}" > version.txt
      
      - name: Cache deployment artifacts
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            jobpost/static/
            jobpost/media/
            dist/
            version.txt
          key: ${{ steps.cache-key.outputs.key }}
      
      - name: Deployment cache summary
        run: |
          echo "📦 JobPost Deployment Cache Created"
          echo "===================================="
          echo ""
          echo "Version: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}"
          echo "Cache key: ${{ steps.cache-key.outputs.key }}"
          echo ""
          echo "Cached artifacts:"
          echo "  ✓ Django static files"
          echo "  ✓ Frontend production build"
          echo "  ✓ Media files"
          echo "  ✓ Version information"
          echo ""
          echo "These artifacts will be reused across deployment jobs"

  deploy-staging:
    name: Deploy to Staging
    needs: build-and-cache
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: staging
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Restore deployment cache
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            jobpost/static/
            jobpost/media/
            dist/
            version.txt
          key: ${{ needs.build-and-cache.outputs.cache-key }}
          fail-on-cache-miss: true
      
      - name: Verify cached artifacts
        run: |
          echo "✅ Deployment artifacts restored from cache"
          echo "Version: $(cat version.txt)"
          
          ls -lh jobpost/static/
          ls -lh dist/
      
      - name: Deploy to staging
        run: |
          echo "🚀 Deploying JobPost to staging"
          echo "Version: ${{ needs.build-and-cache.outputs.version }}"
          
          # Deployment commands here
          # rsync -avz jobpost/static/ staging:/var/www/static/
          # rsync -avz dist/ staging:/var/www/dist/

  deploy-production:
    name: Deploy to Production
    needs: [build-and-cache, deploy-staging]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: production
    if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Restore deployment cache
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            jobpost/static/
            jobpost/media/
            dist/
            version.txt
          key: ${{ needs.build-and-cache.outputs.cache-key }}
          fail-on-cache-miss: true
      
      - name: Deploy to production
        run: |
          echo "🚀 Deploying JobPost to production"
          echo "Version: ${{ needs.build-and-cache.outputs.version }}"
          
          # Production deployment commands
          # ansible-playbook deploy.yml -e "version=${{ needs.build-and-cache.outputs.version }}"
      
      - name: Deployment pipeline summary
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost Deployment Pipeline Summary"
          echo "======================================="
          echo ""
          echo "Deployment version: ${{ needs.build-and-cache.outputs.version }}"
          echo "Cache key: ${{ needs.build-and-cache.outputs.cache-key }}"
          echo ""
          echo "Pipeline benefits:"
          echo "  ✓ Build once, deploy multiple times"
          echo "  ✓ Consistent artifacts across environments"
          echo "  ✓ Faster deployments (no rebuild)"
          echo "  ✓ Reduced resource usage"
          echo ""
          echo "Caching strategy:"
          echo "  • Build job creates deployment cache"
          echo "  • Staging job restores cache"
          echo "  • Production job reuses same cache"
          echo "  • Version-specific keys ensure correctness"
    🚀 JobPost deployment pipeline with artifact caching

    Reusable workflows

    Docs: Reusing workflows

    Create reusable workflows that can be called from other workflows to reduce duplication and improve maintainability.

    Full description

    Reusable workflows allow you to define a workflow once and call it from multiple other workflows, similar to functions in programming. This eliminates duplication, centralizes workflow logic, and makes updates easier to manage across your repository or organization. For the JobPost Django application, reusable workflows can standardize testing, deployment, security scanning, and build processes across multiple projects or branches. A reusable workflow is defined using the workflow_call event trigger and can accept inputs, secrets, and return outputs to the calling workflow.

    Access to Reusable Workflows

    A reusable workflow can be used by another workflow if any of the following is true:

    • Both workflows are in the same repository
    • The called workflow is stored in a public repository, and your organization allows you to use public reusable workflows
    • The called workflow is stored in a private repository and the settings for that repository allow it to be accessed

    Accessibility Matrix:

    Caller Repository Accessible Workflow Repositories
    private private and public
    public public
    ⚠️ Limitations of Reusable Workflows:
    • Nesting Limit: You can connect up to 4 levels of workflows
    • Call Limit: Maximum of 20 unique reusable workflows from a single workflow file (includes nested trees)
    • Environment Variables: env context variables from caller workflow are NOT propagated to called workflow
    • Job-Level Only: Reusable workflows are called directly within a job, not from within job steps
    • No GITHUB_ENV: Cannot use GITHUB_ENV to pass values to job steps in caller workflow

    Supported Keywords for Jobs Calling Reusable Workflows

    When you call a reusable workflow, you can only use the following keywords in the job containing the call:

    • jobs.<job_id>.name
    • jobs.<job_id>.uses
    • jobs.<job_id>.with and jobs.<job_id>.with.<input_id>
    • jobs.<job_id>.secrets, jobs.<job_id>.secrets.<secret_id>, and jobs.<job_id>.secrets.inherit
    • jobs.<job_id>.strategy
    • jobs.<job_id>.needs
    • jobs.<job_id>.if
    • jobs.<job_id>.concurrency
    • jobs.<job_id>.permissions

    Note: If jobs.<job_id>.permissions is not specified, the called workflow will have the default permissions for GITHUB_TOKEN. Permissions can only be downgraded (not elevated) by the called workflow.

    How Reusable Workflows Use Runners:
    • GitHub-hosted runners: Assignment is always evaluated using only the caller's context. Billing is associated with the caller. The caller workflow cannot use GitHub-hosted runners from the called repository
    • Self-hosted runners: Called workflows owned by the same user or organization as the caller workflow can access self-hosted runners from the caller's context. This includes runners in the caller repository and the caller repository's organization

    Syntax

    # Reusable workflow definition (called workflow)
on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      input-name:
        required: true
        type: string
    secrets:
      secret-name:
        required: true
    outputs:
      output-name:
        value: ${{ jobs.job-id.outputs.output-name }}

# Calling a reusable workflow
jobs:
  call-workflow:
    uses: owner/repo/.github/workflows/reusable.yml@main
    with:
      input-name: value
    secrets:
      secret-name: ${{ secrets.SECRET_NAME }}

    Parameters description

    Reusable workflow components:
    • workflow_call - Event trigger that makes a workflow reusable
    • inputs - Parameters passed to the reusable workflow (string, number, boolean)
    • secrets - Sensitive data passed to the reusable workflow
    • outputs - Values returned from the reusable workflow to the caller
    • uses - References the reusable workflow (local path or repository reference)
    • with - Provides input values to the reusable workflow
    • secrets: inherit - Passes all secrets from caller to reusable workflow
    • strategy - Matrix strategy for calling the workflow multiple times
    • needs - Job dependencies
    • if - Conditional execution
    • concurrency - Concurrency control
    • permissions - GITHUB_TOKEN permissions (can only downgrade, not elevate)

    Return value

    Reusable workflows can return outputs that are accessible in the calling workflow. The outputs are defined in the reusable workflow and mapped from job outputs. This enables workflows to pass data between the caller and the called workflow, creating a complete data flow pipeline.

    1. JobPost reusable test workflow

    # .github/workflows/reusable-test.yml
name: Reusable JobPost Tests

on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      python-version:
        description: 'Python version to use'
        required: false
        type: string
        default: '3.11'
      django-settings:
        description: 'Django settings module'
        required: false
        type: string
        default: 'anihpj.settings.test'
      run-migrations:
        description: 'Run migrations before tests'
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: true
    secrets:
      database-url:
        description: 'Database connection URL'
        required: false
    outputs:
      test-result:
        description: 'Test execution result (passed/failed)'
        value: ${{ jobs.test.outputs.result }}
      coverage-percent:
        description: 'Code coverage percentage'
        value: ${{ jobs.test.outputs.coverage }}

jobs:
  test:
    name: Run JobPost Tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      result: ${{ steps.test-run.outputs.result }}
      coverage: ${{ steps.coverage.outputs.percent }}
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Reusable workflow info
        run: |
          echo "🔄 JobPost Reusable Test Workflow"
          echo "=================================="
          echo "Python version: ${{ inputs.python-version }}"
          echo "Django settings: ${{ inputs.django-settings }}"
          echo "Run migrations: ${{ inputs.run-migrations }}"
          echo ""
          echo "This workflow can be called from any other workflow"
      
      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: ${{ inputs.python-version }}
          cache: 'pip'
      
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          pip install -r requirements.txt
          pip install coverage
      
      - name: Run migrations
        if: inputs.run-migrations == true
        env:
          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: ${{ inputs.django-settings }}
          DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.database-url }}
        run: |
          python manage.py migrate --noinput
      
      - name: Run JobPost tests with coverage
        id: test-run
        env:
          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: ${{ inputs.django-settings }}
          DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.database-url }}
        run: |
          coverage run --source='jobpost' manage.py test jobpost
          
          if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
            echo "result=passed" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
            echo "✅ Tests passed"
          else
            echo "result=failed" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
            echo "❌ Tests failed"
            exit 1
          fi
      
      - name: Generate coverage report
        id: coverage
        run: |
          coverage report
          COVERAGE=$(coverage report | grep TOTAL | awk '{print $4}' | sed 's/%//')
          echo "percent=$COVERAGE" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "📊 Coverage: ${COVERAGE}%"
      
      - name: Reusable workflow summary
        run: |
          echo "📋 JobPost Test Summary"
          echo "======================="
          echo "Result: ${{ steps.test-run.outputs.result }}"
          echo "Coverage: ${{ steps.coverage.outputs.percent }}%"
          echo ""
          echo "This workflow was called with:"
          echo "  • Python: ${{ inputs.python-version }}"
          echo "  • Settings: ${{ inputs.django-settings }}"
    🔄 Reusable JobPost test workflow with inputs, secrets, and outputs

    2. JobPost calling reusable test workflow

    # .github/workflows/ci.yml
name: JobPost CI

on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  test-python-39:
    name: Test with Python 3.9
    uses: ./.github/workflows/reusable-test.yml
    with:
      python-version: '3.9'
      django-settings: 'anihpj.settings.test'
      run-migrations: true
    secrets:
      database-url: ${{ secrets.DATABASE_URL }}
  
  test-python-311:
    name: Test with Python 3.11
    uses: ./.github/workflows/reusable-test.yml
    with:
      python-version: '3.11'
      django-settings: 'anihpj.settings.test'
      run-migrations: true
    secrets:
      database-url: ${{ secrets.DATABASE_URL }}
  
  test-python-312:
    name: Test with Python 3.12
    uses: ./.github/workflows/reusable-test.yml
    with:
      python-version: '3.12'
      django-settings: 'anihpj.settings.test'
      run-migrations: true
    secrets:
      database-url: ${{ secrets.DATABASE_URL }}
  
  report-results:
    name: Report Test Results
    needs: [test-python-39, test-python-311, test-python-312]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Display test results
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost Test Results Across Python Versions"
          echo "==============================================="
          echo ""
          echo "Python 3.9:"
          echo "  Result: ${{ needs.test-python-39.outputs.test-result }}"
          echo "  Coverage: ${{ needs.test-python-39.outputs.coverage-percent }}%"
          echo ""
          echo "Python 3.11:"
          echo "  Result: ${{ needs.test-python-311.outputs.test-result }}"
          echo "  Coverage: ${{ needs.test-python-311.outputs.coverage-percent }}%"
          echo ""
          echo "Python 3.12:"
          echo "  Result: ${{ needs.test-python-312.outputs.test-result }}"
          echo "  Coverage: ${{ needs.test-python-312.outputs.coverage-percent }}%"
          echo ""
          echo "Benefits of reusable workflow:"
          echo "  ✓ Single test definition, multiple uses"
          echo "  ✓ Consistent testing across versions"
          echo "  ✓ Easy to update test logic in one place"
          echo "  ✓ Outputs accessible to calling workflow"
    📞 JobPost calling reusable workflow multiple times with different inputs

    3. JobPost reusable deployment workflow

    # .github/workflows/reusable-deploy.yml
name: Reusable JobPost Deployment

on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      environment:
        description: 'Deployment environment (staging/production)'
        required: true
        type: string
      version:
        description: 'Version to deploy'
        required: true
        type: string
      run-migrations:
        description: 'Run database migrations'
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: true
      collect-static:
        description: 'Collect static files'
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: true
    secrets:
      deploy-key:
        description: 'SSH deployment key'
        required: true
      database-url:
        description: 'Database connection URL'
        required: true
      secret-key:
        description: 'Django SECRET_KEY'
        required: true
    outputs:
      deployment-url:
        description: 'URL of deployed application'
        value: ${{ jobs.deploy.outputs.url }}
      deployment-time:
        description: 'Deployment timestamp'
        value: ${{ jobs.deploy.outputs.timestamp }}

jobs:
  deploy:
    name: Deploy JobPost to ${{ inputs.environment }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: ${{ inputs.environment }}
    outputs:
      url: ${{ steps.deploy.outputs.url }}
      timestamp: ${{ steps.deploy.outputs.timestamp }}
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ inputs.version }}
      
      - name: Deployment info
        run: |
          echo "🚀 JobPost Deployment Workflow"
          echo "=============================="
          echo "Environment: ${{ inputs.environment }}"
          echo "Version: ${{ inputs.version }}"
          echo "Run migrations: ${{ inputs.run-migrations }}"
          echo "Collect static: ${{ inputs.collect-static }}"
      
      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
          cache: 'pip'
      
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          pip install -r requirements.txt
      
      - name: Run migrations
        if: inputs.run-migrations == true
        env:
          DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.database-url }}
          SECRET_KEY: ${{ secrets.secret-key }}
        run: |
          python manage.py migrate --noinput
          echo "✅ Migrations completed"
      
      - name: Collect static files
        if: inputs.collect-static == true
        env:
          SECRET_KEY: ${{ secrets.secret-key }}
        run: |
          python manage.py collectstatic --noinput
          echo "✅ Static files collected"
      
      - name: Deploy to server
        id: deploy
        env:
          DEPLOY_KEY: ${{ secrets.deploy-key }}
        run: |
          # Set deployment URL based on environment
          if [[ "${{ inputs.environment }}" == "production" ]]; then
            URL="https://jobpost.example.com"
          else
            URL="https://staging.jobpost.example.com"
          fi
          
          echo "url=$URL" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "timestamp=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          
          echo "🚀 Deploying to $URL"
          
          # Deployment commands (example)
          # rsync -avz --delete-after \
          #   -e "ssh -i $DEPLOY_KEY" \
          #   ./ user@server:/var/www/jobpost/
          
          echo "✅ Deployment completed"
      
      - name: Deployment summary
        run: |
          echo "📋 JobPost Deployment Summary"
          echo "=============================="
          echo "Environment: ${{ inputs.environment }}"
          echo "Version: ${{ inputs.version }}"
          echo "URL: ${{ steps.deploy.outputs.url }}"
          echo "Time: ${{ steps.deploy.outputs.timestamp }}"
          echo ""
          echo "Reusable workflow benefits:"
          echo "  ✓ Standardized deployment process"
          echo "  ✓ Same workflow for staging and production"
          echo "  ✓ Controlled by input parameters"
          echo "  ✓ Returns deployment details to caller"
    🚀 Reusable JobPost deployment workflow with environment-specific logic

    4. JobPost workflow with secrets inheritance

    # .github/workflows/reusable-security-scan.yml
name: Reusable Security Scan

on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      scan-type:
        description: 'Type of security scan (dependencies/code/secrets)'
        required: true
        type: string
    secrets:
      snyk-token:
        required: false
      sonar-token:
        required: false
    # Note: When caller uses "secrets: inherit", all secrets are passed

jobs:
  security-scan:
    name: Run Security Scan
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Security scan info
        run: |
          echo "🔒 JobPost Security Scan"
          echo "========================"
          echo "Scan type: ${{ inputs.scan-type }}"
      
      - name: Dependency scan
        if: inputs.scan-type == 'dependencies'
        env:
          SNYK_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.snyk-token }}
        run: |
          echo "📦 Scanning JobPost dependencies"
          
          if [ -n "$SNYK_TOKEN" ]; then
            # npm install -g snyk
            # snyk test
            echo "✅ Snyk scan completed"
          else
            echo "⚠️  SNYK_TOKEN not provided, skipping"
          fi
      
      - name: Code quality scan
        if: inputs.scan-type == 'code'
        env:
          SONAR_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.sonar-token }}
        run: |
          echo "🔍 Scanning JobPost code quality"
          
          if [ -n "$SONAR_TOKEN" ]; then
            # sonar-scanner
            echo "✅ SonarQube scan completed"
          else
            echo "⚠️  SONAR_TOKEN not provided, skipping"
          fi
      
      - name: Secret scan
        if: inputs.scan-type == 'secrets'
        run: |
          echo "🔐 Scanning for exposed secrets"
          
          # git secrets --scan
          # gitleaks detect --no-git
          
          echo "✅ Secret scan completed"

# .github/workflows/full-security.yml
---
name: JobPost Full Security Suite

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 2 * * 1'  # Weekly on Monday
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  scan-dependencies:
    name: Scan Dependencies
    uses: ./.github/workflows/reusable-security-scan.yml
    with:
      scan-type: 'dependencies'
    secrets: inherit  # Pass all secrets to reusable workflow
  
  scan-code-quality:
    name: Scan Code Quality
    uses: ./.github/workflows/reusable-security-scan.yml
    with:
      scan-type: 'code'
    secrets: inherit
  
  scan-secrets:
    name: Scan for Exposed Secrets
    uses: ./.github/workflows/reusable-security-scan.yml
    with:
      scan-type: 'secrets'
    secrets: inherit
  
  security-summary:
    name: Security Summary
    needs: [scan-dependencies, scan-code-quality, scan-secrets]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Generate security report
        run: |
          echo "🔒 JobPost Security Scan Summary"
          echo "================================"
          echo ""
          echo "Completed scans:"
          echo "  ✓ Dependency vulnerability scan"
          echo "  ✓ Code quality and security scan"
          echo "  ✓ Exposed secrets scan"
          echo ""
          echo "Benefits of secrets: inherit:"
          echo "  ✓ No need to list each secret individually"
          echo "  ✓ Reusable workflow has access to all secrets"
          echo "  ✓ Simplified workflow configuration"
          echo "  ✓ Easy to add new secrets without updating calls"
    🔒 JobPost security scanning with secrets inheritance

    5. JobPost cross-repository reusable workflow

    # In organization repo: my-org/shared-workflows
# .github/workflows/django-ci.yml
name: Shared Django CI Workflow

on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      app-name:
        description: 'Django app name to test'
        required: true
        type: string
      settings-module:
        description: 'Django settings module'
        required: false
        type: string
        default: 'settings.test'
      python-version:
        description: 'Python version'
        required: false
        type: string
        default: '3.11'
    secrets:
      database-url:
        required: false
    outputs:
      test-status:
        value: ${{ jobs.test.outputs.status }}

jobs:
  test:
    name: Django CI for ${{ inputs.app-name }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      status: ${{ steps.test.outputs.status }}
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: ${{ inputs.python-version }}
          cache: 'pip'
      
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          pip install -r requirements.txt
      
      - name: Run tests
        id: test
        env:
          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: ${{ inputs.settings-module }}
          DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.database-url }}
        run: |
          python manage.py test ${{ inputs.app-name }}
          echo "status=passed" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Shared workflow info
        run: |
          echo "✅ Shared Django CI completed"
          echo "App: ${{ inputs.app-name }}"
          echo "Settings: ${{ inputs.settings-module }}"

# In JobPost repository: my-org/jobpost
# .github/workflows/use-shared-ci.yml
---
name: JobPost CI (Using Shared Workflow)

on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  test-jobpost:
    name: Test JobPost App
    uses: my-org/shared-workflows/.github/workflows/django-ci.yml@v1
    with:
      app-name: 'jobpost'
      settings-module: 'anihpj.settings.test'
      python-version: '3.11'
    secrets:
      database-url: ${{ secrets.DATABASE_URL }}
  
  test-results:
    name: Process Test Results
    needs: test-jobpost
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Display results
        run: |
          echo "📊 JobPost Test Results"
          echo "======================="
          echo "Status: ${{ needs.test-jobpost.outputs.test-status }}"
          echo ""
          echo "Cross-repository reusable workflow benefits:"
          echo "  ✓ Share workflows across organization"
          echo "  ✓ Centralized CI/CD templates"
          echo "  ✓ Version-controlled workflow library"
          echo "  ✓ Consistent practices across teams"
          echo ""
          echo "Reference format:"
          echo "  uses: org/repo/.github/workflows/workflow.yml@ref"
          echo "  • org: my-org"
          echo "  • repo: shared-workflows"
          echo "  • workflow: django-ci.yml"
          echo "  • ref: v1 (tag, branch, or SHA)"
    🌐 JobPost using cross-repository reusable workflow from organization

    Workflow templates

    Docs: Workflow templates

    Create workflow templates for your organization to provide pre-configured workflows that users can easily add to their repositories.

    Full description

    Workflow templates (also called starter workflows) are pre-configured workflow files that appear in the "Actions" tab when users create a new workflow in their repository. Organizations can create custom workflow templates to standardize CI/CD practices across multiple repositories. For the JobPost Django application, workflow templates can provide ready-to-use configurations for testing, deployment, security scanning, and code quality checks. Templates are stored in a special .github repository within your organization and include both the workflow YAML file and a metadata file describing the template.

    Template Availability Based on Repository Visibility

    Workflow templates created by users with write access to the organization's .github repository are available in repositories based on the .github repository's visibility:

    • Public .github repository: Templates are available to all public, internal, and private repositories in the organization
    • Internal .github repository: Templates are available to all internal and private repositories in the organization
    • Private .github repository: Templates are available to all private repositories in the organization

    Note: If the organization's .github repository is private or internal, people who want to use the workflow template must have read access to the .github repository.

    The $default-branch Placeholder

    When you create a workflow template, you can use the $default-branch placeholder for the name of the repository's default branch. When a workflow is created from your template, the placeholder will be automatically replaced with the name of the repository's default branch.

    Example:

    on:
  push:
    branches: [ $default-branch ]  # Becomes 'main', 'master', etc.

    Syntax

    # Workflow template structure
# In .github repository: .github/workflow-templates/template-name.yml
# Metadata file: .github/workflow-templates/template-name.properties.json

# Example: octo-organization-ci.yml (workflow template)
name: Octo Organization CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [ $default-branch ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ $default-branch ]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Run a one-line script
        run: echo Hello from Octo Organization

# Example: octo-organization-ci.properties.json (metadata file)
{
  "name": "Octo Organization Workflow",
  "description": "Octo Organization CI starter workflow.",
  "iconName": "example-icon",
  "categories": [
    "Go"
  ],
  "filePatterns": [
    "package.json$",
    "^Dockerfile",
    ".*\\.md$"
  ]
}

    Parameters description

    Workflow template components:
    • Repository location - Templates must be in .github/workflow-templates/ in the .github repository
    • YAML file - The actual workflow file (e.g., django-ci.yml)
    • Properties file - Metadata JSON file (same name with .properties.json extension)
    • name (required) - Display name shown in GitHub Actions UI
    • description (required) - Brief description of the template's purpose
    • iconName (optional) - Icon filename (SVG in workflow-templates) or Octicon reference
    • categories (optional) - Array of category names (General names, Linguist languages, or tech stacks)
    • filePatterns (optional) - Array of regular expressions to match repository files (helps GitHub suggest relevant templates)

    Metadata File Properties

    Property Required Description
    name ✅ Yes The name of the workflow template as shown in UI
    description ✅ Yes A description of the workflow template
    iconName ❌ No Icon filename (SVG) or Octicon reference
    categories ❌ No Language/technology categories for filtering
    filePatterns ❌ No Regex patterns to match repository files

    Return value

    When users create a new workflow in a repository within your organization, workflow templates appear as suggested options in the GitHub Actions UI. Users can select a template to create a pre-configured workflow file in their repository, which they can then customize as needed.

    1. JobPost Django CI workflow template

    # In organization's .github repository
# .github/workflow-templates/jobpost-django-ci.yml
name: Django CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [ $default-branch ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ $default-branch ]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      max-parallel: 4
      matrix:
        python-version: [3.9, 3.11, 3.12]

    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v4
    
    - name: Set up Python ${{ matrix.python-version }}
      uses: actions/setup-python@v5
      with:
        python-version: ${{ matrix.python-version }}
        cache: 'pip'
    
    - name: Install dependencies
      run: |
        python -m pip install --upgrade pip
        pip install -r requirements.txt
    
    - name: Run migrations
      env:
        DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: settings.test
      run: |
        python manage.py migrate
    
    - name: Run tests
      env:
        DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: settings.test
      run: |
        python manage.py test
    
    - name: Run linting
      run: |
        pip install flake8
        flake8 . --count --select=E9,F63,F7,F82 --show-source --statistics

# .github/workflow-templates/jobpost-django-ci.properties.json
{
  "name": "JobPost Django CI",
  "description": "Continuous Integration workflow for Django applications with PostgreSQL support",
  "iconName": "python",
  "categories": [
    "Python",
    "CI"
  ],
  "filePatterns": [
    "manage.py$",
    "requirements.txt$",
    "*.py$"
  ]
}
    📋 JobPost Django CI workflow template for organization repositories

    2. JobPost deployment workflow template

    # .github/workflow-templates/jobpost-deploy.yml
name: Deploy JobPost

on:
  push:
    branches: [ $default-branch ]
    tags:
      - 'v*'
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      environment:
        description: 'Deployment environment'
        required: true
        type: choice
        options:
          - staging
          - production

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: ${{ github.event.inputs.environment || 'staging' }}
    
    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v4
    
    - name: Set up Python
      uses: actions/setup-python@v5
      with:
        python-version: '3.11'
        cache: 'pip'
    
    - name: Install dependencies
      run: |
        pip install -r requirements.txt
    
    - name: Run migrations
      env:
        DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.DATABASE_URL }}
        SECRET_KEY: ${{ secrets.SECRET_KEY }}
      run: |
        python manage.py migrate --noinput
    
    - name: Collect static files
      env:
        SECRET_KEY: ${{ secrets.SECRET_KEY }}
      run: |
        python manage.py collectstatic --noinput
    
    - name: Deploy to server
      env:
        DEPLOY_KEY: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_KEY }}
      run: |
        echo "Deploying JobPost application"
        # Add your deployment commands here
        # Example: rsync, scp, or deployment service commands
    
    - name: Deployment notification
      if: success()
      run: |
        echo "✅ Deployment completed successfully"

# .github/workflow-templates/jobpost-deploy.properties.json
{
  "name": "JobPost Deployment",
  "description": "Deploy Django JobPost application to staging or production environments",
  "iconName": "rocket",
  "categories": [
    "Deployment",
    "Python"
  ],
  "filePatterns": [
    "manage.py$",
    "wsgi.py$",
    "asgi.py$"
  ]
}
    🚀 JobPost deployment workflow template with environment selection

    3. JobPost security scanning workflow template

    # .github/workflow-templates/jobpost-security-scan.yml
name: Security Scan

on:
  push:
    branches: [ $default-branch ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ $default-branch ]
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 2 * * 1'  # Weekly on Monday at 2 AM

jobs:
  dependency-scan:
    name: Scan Dependencies
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v4
    
    - name: Set up Python
      uses: actions/setup-python@v5
      with:
        python-version: '3.11'
    
    - name: Install dependencies
      run: |
        pip install -r requirements.txt
        pip install safety bandit
    
    - name: Run Safety check
      run: |
        safety check --json || true
    
    - name: Run Bandit security scan
      run: |
        bandit -r . -f json -o bandit-report.json || true
    
    - name: Upload security reports
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: security-reports
        path: |
          bandit-report.json
  
  code-quality:
    name: Code Quality
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v4
    
    - name: Set up Python
      uses: actions/setup-python@v5
      with:
        python-version: '3.11'
    
    - name: Install tools
      run: |
        pip install flake8 pylint mypy
    
    - name: Run Flake8
      run: |
        flake8 . --count --statistics
    
    - name: Run Pylint
      run: |
        pylint **/*.py || true
    
    - name: Run MyPy type checking
      run: |
        mypy . || true

# .github/workflow-templates/jobpost-security-scan.properties.json
{
  "name": "JobPost Security & Quality Scan",
  "description": "Comprehensive security scanning and code quality checks for Django applications",
  "iconName": "shield",
  "categories": [
    "Security",
    "Code Quality",
    "Python"
  ],
  "filePatterns": [
    "*.py$",
    "requirements.txt$"
  ]
}
    🔒 JobPost security scanning workflow template with multiple tools

    4. JobPost database workflow template

    # .github/workflow-templates/jobpost-database-ops.yml
name: Database Operations

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      operation:
        description: 'Database operation to perform'
        required: true
        type: choice
        options:
          - migrate
          - backup
          - seed-data
          - check-migrations
      environment:
        description: 'Target environment'
        required: true
        type: choice
        options:
          - staging
          - production

jobs:
  database-operation:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: ${{ github.event.inputs.environment }}
    
    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v4
    
    - name: Set up Python
      uses: actions/setup-python@v5
      with:
        python-version: '3.11'
        cache: 'pip'
    
    - name: Install dependencies
      run: |
        pip install -r requirements.txt
    
    - name: Run migration
      if: github.event.inputs.operation == 'migrate'
      env:
        DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.DATABASE_URL }}
        SECRET_KEY: ${{ secrets.SECRET_KEY }}
      run: |
        echo "🔄 Running database migrations"
        python manage.py migrate --noinput
        echo "✅ Migrations completed"
    
    - name: Backup database
      if: github.event.inputs.operation == 'backup'
      env:
        DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.DATABASE_URL }}
      run: |
        echo "💾 Creating database backup"
        # Add backup commands here
        # Example: pg_dump or Django dumpdata
        python manage.py dumpdata --natural-foreign --natural-primary \
          --exclude auth.permission --exclude contenttypes \
          --indent 2 > backup-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).json
        echo "✅ Backup completed"
    
    - name: Seed test data
      if: github.event.inputs.operation == 'seed-data'
      env:
        DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.DATABASE_URL }}
        SECRET_KEY: ${{ secrets.SECRET_KEY }}
      run: |
        echo "🌱 Seeding database with test data"
        python manage.py loaddata fixtures/initial_data.json
        echo "✅ Data seeding completed"
    
    - name: Check migrations
      if: github.event.inputs.operation == 'check-migrations'
      env:
        DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.DATABASE_URL }}
        SECRET_KEY: ${{ secrets.SECRET_KEY }}
      run: |
        echo "🔍 Checking for pending migrations"
        python manage.py makemigrations --check --dry-run
        python manage.py showmigrations
        echo "✅ Migration check completed"
    
    - name: Upload backup artifact
      if: github.event.inputs.operation == 'backup'
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: database-backup-${{ github.event.inputs.environment }}
        path: backup-*.json
        retention-days: 30

# .github/workflow-templates/jobpost-database-ops.properties.json
{
  "name": "JobPost Database Operations",
  "description": "Manage Django database migrations, backups, and data seeding operations",
  "iconName": "database",
  "categories": [
    "Deployment",
    "Utilities"
  ],
  "filePatterns": [
    "manage.py$",
    "migrations/*.py$"
  ]
}
    💾 JobPost database operations workflow template for manual triggers

    5. JobPost complete workflow template with documentation

    # .github/workflow-templates/jobpost-complete-ci-cd.yml
name: Complete CI/CD Pipeline

# This workflow provides a complete CI/CD pipeline for Django JobPost applications
# Features:
#   - Multi-version Python testing
#   - Code quality and security checks
#   - Automatic deployment to staging
#   - Manual approval for production
#   - Slack notifications

on:
  push:
    branches: [ $default-branch ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ $default-branch ]
  release:
    types: [published]

env:
  PYTHON_VERSION: '3.11'
  DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: settings.test

jobs:
  test:
    name: Run Tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        python-version: ['3.9', '3.11', '3.12']
    
    steps:
    - name: Checkout JobPost
      uses: actions/checkout@v4
    
    - name: Set up Python ${{ matrix.python-version }}
      uses: actions/setup-python@v5
      with:
        python-version: ${{ matrix.python-version }}
        cache: 'pip'
    
    - name: Install dependencies
      run: |
        pip install -r requirements.txt
        pip install coverage
    
    - name: Run tests with coverage
      run: |
        coverage run --source='.' manage.py test
        coverage report
        coverage xml
    
    - name: Upload coverage to Codecov
      uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4
      with:
        file: ./coverage.xml
        flags: python-${{ matrix.python-version }}
  
  lint:
    name: Code Quality
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v4
    
    - name: Set up Python
      uses: actions/setup-python@v5
      with:
        python-version: ${{ env.PYTHON_VERSION }}
    
    - name: Run linters
      run: |
        pip install flake8 black isort
        black --check .
        isort --check-only .
        flake8 .
  
  security:
    name: Security Scan
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v4
    
    - name: Run security checks
      run: |
        pip install safety bandit
        safety check
        bandit -r .
  
  deploy-staging:
    name: Deploy to Staging
    needs: [test, lint, security]
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: staging
    
    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v4
    
    - name: Deploy to staging
      env:
        DEPLOY_KEY: ${{ secrets.STAGING_DEPLOY_KEY }}
      run: |
        echo "🚀 Deploying to staging environment"
        # Add staging deployment commands
  
  deploy-production:
    name: Deploy to Production
    needs: [test, lint, security]
    if: github.event_name == 'release'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: production
    
    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v4
    
    - name: Deploy to production
      env:
        DEPLOY_KEY: ${{ secrets.PRODUCTION_DEPLOY_KEY }}
      run: |
        echo "🚀 Deploying to production environment"
        # Add production deployment commands
  
  notify:
    name: Notify Team
    needs: [deploy-staging, deploy-production]
    if: always()
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
    - name: Send notification
      run: |
        echo "📢 Sending deployment notification"
        # Add Slack/Discord/email notification

# .github/workflow-templates/jobpost-complete-ci-cd.properties.json
{
  "name": "JobPost Complete CI/CD Pipeline",
  "description": "Full-featured CI/CD pipeline with testing, quality checks, security scanning, and automated deployment",
  "iconName": "workflow",
  "categories": [
    "CI",
    "Deployment",
    "Python",
    "Security"
  ],
  "filePatterns": [
    "manage.py$",
    "requirements.txt$",
    "*.py$"
  ]
}

# README.md for using this template
## JobPost Complete CI/CD Pipeline Template

### Prerequisites
1. Required secrets:
   - `STAGING_DEPLOY_KEY`: SSH key for staging deployment
   - `PRODUCTION_DEPLOY_KEY`: SSH key for production deployment
   - `DATABASE_URL`: Database connection string
   - `SECRET_KEY`: Django secret key

2. Required environments:
   - `staging`: Staging environment configuration
   - `production`: Production environment with protection rules

### Customization
1. Update `$default-branch` to match your default branch name
2. Modify Python versions in the matrix
3. Add your deployment commands in deploy jobs
4. Configure notification endpoints

### Usage
This template will:
- Run tests on every push and PR
- Deploy to staging on main branch push
- Deploy to production on release
- Send notifications on deployment completion
    🎯 JobPost complete CI/CD workflow template with full documentation

    YAML anchors and aliases

    Docs: YAML specification

    Use YAML anchors and aliases to reduce duplication and improve maintainability in GitHub Actions workflows.

    Full description

    YAML anchors (&) and aliases (*) allow you to define reusable content within a YAML file and reference it multiple times. An anchor creates a named reference point, and an alias recalls that reference. This is particularly useful in GitHub Actions workflows to avoid repeating the same configuration across multiple jobs or steps. For the JobPost Django application, YAML anchors can standardize step configurations, environment variables, or job settings across multiple workflows. The result is equivalent to writing out the full YAML content in each location where the alias appears.

    How YAML Anchors and Aliases Work

    YAML anchors and aliases provide a way to reference and reuse content within a YAML file:

    • Anchor (&): Creates a reusable reference point. Syntax: &anchor_name
    • Alias (*): References the anchor. Syntax: *anchor_name
    • Result: The alias is replaced with the exact content from the anchor
    • Equivalent: Using an alias produces the same result as duplicating the YAML content
    Example: Environment Variables with Anchors

    Define environment variables once and reuse them across multiple jobs:

    # Define anchor
env: &env_vars
  NODE_ENV: development
  API_URL: https://api.example.com

jobs:
  job1:
    env: *env_vars  # Reuse environment variables
    steps:
      - run: echo $NODE_ENV

  job2:
    env: *env_vars  # Reuse same environment variables
    steps:
      - run: echo $API_URL

    Result: Both jobs will have identical env sections with NODE_ENV and API_URL.

    Example: Job Configuration Reuse

    Define a base job configuration and reuse it for multiple jobs:

    jobs:
  # Define anchor with base configuration
  test: &base_job
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 10
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: npm test

  # Reuse base configuration
  alt-test: *base_job

    Result: The alt-test job is identical to test. This is equivalent to copying all the YAML from test into alt-test.

    Syntax

    # Define an anchor with &anchor-name
common-step: &common-setup
  name: Setup Python
  uses: actions/setup-python@v5
  with:
    python-version: '3.11'

# Reference the anchor with *anchor-name
jobs:
  test:
    steps:
      - *common-setup  # Reuses the common-setup configuration
      
# Merge with << and override specific values
jobs:
  test:
    steps:
      - <<: *common-setup
        with:
          python-version: '3.12'  # Override

    Parameters description

    YAML anchors and aliases components:
    • &anchor-name - Creates an anchor (reference point) with the specified name
    • *anchor-name - Creates an alias that references the anchor
    • <<: *anchor-name - Merges the referenced content into the current location
    • Override values - When merging, you can override specific keys
    • Limitations - Anchors must be defined before use; some GitHub Actions fields don't support anchors
    • Scope - Anchors are scoped to the YAML file where they're defined
    • Duplication Equivalent - Using anchors/aliases produces the same result as writing out the full YAML in each location

    Return value

    YAML anchors and aliases don't return values; they're a YAML feature for reducing duplication. When the YAML is parsed, aliases are replaced with the content from their corresponding anchors, creating the expanded configuration that GitHub Actions executes. The final result is identical to manually duplicating the YAML content in each location.

    1. JobPost common setup steps with anchors

    name: JobPost CI with YAML Anchors

on: [push, pull_request]

# Define reusable step configurations
x-steps:
  checkout: &checkout-step
    name: Checkout JobPost
    uses: actions/checkout@v4
  
  setup-python: &setup-python-step
    name: Setup Python
    uses: actions/setup-python@v5
    with:
      python-version: '3.11'
      cache: 'pip'
  
  install-deps: &install-deps-step
    name: Install JobPost dependencies
    run: |
      pip install -r requirements.txt
  
  django-checks: &django-checks-step
    name: Run Django system checks
    env:
      DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.test
    run: |
      python manage.py check --deploy

jobs:
  test:
    name: Run JobPost Tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - *checkout-step
      - *setup-python-step
      - *install-deps-step
      - *django-checks-step
      
      - name: Run tests
        env:
          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.test
        run: |
          python manage.py test jobpost
  
  lint:
    name: Lint JobPost Code
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - *checkout-step
      - *setup-python-step
      - *install-deps-step
      
      - name: Install linting tools
        run: |
          pip install flake8 black isort
      
      - name: Run linters
        run: |
          black --check jobpost/
          isort --check-only jobpost/
          flake8 jobpost/
  
  security:
    name: Security Checks
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - *checkout-step
      - *setup-python-step
      - *install-deps-step
      - *django-checks-step
      
      - name: Run security scan
        run: |
          pip install bandit safety
          bandit -r jobpost/
          safety check
  
  summary:
    name: Summary
    needs: [test, lint, security]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Display benefits
        run: |
          echo "🎯 YAML Anchors Benefits"
          echo "========================"
          echo ""
          echo "Defined once:"
          echo "  • checkout-step"
          echo "  • setup-python-step"
          echo "  • install-deps-step"
          echo "  • django-checks-step"
          echo ""
          echo "Reused across:"
          echo "  ✓ test job (4 steps)"
          echo "  ✓ lint job (3 steps)"
          echo "  ✓ security job (4 steps)"
          echo ""
          echo "Benefits:"
          echo "  ✓ Single source of truth"
          echo "  ✓ Easy to update"
          echo "  ✓ No duplication"
          echo "  ✓ Consistent configuration"
    ⚓ JobPost workflow with YAML anchors for common steps

    2. JobPost environment variables with anchors

    name: JobPost with Environment Anchors

on: [push, pull_request]

# Define reusable environment configurations
x-env:
  common-env: &common-env
    DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.test
    PYTHONUNBUFFERED: '1'
    PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE: '1'
  
  database-env: &database-env
    <<: *common-env
    DATABASE_URL: postgresql://localhost/jobpost_test
    DATABASE_ENGINE: django.db.backends.postgresql
  
  cache-env: &cache-env
    <<: *common-env
    CACHE_BACKEND: django.core.cache.backends.redis.RedisCache
    CACHE_LOCATION: redis://localhost:6379/1

jobs:
  test-with-postgres:
    name: Test with PostgreSQL
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    services:
      postgres:
        image: postgres:15
        env:
          POSTGRES_DB: jobpost_test
          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres
        options: >-
          --health-cmd pg_isready
          --health-interval 10s
          --health-timeout 5s
          --health-retries 5
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
          cache: 'pip'
      
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          pip install -r requirements.txt
          pip install psycopg2-binary
      
      - name: Run migrations
        env: *database-env
        run: |
          python manage.py migrate
      
      - name: Run tests
        env: *database-env
        run: |
          python manage.py test jobpost
      
      - name: Environment summary
        env: *database-env
        run: |
          echo "📋 JobPost Database Environment"
          echo "==============================="
          echo "Settings: $DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE"
          echo "Database: $DATABASE_ENGINE"
          echo "Database URL: $DATABASE_URL"
          echo "Python buffering: $PYTHONUNBUFFERED"
  
  test-with-redis:
    name: Test with Redis Cache
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    services:
      redis:
        image: redis:7
        options: >-
          --health-cmd "redis-cli ping"
          --health-interval 10s
          --health-timeout 5s
          --health-retries 5
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
          cache: 'pip'
      
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          pip install -r requirements.txt
          pip install redis django-redis
      
      - name: Test cache operations
        env: *cache-env
        run: |
          python manage.py shell -c "from django.core.cache import cache; cache.set('test', 'value'); print(cache.get('test'))"
      
      - name: Run tests with cache
        env: *cache-env
        run: |
          python manage.py test jobpost
      
      - name: Environment summary
        env: *cache-env
        run: |
          echo "📋 JobPost Cache Environment"
          echo "============================"
          echo "Settings: $DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE"
          echo "Cache backend: $CACHE_BACKEND"
          echo "Cache location: $CACHE_LOCATION"
  
  summary:
    name: Anchors Summary
    needs: [test-with-postgres, test-with-redis]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Display environment anchor benefits
        run: |
          echo "🎯 Environment Anchors Benefits"
          echo "==============================="
          echo ""
          echo "Defined environment sets:"
          echo "  • common-env (base settings)"
          echo "  • database-env (extends common-env)"
          echo "  • cache-env (extends common-env)"
          echo ""
          echo "Advantages:"
          echo "  ✓ Consistent environment across jobs"
          echo "  ✓ Easy to update base configuration"
          echo "  ✓ Merge and override pattern"
          echo "  ✓ Reduced duplication"
          echo ""
          echo "Pattern used:"
          echo "  <<: *common-env  (merge base)"
          echo "  KEY: value       (add/override)"
    🌍 JobPost environment variables with YAML anchors and merging

    3. JobPost matrix configurations with anchors

    name: JobPost Matrix with Anchors

on: [push, pull_request]

# Define reusable matrix configurations
x-matrix:
  python-versions: &python-versions
    - '3.9'
    - '3.11'
    - '3.12'
  
  os-matrix: &os-matrix
    - ubuntu-latest
    - windows-latest
    - macos-latest
  
  django-versions: &django-versions
    - '4.2'
    - '5.0'
    - '5.1'

jobs:
  test-python-matrix:
    name: Test Python ${{ matrix.python-version }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        python-version: *python-versions
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Python ${{ matrix.python-version }}
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: ${{ matrix.python-version }}
          cache: 'pip'
      
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          pip install -r requirements.txt
      
      - name: Run tests
        env:
          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.test
        run: |
          python manage.py test jobpost
      
      - name: Matrix info
        run: |
          echo "🐍 Python Version: ${{ matrix.python-version }}"
          echo "Anchor used: *python-versions"
  
  test-os-matrix:
    name: Test on ${{ matrix.os }}
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      matrix:
        os: *os-matrix
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
          cache: 'pip'
      
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          pip install -r requirements.txt
      
      - name: Run tests
        env:
          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.test
        run: |
          python manage.py test jobpost
      
      - name: Matrix info
        run: |
          echo "💻 Operating System: ${{ matrix.os }}"
          echo "Anchor used: *os-matrix"
  
  test-django-matrix:
    name: Test Django ${{ matrix.django-version }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        django-version: *django-versions
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
          cache: 'pip'
      
      - name: Install Django ${{ matrix.django-version }}
        run: |
          pip install Django==${{ matrix.django-version }}.*
          pip install -r requirements.txt
      
      - name: Run tests
        env:
          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.test
        run: |
          python manage.py test jobpost
      
      - name: Matrix info
        run: |
          echo "🎯 Django Version: ${{ matrix.django-version }}"
          echo "Anchor used: *django-versions"
  
  matrix-summary:
    name: Matrix Anchors Summary
    needs: [test-python-matrix, test-os-matrix, test-django-matrix]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Display matrix benefits
        run: |
          echo "🎯 Matrix Anchors Benefits"
          echo "=========================="
          echo ""
          echo "Defined matrices:"
          echo "  • python-versions: 3 versions"
          echo "  • os-matrix: 3 operating systems"
          echo "  • django-versions: 3 Django versions"
          echo ""
          echo "Jobs using anchors:"
          echo "  ✓ test-python-matrix (3 combinations)"
          echo "  ✓ test-os-matrix (3 combinations)"
          echo "  ✓ test-django-matrix (3 combinations)"
          echo ""
          echo "Benefits:"
          echo "  ✓ Centralized version management"
          echo "  ✓ Easy to add/remove versions"
          echo "  ✓ Consistent across jobs"
          echo "  ✓ Single source of truth"
    🔢 JobPost matrix configurations with YAML anchors

    4. JobPost complex merge and override patterns

    name: JobPost Advanced Anchors

on: [push, pull_request]

# Define base configurations
x-base:
  base-job: &base-job
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 30
    env:
      DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.test
      PYTHONUNBUFFERED: '1'
  
  base-setup: &base-setup
    - name: Checkout JobPost
      uses: actions/checkout@v4
    
    - name: Setup Python
      uses: actions/setup-python@v5
      with:
        python-version: '3.11'
        cache: 'pip'
    
    - name: Install dependencies
      run: pip install -r requirements.txt

jobs:
  # Job using base configuration as-is
  quick-test:
    <<: *base-job
    name: Quick JobPost Test
    
    steps:
      *base-setup
      
      - name: Run quick tests
        run: |
          python manage.py test jobpost.tests.QuickTests
  
  # Job overriding timeout
  long-test:
    <<: *base-job
    name: Long JobPost Test
    timeout-minutes: 60  # Override timeout
    
    steps:
      *base-setup
      
      - name: Run all tests
        run: |
          python manage.py test jobpost
  
  # Job with additional environment variables
  integration-test:
    <<: *base-job
    name: Integration Test
    env:
      <<: *base-job.env
      DATABASE_URL: postgresql://localhost/jobpost_integration
      REDIS_URL: redis://localhost:6379/0
    
    services:
      postgres:
        image: postgres:15
        env:
          POSTGRES_DB: jobpost_integration
          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres
      
      redis:
        image: redis:7
    
    steps:
      *base-setup
      
      - name: Install additional dependencies
        run: |
          pip install psycopg2-binary redis
      
      - name: Run integration tests
        run: |
          python manage.py test jobpost.tests.IntegrationTests
  
  # Job using base but with different runner
  windows-test:
    <<: *base-job
    name: Windows Test
    runs-on: windows-latest  # Override runner
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout JobPost
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
      
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          pip install -r requirements.txt
      
      - name: Run tests
        run: |
          python manage.py test jobpost
  
  summary:
    name: Merge and Override Summary
    needs: [quick-test, long-test, integration-test, windows-test]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Display merge patterns
        run: |
          echo "🎯 YAML Merge and Override Patterns"
          echo "===================================="
          echo ""
          echo "Pattern 1: Use base as-is"
          echo "  <<: *base-job"
          echo "  Result: Inherits all base properties"
          echo ""
          echo "Pattern 2: Override specific property"
          echo "  <<: *base-job"
          echo "  timeout-minutes: 60"
          echo "  Result: Override timeout, keep others"
          echo ""
          echo "Pattern 3: Merge nested objects"
          echo "  <<: *base-job"
          echo "  env:"
          echo "    <<: *base-job.env"
          echo "    NEW_VAR: value"
          echo "  Result: Base env + new variable"
          echo ""
          echo "Pattern 4: Complete override"
          echo "  <<: *base-job"
          echo "  runs-on: windows-latest"
          echo "  Result: Override runner, keep others"
          echo ""
          echo "Benefits:"
          echo "  ✓ Flexible configuration"
          echo "  ✓ DRY principle"
          echo "  ✓ Easy maintenance"
          echo "  ✓ Consistent base configuration"
    🔀 JobPost advanced YAML merge and override patterns

    5. JobPost comprehensive anchors example

    name: JobPost Complete Anchors Example

on: [push, pull_request]

# Define all reusable configurations
x-config:
  # Python versions
  python-versions: &python-versions ['3.9', '3.11', '3.12']
  
  # Common environment
  common-env: &common-env
    DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.test
    PYTHONUNBUFFERED: '1'
    PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE: '1'
  
  # Base job configuration
  base-job: &base-job
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 30
    env: *common-env
  
  # Common steps
  checkout: &checkout
    name: Checkout JobPost
    uses: actions/checkout@v4
  
  setup-python: &setup-python
    name: Setup Python
    uses: actions/setup-python@v5
    with:
      python-version: '3.11'
      cache: 'pip'
  
  install-deps: &install-deps
    name: Install dependencies
    run: |
      pip install -r requirements.txt
  
  # Test configuration
  test-step: &test-step
    name: Run tests
    run: |
      python manage.py test jobpost
  
  # Linting configuration
  lint-tools: &lint-tools
    name: Install linting tools
    run: |
      pip install flake8 black isort mypy
  
  lint-commands: &lint-commands
    name: Run linters
    run: |
      black --check jobpost/
      isort --check-only jobpost/
      flake8 jobpost/
      mypy jobpost/ || true

jobs:
  test:
    <<: *base-job
    name: Test JobPost
    
    steps:
      - *checkout
      - *setup-python
      - *install-deps
      - *test-step
  
  test-matrix:
    <<: *base-job
    name: Test Python ${{ matrix.python-version }}
    strategy:
      matrix:
        python-version: *python-versions
    
    steps:
      - *checkout
      
      - name: Setup Python ${{ matrix.python-version }}
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: ${{ matrix.python-version }}
          cache: 'pip'
      
      - *install-deps
      - *test-step
  
  lint:
    <<: *base-job
    name: Lint JobPost
    
    steps:
      - *checkout
      - *setup-python
      - *install-deps
      - *lint-tools
      - *lint-commands
  
  security:
    <<: *base-job
    name: Security Scan
    
    steps:
      - *checkout
      - *setup-python
      - *install-deps
      
      - name: Install security tools
        run: |
          pip install bandit safety
      
      - name: Run security scan
        run: |
          bandit -r jobpost/
          safety check
  
  coverage:
    <<: *base-job
    name: Code Coverage
    timeout-minutes: 45  # Override timeout
    
    steps:
      - *checkout
      - *setup-python
      - *install-deps
      
      - name: Install coverage
        run: |
          pip install coverage
      
      - name: Run tests with coverage
        run: |
          coverage run --source='jobpost' manage.py test jobpost
          coverage report
          coverage html
      
      - name: Upload coverage report
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: coverage-report
          path: htmlcov/
  
  summary:
    name: Comprehensive Summary
    needs: [test, test-matrix, lint, security, coverage]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Display comprehensive example
        run: |
          echo "🎯 JobPost Comprehensive Anchors Example"
          echo "========================================"
          echo ""
          echo "Anchors defined:"
          echo "  • python-versions (array)"
          echo "  • common-env (object)"
          echo "  • base-job (job config)"
          echo "  • checkout (step)"
          echo "  • setup-python (step)"
          echo "  • install-deps (step)"
          echo "  • test-step (step)"
          echo "  • lint-tools (step)"
          echo "  • lint-commands (step)"
          echo ""
          echo "Jobs using anchors:"
          echo "  ✓ test (5 anchors)"
          echo "  ✓ test-matrix (4 anchors + 1 array)"
          echo "  ✓ lint (6 anchors)"
          echo "  ✓ security (4 anchors)"
          echo "  ✓ coverage (4 anchors + override)"
          echo ""
          echo "Lines saved: ~100+ lines"
          echo "Maintenance: Centralized in x-config"
          echo ""
          echo "Best practices demonstrated:"
          echo "  ✓ Logical grouping (x-config)"
          echo "  ✓ Descriptive anchor names"
          echo "  ✓ Mix of arrays, objects, steps"
          echo "  ✓ Merge and override patterns"
          echo "  ✓ Matrix with anchors"
          echo ""
          echo "Result:"
          echo "  ✅ DRY workflow"
          echo "  ✅ Easy to maintain"
          echo "  ✅ Consistent configuration"
          echo "  ✅ Reduced errors"
    🎯 JobPost comprehensive YAML anchors example with all patterns

    name author

    Docs: Action metadata - name

    Define the name and author of your custom GitHub Action in the action metadata file.

    Full description

    The name field is required in action metadata and defines the display name of your action in the GitHub Marketplace and Actions tab. The author field is optional and specifies the action's creator or maintainer. For the JobPost Django application, custom actions might include deployment scripts, database management tools, or testing utilities. Clear naming helps users understand the action's purpose, while proper author attribution establishes trust and provides contact information for support.

    Syntax

    # action.yml
name: 'Action Display Name'
author: 'Your Name or Organization'
description: 'Brief description of what the action does'

    Parameters description

    Name and author fields:
    • name - Required. The display name shown in GitHub Marketplace and workflow logs
    • author - Optional. The creator or maintainer of the action
    • Naming guidelines - Use clear, descriptive names that explain the action's purpose
    • Character limit - Keep names concise but descriptive (recommended under 50 characters)
    • Uniqueness - Choose unique names to avoid confusion with other actions

    Return value

    The name and author fields don't return values; they provide metadata that GitHub displays in the Marketplace, repository README, and workflow logs. These fields help users discover and identify actions in the GitHub ecosystem.

    1. JobPost Django deployment action

    # action.yml
name: 'Deploy JobPost Django App'
author: 'JobPost Development Team'
description: 'Deploy JobPost Django application to production or staging environments with database migrations and static file collection'

inputs:
  environment:
    description: 'Target environment (staging/production)'
    required: true
  version:
    description: 'Version to deploy'
    required: true

runs:
  using: 'composite'
  steps:
    - name: Display action info
      shell: bash
      run: |
        echo "🚀 JobPost Django Deployment Action"
        echo "===================================="
        echo "Action: Deploy JobPost Django App"
        echo "Author: JobPost Development Team"
        echo "Environment: ${{ inputs.environment }}"
        echo "Version: ${{ inputs.version }}"
        echo ""
        echo "This custom action handles:"
        echo "  • Database migrations"
        echo "  • Static file collection"
        echo "  • Application deployment"
        echo "  • Health checks"
    🚀 JobPost deployment action with clear name and author

    2. JobPost database management action

    # action.yml
name: 'JobPost Database Manager'
author: 'anihpj'
description: 'Manage JobPost Django database operations including migrations, backups, and data seeding'

inputs:
  operation:
    description: 'Operation to perform (migrate/backup/seed/reset)'
    required: true
  database-url:
    description: 'Database connection URL'
    required: true

runs:
  using: 'composite'
  steps:
    - name: Action information
      shell: bash
      run: |
        echo "💾 JobPost Database Manager"
        echo "==========================="
        echo "Action Name: JobPost Database Manager"
        echo "Author: anihpj"
        echo "Operation: ${{ inputs.operation }}"
        echo ""
        echo "Purpose:"
        echo "  This action provides centralized database"
        echo "  management for JobPost Django application"
        echo ""
        echo "Supported operations:"
        echo "  • migrate - Run Django migrations"
        echo "  • backup - Create database backup"
        echo "  • seed - Load fixture data"
        echo "  • reset - Reset database to clean state"
    💾 JobPost database management action with organization author

    3. JobPost test runner action

    # action.yml
name: 'JobPost Django Test Runner'
author: 'JobPost QA Team '
description: 'Run JobPost Django tests with coverage reporting and parallel execution support'

inputs:
  python-version:
    description: 'Python version to use'
    required: false
    default: '3.11'
  test-path:
    description: 'Specific test path or module'
    required: false
    default: 'jobpost'
  parallel:
    description: 'Run tests in parallel'
    required: false
    default: 'true'

runs:
  using: 'composite'
  steps:
    - name: Test runner information
      shell: bash
      run: |
        echo "🧪 JobPost Django Test Runner"
        echo "=============================="
        echo "Action: JobPost Django Test Runner"
        echo "Author: JobPost QA Team "
        echo ""
        echo "Configuration:"
        echo "  Python: ${{ inputs.python-version }}"
        echo "  Test path: ${{ inputs.test-path }}"
        echo "  Parallel: ${{ inputs.parallel }}"
        echo ""
        echo "Features:"
        echo "  ✓ Multiple Python version support"
        echo "  ✓ Parallel test execution"
        echo "  ✓ Coverage reporting"
        echo "  ✓ Flexible test selection"
        echo ""
        echo "Contact: qa@jobpost.example.com"
    🧪 JobPost test runner action with team contact information

    4. JobPost security scanner action

    # action.yml
name: 'JobPost Security Scanner'
author: 'JobPost Security Team | anihpj Organization'
description: 'Comprehensive security scanning for JobPost Django application including dependency vulnerabilities, code analysis, and secret detection'

inputs:
  scan-type:
    description: 'Type of scan to perform (full/dependencies/code/secrets)'
    required: false
    default: 'full'
  severity-threshold:
    description: 'Minimum severity to report (low/medium/high/critical)'
    required: false
    default: 'medium'

runs:
